Test 10: 2D Cutting Stock - Rectangle Packing (C++) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 2D Cutting Stock Problem (Rectangle Bin Packing) in C++.

Given the option to purchase fixed size large 2D boards, and a list of rectangles needed,
minimize the number of boards purchased by cleverly planning your cuts and minimising waste.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 4-8 rectangles, 100x100-200x150 boards
- **Ludicrous**: 500-50000 rectangles, 2000x2000-10000x10000 boards

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N board_width board_height
Next N lines: w h (rectangle dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_boards
Next N lines: board_index x y rotated (one per rectangle, rotated is 0 or 1)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size and board dimensions
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for 50000 rectangles
3. **Quality**: Minimize number of boards used while producing valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Rectangles may be rotated 90° if it helps
- No overlapping allowed
- Rectangles must not exceed board boundaries

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35725 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack a set of rectangles into as few fixed-size boards as possible, with no overlapping. Rectangles may be rotated 90° to fit better. Think of it like fitting as many labels as you can onto sheets of card stock.

This 2D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of rectangles. The baseline stacks rectangles in simple horizontal rows.

 0.0000
[4 rectangles, 100x100 board] Compilation failed:
tmp_3wfcqno.cpp
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmp_3wfcqno.cpp(1): fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: 'bits/stdc++.h': No such file or directory

2D Cutting Stock - 4 rectangles, 100x100 board

View Code (29134 chars)
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

#ifdef _MSC_VER
#pragma warning(push)
#pragma warning(disable: 4267 26451)
#endif

static_assert(sizeof(int*) == 8, "64-bit required");

struct Rect {
    int w, h, id;
    double score;
};

struct Placement {
    int x, y, board, rot;
};

struct SkylineNode {
    int x, y, width, height;
};

static inline long long node_area(const SkylineNode& n) {
    return (long long)n.width * n.height;
}

static inline bool node_covers(const SkylineNode& n, int w, int h) {
    return n.width >= w && n.height >= h;
}

class Solver {
public:
    int bw, bh, N;
    vector<Rect> rects;
    vector<Placement> result;
    mt19937 rng;

    Solver(int bw_, int bh_, int n_, unsigned seed = 0)
        : bw(bw_), bh(bh_), N(n_), result(n_), rng(seed) {}

    // ---------- SKYLINE DATA STRUCTURE ----------
    static inline void rm_skyline(vector<SkylineNode>& sk, int idx) {
        sk.erase(sk.begin() + idx);
    }

    // Merge adjacent nodes at the same height, starting from idx going left/right
    static inline void merge_at(vector<SkylineNode>& sk, int idx) {
        // merge left
        while (idx > 0 && sk[idx - 1].y == sk[idx].y) {
            sk[idx - 1].width += sk[idx].width;
            sk[idx - 1].height = max(sk[idx - 1].height, sk[idx].height);
            rm_skyline(sk, idx);
            --idx;
        }
        // merge right
        while (idx + 1 < (int)sk.size() && sk[idx + 1].y == sk[idx].y) {
            sk[idx].width += sk[idx + 1].width;
            sk[idx].height = max(sk[idx].height, sk[idx + 1].height);
            rm_skyline(sk, idx + 1);
        }
    }

    static inline void merge_all(vector<SkylineNode>& sk) {
        for (int i = (int)sk.size() - 1; i > 0; --i) {
            if (sk[i].y == sk[i - 1].y) {
                sk[i - 1].width += sk[i].width;
                sk[i - 1].height = max(sk[i - 1].height, sk[i].height);
                rm_skyline(sk, i);
            }
        }
    }

    // ---------- SKYLINE OPERATIONS ----------
    // Find best fit position for (rw, rh) on skyline. Returns -1 if no fit.
    // fit_mode: 0=Best Area Fit, 1=Best Short Side Fit, 2=Best Long Side Fit, 3=Worst Fit
    int find_skyline_pos(const vector<SkylineNode>& sk, int rw, int rh,
                         int* out_x, int* out_y, int fit_mode, mt19937& R) const {
        int best = -1;
        int best_x = 0, best_y = 0;
        long long best_waste = LLONG_MAX;
        int best_ys = INT_MAX, best_sst = INT_MAX, best_lst = INT_MAX, best_hsum = INT_MAX;

        const int S = (int)sk.size();
        for (int i = 0; i < S; ++i) {
            if (sk[i].width < rw) continue;
            int px = sk[i].x;
            int py = sk[i].y;

            // max height under the rectangle
            int maxY = py;
            int cw = 0;
            bool ok = true;
            for (int j = i; j < S && cw < rw; ++j) {
                maxY = max(maxY, sk[j].y);
                if (maxY + rh > bh) { ok = false; break; }
                cw += sk[j].width;
            }
            if (!ok || cw < rw) continue;

            int waste = cw * maxY - rw * rh;
            int ys = maxY - py;
            int sst = min(rw, rh), lst = max(rw, rh);

            bool better = false;
            switch (fit_mode) {
                case 0: better = waste < best_waste; break;
                case 1:
                    if (best_ys != ys) better = ys < best_ys;
                    else better = waste < best_waste;
                    break;
                case 2:
                    if (best_lst != lst) better = lst < best_lst;
                    else if (best_sst != sst) better = sst < best_sst;
                    else better = waste < best_waste;
                    break;
                case 3: better = waste > best_waste; break;
            }
            if (better) {
                best = i; best_x = px; best_y = py;
                best_waste = waste; best_ys = ys;
                best_sst = sst; best_lst = lst;
                best_hsum = maxY;
            } else if (!better && fit_mode < 3) {
                // tie: check exact same waste
                switch (fit_mode) {
                    case 0:
                        if (waste == best_waste && R() % 100 < 30) {
                            best = i; best_x = px; best_y = py;
                            best_waste = waste; best_ys = ys;
                            best_sst = sst; best_lst = lst;
                        }
                        break;
                    case 1:
                        if (waste == best_waste && R() % 100 < 30) {
                            best = i; best_x = px; best_y = py;
                            best_waste = waste; best_ys = ys;
                        }
                        break;
                    case 2:
                        if (waste == best_waste && R() % 100 < 30) {
                            best = i; best_x = px; best_y = py;
                            best_waste = waste;
                        }
                        break;
                }
            }
        }
        if (best >= 0) { *out_x = best_x; *out_y = best_y; }
        return best;
    }

    void place_on_skyline(vector<SkylineNode>& sk, int idx, int rw, int rh, int px, int py) {
        int spanW = 0, j = idx;
        while (j < (int)sk.size() && spanW < rw) {
            spanW += sk[j].width;
            ++j;
        }
        int spanS = idx, spanE = j; // [spanS, spanE)

        sk[idx].y += rh;
        sk[idx].height -= rh;
        sk[idx].width = rw;
        // update height for spanned nodes not at spanS
        for (int k = idx + 1; k < spanE; ++k) {
            if (sk[k].y + rh < bh) sk[k].height -= rh;
        }
        // adjust right edge if rectangle doesn't end exactly at a node boundary
        int usedW = 0;
        for (int k = spanS; k < spanE; ++k) usedW += sk[k].width;
        if (rw < usedW) {
            int leftover = usedW - rw;
            int k = spanE - 1;
            // backtrack from the right
            while (k > spanS && leftover >= sk[k].width) {
                leftover -= sk[k].width;
                rm_skyline(sk, k);
                --k;
            }
            if (k >= spanS && leftover > 0) {
                sk[k].width -= leftover;
            }
        } else if (rw > usedW && spanE < (int)sk.size()) {
            sk[spanE].x -= (rw - usedW);
            sk[spanE].width += (rw - usedW);
        }

        // clean up zero-height nodes
        for (int k = (int)sk.size() - 1; k >= 0; --k) {
            if (sk[k].height <= 0) rm_skyline(sk, k);
        }
        merge_all(sk);
    }

    // ---------- PACKING ONE CONFIGURATION ----------
    int pack_config(const vector<int>& order, int fit_mode,
                    bool smart_rot, bool rev_second_half,
                    mt19937& R, vector<Placement>& res) {
        vector<vector<SkylineNode>> boards;
        boards.push_back({{0, 0, bw, bh}});
        int nb = 1;

        for (int oi = 0; oi < (int)order.size(); ++oi) {
            int idx = order[oi];
            if (rev_second_half && oi >= (int)order.size() / 2) {
                idx = order[(int)order.size() - 1 - (oi - (int)order.size() / 2)];
            }
            int rw = rects[idx].w, rh = rects[idx].h;
            int ow = rw, oh = rh;

            int chosen_rot = 0;
            if (smart_rot) {
                if (rw > bw) { if (rh <= bw) { swap(rw, rh); chosen_rot = 1; } }
                else if (rh > bh) { if (rw <= bh) { swap(rw, rh); chosen_rot = 1; } }
                else if (rw != rh && R() % 100 < 40) {
                    swap(rw, rh); chosen_rot = 1;
                }
            } else if (rw > bw && rh <= bw) {
                swap(rw, rh); chosen_rot = 1;
            } else if (rh > bh && rw <= bh) {
                swap(rw, rh); chosen_rot = 1;
            }

            bool placed = false;
            int bestBoard = -1, bestIdx = -1, bestX = 0, bestY = 0;
            long long bestWaste = LLONG_MAX, bestYs = LLONG_MAX;
            int bestLST = INT_MAX;

            // search boards with largest heights first (least remaining space)
            int firstBoard = 0;
            // quick heuristic: check last board first (likely most empty)
            vector<int> searchOrder;
            searchOrder.push_back(nb - 1);
            for (int b = nb - 2; b >= 0; --b) searchOrder.push_back(b);
            // occasionally randomize
            if (R() % 100 < 20 && nb > 2) {
                shuffle(searchOrder.begin() + 1, searchOrder.end(), R);
            }

            for (int b : searchOrder) {
                int x = 0, y = 0;
                int si = find_skyline_pos(boards[b], rw, rh, &x, &y, fit_mode, R);
                if (si < 0) continue;

                long long waste = 0;
                int ys = 0, lst = 0;
                // compute waste metric
                {
                    const auto& sk = boards[b];
                    int cw = 0, maxY = y;
                    for (int j = si; j < (int)sk.size() && cw < rw; ++j) {
                        maxY = max(maxY, sk[j].y);
                        cw += sk[j].width;
                    }
                    waste = (long long)cw * maxY - (long long)rw * rh;
                    ys = maxY - y;
                    lst = max(rw, rh);
                }

                bool better = false;
                if (bestBoard < 0) {
                    better = true;
                } else if (fit_mode == 3) {
                    // worst fit: pick the board with most waste (most room)
                    if (waste > bestWaste) better = true;
                    else if (waste == bestWaste && ys > bestYs) better = true;
                } else {
                    // best fit: pick the board where it fits most tightly
                    if (waste < bestWaste) better = true;
                    else if (waste == bestWaste && ys < bestYs) better = true;
                    else if (waste == bestWaste && ys == bestYs && lst < bestLST) better = true;
                }
                if (better) {
                    bestBoard = b; bestIdx = si;
                    bestX = x; bestY = y;
                    bestWaste = waste; bestYs = ys; bestLST = lst;
                }
            }

            if (bestBoard >= 0) {
                place_on_skyline(boards[bestBoard], bestIdx, rw, rh, bestX, bestY);
                res[idx] = {bestX, bestY, bestBoard, chosen_rot};
                placed = true;
            }

            if (!placed) {
                boards.push_back({{0, 0, bw, bh}});
                int b = nb;
                nb++;
                int x = 0, y = 0;
                int si = find_skyline_pos(boards[b], rw, rh, &x, &y, fit_mode, R);
                if (si >= 0) {
                    place_on_skyline(boards[b], si, rw, rh, x, y);
                    res[idx] = {x, y, b, chosen_rot};
                    placed = true;
                }
            }

            if (!placed) return INT_MAX; // should never happen
        }
        return nb;
    }

    // ---------- GUILLotine FALLBACK ----------
    int guillotine_pack(vector<Placement>& res) {
        struct GNode {
            int x, y, w, h;
        };
        vector<vector<GNode>> boards;
        boards.push_back({{0, 0, bw, bh}});
        int nb = 1;

        for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
            int rw = rects[i].w, rh = rects[i].h;
            int rot = 0;
            if (rw > bw && rh <= bw) { swap(rw, rh); rot = 1; }
            else if (rh > bh && rw <= bh) { swap(rw, rh); rot = 1; }
            else if (rw != rh && rng() % 2) { swap(rw, rh); rot = 1; }

            bool placed = false;
            for (int b = 0; b < nb && !placed; ++b) {
                int bi = -1;
                long long ba = LLONG_MAX;
                for (int j = 0; j < (int)boards[b].size(); ++j) {
                    auto& n = boards[b][j];
                    if (n.w >= rw && n.h >= rh) {
                        long long a = (long long)n.w * n.h;
                        if (a < ba) { ba = a; bi = j; }
                    }
                }
                if (bi >= 0) {
                    auto& n = boards[b][bi];
                    res[i] = {n.x, n.y, b, rot};
                    // split: longer remaining side
                    if ((n.w - rw) >= (n.h - rh)) {
                        if (n.w > rw) boards[b].push_back({n.x + rw, n.y, n.w - rw, rh});
                        if (n.h > rh) boards[b].push_back({n.x, n.y + rh, n.w, n.h - rh});
                    } else {
                        if (n.h > rh) boards[b].push_back({n.x, n.y + rh, rw, n.h - rh});
                        if (n.w > rw) boards[b].push_back({n.x + rw, n.y, n.w - rw, n.h});
                    }
                    boards[b].erase(boards[b].begin() + bi);
                    placed = true;
                }
            }
            if (!placed) {
                boards.push_back({{0, 0, bw, bh}});
                nb++;
                auto& n = boards[nb - 1][0];
                if (n.w >= rw && n.h >= rh) {
                    res[i] = {0, 0, nb - 1, rot};
                    if ((n.w - rw) >= (n.h - rh)) {
                        if (n.w > rw) boards[nb-1].push_back({rw, 0, n.w-rw, rh});
                        if (n.h > rh) boards[nb-1].push_back({0, rh, n.w, n.h-rh});
                    } else {
                        if (n.h > rh) boards[nb-1].push_back({0, rh, rw, n.h-rh});
                        if (n.w > rw) boards[nb-1].push_back({rw, 0, n.w-rw, n.h});
                    }
                    boards[nb-1].erase(boards[nb-1].begin());
                } else {
                    return INT_MAX;
                }
            }
        }
        return nb;
    }

    // ---------- MAIN SOLVE ----------
    int solve() {
        // Score rectangles by area (larger first)
        for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
            rects[i].score = -(double)rects[i].w * rects[i].h;
            rects[i].id = i;
        }

        // Determine number of trials based on N
        int base_trials;
        if (N <= 10) base_trials = 100;
        else if (N <= 30) base_trials = 50;
        else if (N <= 100) base_trials = 25;
        else if (N <= 500) base_trials = 15;
        else if (N <= 2000) base_trials = 8;
        else if (N <= 10000) base_trials = 4;
        else base_trials = 2;

        int best_count = INT_MAX;
        vector<Placement> best_res(N);
        vector<int> order(N);
        iota(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);

        // ---- Phase 1: Multi-heuristic with sorting variants ----
        struct SortVariant {
            const char* name;
            function<void(vector<Rect>&)> sort_fn;
        };
        vector<SortVariant> sortVariants;

        // Sort by area descending
        sortVariants.push_back({"area_desc", [](vector<Rect>& v) {
            sort(v.begin(), v.end(), [](const Rect& a, const Rect& b) {
                return a.w * a.h > b.w * b.h;
            });
        }});
        // Sort by area ascending
        sortVariants.push_back({"area_asc", [](vector<Rect>& v) {
            sort(v.begin(), v.end(), [](const Rect& a, const Rect& b) {
                return a.w * a.h < b.w * b.h;
            });
        }});
        // Sort by max(w,h) descending
        sortVariants.push_back({"maxside_desc", [](vector<Rect>& v) {
            sort(v.begin(), v.end(), [](const Rect& a, const Rect& b) {
                return max(a.w, a.h) > max(b.w, b.h);
            });
        }});
        // Sort by perimeter descending
        sortVariants.push_back({"perim_desc", [](vector<Rect>& v) {
            sort(v.begin(), v.end(), [](const Rect& a, const Rect& b) {
                return 2*(a.w+a.h) > 2*(b.w+b.h);
            });
        }});
        // Sort by width descending
        sortVariants.push_back({"width_desc", [](vector<Rect>& v) {
            sort(v.begin(), v.end(), [](const Rect& a, const Rect& b) {
                return a.w > b.w;
            });
        }});
        // Sort by height descending
        sortVariants.push_back({"height_desc", [](vector<Rect>& v) {
            sort(v.begin(), v.end(), [](const Rect& a, const Rect& b) {
                return a.h > b.h;
            });
        }});
        // Sort by width*height descending, then width descending
        sortVariants.push_back({"area_w", [](vector<Rect>& v) {
            sort(v.begin(), v.end(), [](const Rect& a, const Rect& b) {
                long long aa = (long long)a.w*a.h, bb = (long long)b.w*b.h;
                if (aa != bb) return aa > bb;
                return a.w > b.w;
            });
        }});

        vector<int> fit_modes = {0, 1, 2, 3}; // BAF, BSSF, BLSF, WF

        int total_trials = 0;
        const int MAX_TRIALS = 300;

        for (auto& sv : sortVariants) {
            if (total_trials >= MAX_TRIALS) break;
            auto sorted = rects;
            sv.sort_fn(sorted);
            vector<int> ord;
            for (auto& r : sorted) ord.push_back(r.id);

            for (int fm : fit_modes) {
                if (total_trials >= MAX_TRIALS) break;
                for (int t = 0; t < base_trials; ++t) {
                    if (total_trials >= MAX_TRIALS) break;
                    mt19937 R(total_trials * 9973 + 42);
                    vector<Placement> trial_res(N);
                    int cnt = pack_config(ord, fm, true, false, R, trial_res);
                    if (cnt < best_count) {
                        best_count = cnt;
                        best_res = trial_res;
                    }
                    total_trials++;
                }
            }
        }

        // ---- Phase 2: Randomized order trials ----
        int rand_trials = min(base_trials * 3, MAX_TRIALS - total_trials);
        for (int t = 0; t < rand_trials && total_trials < MAX_TRIALS; ++t) {
            mt19937 R(t * 13337 + 7);
            vector<int> ord(N);
            iota(ord.begin(), ord.end(), 0);
            // biased shuffle: 40% area-desc bias, 60% pure random
            for (int i = N - 1; i > 0; --i) {
                int j;
                if (R() % 100 < 40) {
                    // pick from top i+1 with area bias
                    int top = min(i + 1, N);
                    int k = R() % (top / 2 + 1);
                    j = k;
                    if (j > i) j = i;
                } else {
                    j = R() % (i + 1);
                }
                swap(ord[i], ord[j]);
            }
            int fm = R() % 4;
            vector<Placement> trial_res(N);
            int cnt = pack_config(ord, fm, true, false, R, trial_res);
            if (cnt < best_count) {
                best_count = cnt;
                best_res = trial_res;
            }
            total_trials++;
        }

        // ---- Phase 3: Reverse second half trials ----
        if (N >= 4 && total_trials < MAX_TRIALS) {
            auto sorted = rects;
            sort(sorted.begin(), sorted.end(), [](const Rect& a, const Rect& b) {
                return max(a.w, a.h) > max(b.w, b.h);
            });
            vector<int> ord;
            for (auto& r : sorted) ord.push_back(r.id);

            for (int t = 0; t < min(base_trials * 2, MAX_TRIALS - total_trials); ++t) {
                mt19937 R(t * 7727 + 13);
                vector<Placement> trial_res(N);
                int cnt = pack_config(ord, R() % 3, true, true, R, trial_res);
                if (cnt < best_count) {
                    best_count = cnt;
                    best_res = trial_res;
                }
                total_trials++;
            }
        }

        // ---- Phase 4: BFD reinsertion (small instances) ----
        if (N <= 5000 && best_count > 1 && total_trials < MAX_TRIALS) {
            int reinsert_trials = min(30, MAX_TRIALS - total_trials);
            for (int t = 0; t < reinsert_trials; ++t) {
                mt19937 R(t * 5557 + 99);

                // Start from best solution's board assignments
                vector<vector<int>> boards_by_item(best_count);
                for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
                    boards_by_item[best_res[i].board].push_back(i);
                }

                // Sort each board's items by area descending
                for (auto& b : boards_by_item) {
                    sort(b.begin(), b.end(), [this](int a, int b_) {
                        return (long long)rects[a].w * rects[a].h > (long long)rects[b_].w * rects[b_].h;
                    });
                }

                // Compute board "fitness" = used area / board area
                vector<double> board_fitness(best_count);
                vector<long long> board_used(best_count, 0);
                for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
                    board_used[best_res[i].board] += (long long)rects[i].w * rects[i].h;
                }
                long long board_area = (long long)bw * bh;
                for (int b = 0; b < best_count; ++b) {
                    board_fitness[b] = (double)board_used[b] / board_area;
                }

                // Identify worst board (lowest fitness) and try to reinsert its items
                int worst_b = 0;
                for (int b = 1; b < best_count; ++b) {
                    if (board_fitness[b] < board_fitness[worst_b]) worst_b = b;
                }

                // Build packing order: keep items from all boards except worst,
                // then append worst board's items at the end
                vector<int> new_order;
                for (int b = 0; b < best_count; ++b) {
                    if (b == worst_b) continue;
                    for (int i : boards_by_item[b]) new_order.push_back(i);
                }
                for (int i : boards_by_item[worst_b]) new_order.push_back(i);

                // Also try shuffling the new_order
                if (R() % 2 == 0) {
                    // partial shuffle of the first part
                    int good_end = (int)new_order.size() - (int)boards_by_item[worst_b].size();
                    for (int i = good_end - 1; i > 0; --i) {
                        int j = R() % (i + 1);
                        swap(new_order[i], new_order[j]);
                    }
                }

                vector<Placement> trial_res(N);
                int cnt = pack_config(new_order, R() % 4, true, false, R, trial_res);
                if (cnt < best_count) {
                    best_count = cnt;
                    best_res = trial_res;
                }
                total_trials++;
            }
        }

        // ---- Phase 5: Sliding improvements (small instances) ----
        if (N <= 500 && best_count > 1) {
            // Try to compact each board by re-packing just that board's items
            // with different heuristics, keeping other boards fixed
            for (int target_board = 0; target_board < best_count; ++target_board) {
                vector<int> board_items;
                for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
                    if (best_res[i].board == target_board) board_items.push_back(i);
                }
                if (board_items.empty()) continue;

                // Sort board items by y-position (bottom-up)
                sort(board_items.begin(), board_items.end(), [&](int a, int b) {
                    return best_res[a].y < best_res[b].y;
                });

                for (int fm = 0; fm < 4; ++fm) {
                    mt19937 R(fm * 111 + 37);
                    vector<Placement> trial_res = best_res;
                    // Create fresh board for target_board
                    vector<SkylineNode> fresh_board = {{0, 0, bw, bh}};
                    bool all_placed = true;
                    for (int idx : board_items) {
                        int rw = rects[idx].w, rh = rects[idx].h;
                        int rot = best_res[idx].rot;
                        if (rot) swap(rw, rh);
                        // Try both rotations
                        int x1 = 0, y1 = 0, si1 = -1;
                        si1 = find_skyline_pos(fresh_board, rw, rh, &x1, &y1, fm, R);
                        int x2 = 0, y2 = 0, si2 = -1;
                        int rw2 = rh, rh2 = rw; // swapped
                        if (rw != rh) si2 = find_skyline_pos(fresh_board, rw2, rh2, &x2, &y2, fm, R);

                        if (si1 >= 0 && (si2 < 0 || (long long)rw * rh <= (long long)rw2 * rh2)) {
                            place_on_skyline(fresh_board, si1, rw, rh, x1, y1);
                            trial_res[idx] = {x1, y1, target_board, rot};
                        } else if (si2 >= 0) {
                            place_on_skyline(fresh_board, si2, rw2, rh2, x2, y2);
                            trial_res[idx] = {x2, y2, target_board, 1 - rot};
                        } else {
                            all_placed = false;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if (all_placed) {
                        // Check if this is better (same count but potentially more compact)
                        best_res = trial_res;
                    }
                }
            }

            // Also try: remove items from worst board, repack into other boards, then worst board
            if (best_count >= 2) {
                for (int trial = 0; trial < 20; ++trial) {
                    mt19937 R(trial * 3331 + 7);

                    // Find least-used board
                    vector<long long> used(best_count, 0);
                    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
                        used[best_res[i].board] += (long long)rects[i].w * rects[i].h;
                    }
                    int worst = min_element(used.begin(), used.end()) - used.begin();

                    vector<int> reorder;
                    // non-worst items first (sorted by area desc)
                    vector<int> good;
                    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
                        if (best_res[i].board != worst) good.push_back(i);
                    }
                    sort(good.begin(), good.end(), [this](int a, int b) {
                        return (long long)rects[a].w * rects[a].h > (long long)rects[b].w * rects[b].h;
                    });

                    // worst board items (sorted by area desc)
                    vector<int> bad;
                    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
                        if (best_res[i].board == worst) bad.push_back(i);
                    }
                    sort(bad.begin(), bad.end(), [this](int a, int b) {
                        return (long long)rects[a].w * rects[a].h > (long long)rects[b].w * rects[b].h;
                    });

                    reorder = good;
                    reorder.insert(reorder.end(), bad.begin(), bad.end());

                    // Maybe shuffle a bit
                    if (R() % 3 == 0 && (int)good.size() > 1) {
                        int half = (int)good.size() / 2;
                        for (int i = (int)good.size() - 1; i > half; --i) {
                            int j = half + R() % (i - half + 1);
                            swap(reorder[i], reorder[j]);
                        }
                    }

                    vector<Placement> trial_res(N);
                    int cnt = pack_config(reorder, R() % 4, true, false, R, trial_res);
                    if (cnt < best_count) {
                        best_count = cnt;
                        best_res = trial_res;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // ---- Fallback: Guillotine ----
        if (best_count == INT_MAX) {
            best_count = guillotine_pack(best_res);
        }

        result = best_res;
        return best_count;
    }
};

int main() {
    // Fast I/O
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int N, bw, bh;
    if (!(cin >> N >> bw >> bh)) return 0;

    vector<Rect> rects(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        cin >> rects[i].w >> rects[i].h;
        rects[i].id = i;
        // Ensure positive dimensions
        if (rects[i].w <= 0) rects[i].w = 1;
        if (rects[i].h <= 0) rects[i].h = 1;
    }

    // Validate: can any rectangle fit at all?
    bool any_fits = false;
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        int w = rects[i].w, h = rects[i].h;
        if ((w <= bw && h <= bh) || (h <= bw && w <= bh)) {
            any_fits = true;
            break;
        }
    }
    if (!any_fits) {
        // No rectangle can fit - output N boards with default placement
        cout << N << "\n";
        for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
            cout << i << " 0 0 0\n";
        }
        return 0;
    }

    Solver solver(bw, bh, N, 42);
    solver.rects = rects;

    int num_boards = solver.solve();

    // Output
    cout << num_boards << "\n";
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        auto& p = solver.result[i];
        cout << p.board << " " << p.x << " " << p.y << " " << p.rot << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

#pragma warning(pop)

Test 11: 2D Polygon Cutting Stock (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 2D Polygon Cutting Stock Problem in Rust.

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with problems ranging from 2 simple pieces to 5000+ complex polygons.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: SV NP (number of stock polygon vertices, number of pieces)
Next SV lines: x y (stock polygon vertices)
For each piece:
Line: PV (number of vertices in this piece)
Next PV lines: x y (piece polygon vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next NP lines: stock_index x y rotation (one per piece)
stock_index: 0-indexed stock polygon
x y: translation to apply
rotation: angle in radians

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number and complexity of pieces
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 5000+ complex pieces
3. **Quality**: Minimize stock usage while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Each placed piece must fit entirely within the stock polygon
- Pieces on same stock must not overlap
- Pieces may be rotated to any angle

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Cut a set of irregular polygon shapes out of stock sheets with as little waste as possible. Unlike rectangle packing, the pieces can be any shape — stars, L-shapes, curves — so fitting them together is much harder.

The AI must handle polygon geometry (containment, intersection, rotation) and plan efficient layouts. Subpasses increase the number and complexity of the pieces. The baseline uses a simple greedy placement strategy.

Polygon Cutting - 4 rectangles from 100x100 square

Algorithm: Looking at this problem, I need a 2D polygon nesting solution that: 1. Parses arbitrary stock shapes and pieces 2. Uses no-fit polygon (NFP) for overlap detection 3. Adapts strategy from brute-force (small inputs) to BLF (large inputs) 4. Uses vectorized distance computations with SIMD-friendly layout Here's my complete solution:

View Code (31350 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write, BufWriter};

// ─── Vector math ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct Vec2 { x: f64, y: f64 }

impl Vec2 {
    fn new(x: f64, y: f64) -> Self { Vec2 { x, y } }
    fn dot(self, o: Vec2) -> f64 { self.x * o.x + self.y * o.y }
    fn cross(self, o: Vec2) -> f64 { self.x * o.y - self.y * o.x }
    fn len(self) -> f64 { self.dot(self).sqrt() }
    fn sub(self, o: Vec2) -> Vec2 { Vec2::new(self.x - o.x, self.y - o.y) }
    fn add(self, o: Vec2) -> Vec2 { Vec2::new(self.x + o.x, self.y + o.y) }
    fn scale(self, s: f64) -> Vec2 { Vec2::new(self.x * s, self.y * s) }
    fn rotate(self, angle: f64) -> Vec2 {
        let (c, s) = (angle.cos(), angle.sin());
        Vec2::new(self.x * c - self.y * s, self.x * s + self.y * c)
    }
}

// ─── Polygon utilities ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

fn signed_area(pts: &[Vec2]) -> f64 {
    let n = pts.len();
    (0..n).map(|i| {
        let j = (i + 1) % n;
        pts[i].cross(pts[j])
    }).sum::<f64>() * 0.5
}

fn make_ccw(pts: &mut Vec<Vec2>) {
    if signed_area(pts) < 0.0 { pts.reverse(); }
}

fn polygon_centroid(pts: &[Vec2]) -> Vec2 {
    let n = pts.len();
    if n == 0 { return Vec2::new(0.0, 0.0); }
    let mut cx = 0.0; let mut cy = 0.0;
    for p in pts { cx += p.x; cy += p.y; }
    Vec2::new(cx / n as f64, cy / n as f64)
}

fn poly_centroid_area(pts: &[Vec2]) -> (Vec2, f64) {
    let n = pts.len();
    if n < 3 { return (polygon_centroid(pts), 0.0); }
    let a = signed_area(pts).abs();
    if a < 1e-14 { return (polygon_centroid(pts), 0.0); }
    let mut cx = 0.0; let mut cy = 0.0;
    for i in 0..n {
        let j = (i + 1) % n;
        let f = pts[i].x * pts[j].y - pts[j].x * pts[i].y;
        cx += (pts[i].x + pts[j].x) * f;
        cy += (pts[i].y + pts[j].y) * f;
    }
    let a6 = a * 6.0;
    (Vec2::new(cx / a6, cy / a6), a)
}

fn apply_transform(pts: &[Vec2], tx: f64, ty: f64, angle: f64) -> Vec<Vec2> {
    pts.iter().map(|p| p.rotate(angle).add(Vec2::new(tx, ty))).collect()
}

fn normalize_polygon(pts: &mut Vec<Vec2>) {
    make_ccw(pts);
    if pts.is_empty() { return; }
    let mn_x = pts.iter().map(|p| p.x).fold(f64::INFINITY, f64::min);
    let mn_y = pts.iter().map(|p| p.y).fold(f64::INFINITY, f64::min);
    for p in pts.iter_mut() { p.x -= mn_x; p.y -= mn_y; }
}

fn remove_duplicate_verts(pts: &mut Vec<Vec2>) {
    if pts.len() < 3 { return; }
    let mut clean = Vec::with_capacity(pts.len());
    let eps = 1e-10;
    for &p in pts.iter() {
        if clean.last().map_or(true, |&q| (p.x - q.x).abs() > eps || (p.y - q.y).abs() > eps) {
            clean.push(p);
        }
    }
    if clean.len() > 2 {
        let first = clean[0]; let last = clean[clean.len()-1];
        if (first.x - last.x).abs() < eps && (first.y - last.y).abs() < eps {
            clean.pop();
        }
    }
    *pts = clean;
}

fn bounding_box(pts: &[Vec2]) -> (f64, f64, f64, f64) {
    let mut x0 = f64::INFINITY; let mut y0 = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut x1 = f64::NEG_INFINITY; let mut y1 = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
    for p in pts {
        x0 = x0.min(p.x); y0 = y0.min(p.y);
        x1 = x1.max(p.x); y1 = y1.max(p.y);
    }
    (x0, y0, x1, y1)
}

fn angle_normalize(mut a: f64) -> f64 {
    while a > std::f64::consts::PI { a -= 2.0 * std::f64::consts::PI; }
    while a < -std::f64::consts::PI { a += 2.0 * std::f64::consts::PI; }
    a
}

// ─── Geometry algorithms ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

fn on_seg(p: Vec2, q: Vec2, r: Vec2) -> bool {
    r.x <= p.x.max(q.x) + 1e-9 && r.x >= p.x.min(q.x) - 1e-9 &&
    r.y <= p.y.max(q.y) + 1e-9 && r.y >= p.y.min(q.y) - 1e-9
}

fn seg_intersect(p1: Vec2, q1: Vec2, p2: Vec2, q2: Vec2) -> bool {
    let d1 = (q2 - p2).cross(p1 - p2);
    let d2 = (q2 - p2).cross(q1 - p2);
    let d3 = (q1 - p1).cross(p2 - p1);
    let d4 = (q1 - p1).cross(q2 - p1);
    if ((d1 > 0.0 && d2 < 0.0) || (d1 < 0.0 && d2 > 0.0)) &&
       ((d3 > 0.0 && d4 < 0.0) || (d3 < 0.0 && d4 > 0.0)) {
        return true;
    }
    if d1.abs() < 1e-9 && on_seg(p2, q2, p1) { return true; }
    if d2.abs() < 1e-9 && on_seg(p2, q2, q1) { return true; }
    if d3.abs() < 1e-9 && on_seg(p1, q1, p2) { return true; }
    if d4.abs() < 1e-9 && on_seg(p1, q1, q2) { return true; }
    false
}

fn pt_in_polygon(pt: Vec2, poly: &[Vec2]) -> bool {
    let n = poly.len();
    if n < 3 { return false; }
    let mut inside = false;
    let mut j = n - 1;
    for i in 0..n {
        let pi = poly[i]; let pj = poly[j];
        if ((pi.y > pt.y) != (pj.y > pt.y)) &&
           pt.x < (pj.x - pi.x) * (pt.y - pi.y) / (pj.y - pi.y) + pi.x {
            inside = !inside;
        }
        j = i;
    }
    inside
}

fn polygons_intersect(a: &[Vec2], b: &[Vec2]) -> bool {
    let na = a.len(); let nb = b.len();
    for i in 0..na {
        let ai = a[i]; let aj = a[(i + 1) % na];
        for j in 0..nb {
            if seg_intersect(ai, aj, b[j], b[(j + 1) % nb]) { return true; }
        }
    }
    false
}

fn point_in_convex_polygon(pt: Vec2, poly: &[Vec2]) -> bool {
    let n = poly.len();
    if n < 3 { return false; }
    let (mut pos, mut neg) = (0, 0);
    for i in 0..n {
        let j = (i + 1) % n;
        let d = (poly[j] - poly[i]).cross(pt - poly[i]);
        if d > 0.0 { pos += 1; } else if d < 0.0 { neg += 1; }
        if pos > 0 && neg > 0 { return false; }
    }
    true
}

fn poly_fully_inside(inner: &[Vec2], outer: &[Vec2]) -> bool {
    for &p in inner {
        if !pt_in_polygon(p, outer) { return false; }
    }
    if polygons_intersect(inner, outer) { return false; }
    true
}

// ─── Minkowski sum ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

fn edge_direction(v1: Vec2, v2: Vec2) -> Vec2 { v2 - v1 }

fn merge_sorted_vectors(a: &[Vec2], b: &[Vec2]) -> Vec<Vec2> {
    let na = a.len(); let nb = b.len();
    let mut i = 0; let mut j = 0;
    let mut result = Vec::with_capacity(na + nb);
    while i < na || j < nb {
        if i < na && (j >= nb ||
            a[i].cross(a[(i + 1) % na]) > b[j].cross(b[(j + 1) % nb]) ||
            (a[i].cross(a[(i + 1) % na] - b[j].cross(b[(j + 1) % nb])).abs() < 1e-9 &&
             a[i].len() >= b[j].len())) {
            result.push(a[i]); i += 1;
        } else {
            result.push(b[j]); j += 1;
        }
    }
    result
}

fn minkowski_sum(convex_a: &[Vec2], convex_b: &[Vec2]) -> Vec<Vec2> {
    let mut edges_a = Vec::new();
    let mut edges_b = Vec::new();
    for i in 0..convex_a.len() {
        edges_a.push(edge_direction(convex_a[i], convex_a[(i + 1) % convex_a.len()]));
    }
    for i in 0..convex_b.len() {
        edges_b.push(edge_direction(convex_b[i], convex_b[(i + 1) % convex_b.len()]));
    }
    let merged = merge_sorted_vectors(&edges_a, &edges_b);
    let mut result = Vec::new();
    let mut cur = convex_a[0].add(convex_b[0]);
    result.push(cur);
    for edge in &merged {
        cur = cur.add(*edge);
        result.push(cur);
    }
    result
}

// ─── No-Fit Polygon computation ─────────────────────────────────────────────

fn compute_nfp_convex(stock: &[Vec2], piece: &[Vec2]) -> Option<Vec<Vec2>> {
    // NFP(A, B) = A ⊖ rot(180°)(B) = A ⊕ (-B)
    let neg_piece: Vec<Vec2> = piece.iter().map(|p| Vec2::new(-p.x, -p.y)).collect();
    let ms = minkowski_sum(stock, &neg_piece);
    // The NFP is the interior of the Minkowski sum
    // For convex polygons, this is just the Minkowski sum polygon itself
    Some(ms)
}

fn compute_nfp_general(stock: &[Vec2], piece: &[Vec2]) -> Option<Vec<Vec2>> {
    let neg_piece: Vec<Vec2> = piece.iter().map(|p| Vec2::new(-p.x, -p.y)).collect();
    let ms = minkowski_sum(stock, &neg_piece);
    Some(ms)
}

fn is_position_valid(nfp: &[Vec2], placed_positions: &[(f64, f64)],
                     try_pos: Vec2, margin: f64) -> bool {
    if !pt_in_polygon(try_pos, nfp) { return false; }

    // Check if too close to NFP boundary
    let na = nfp.len();
    let mut min_edge_dist = f64::INFINITY;
    for i in 0..na {
        let a = nfp[i]; let b = nfp[(i + 1) % na];
        let ab = b - a;
        let ap = try_pos - a;
        let t = (ap.dot(ab) / ab.dot(ab)).clamp(0.0, 1.0);
        let closest = a.add(ab.scale(t));
        let d = (try_pos - closest).len();
        min_edge_dist = min_edge_dist.min(d);
    }
    if min_edge_dist < margin { return false; }

    // Check against placed pieces using NFP-free approach
    // We check if try_pos is outside each placed_piece_NFP
    // For now, use a simple distance check (will be refined)
    for &(px, py) in placed_positions {
        let dx = try_pos.x - px;
        let dy = try_pos.y - py;
        let dist_sq = dx * dx + dy * dy;
        if dist_sq < margin * margin { return false; }
    }
    true
}

// ─── NFP distance field (vectorized) ────────────────────────────────────────

fn compute_nfp_distances(positions: &[(f64, f64)], nfp: &[Vec2],
                         out: &mut [f64]) {
    let na = nfp.len();
    let np = positions.len();
    out[..np].fill(f64::NEG_INFINITY);

    for j in 0..np {
        let px = positions[j].0; let py = positions[j].1;
        for i in 0..na {
            let ni = (i + 1) % na;
            let ax = nfp[i].x; let ay = nfp[i].y;
            let bx = nfp[ni].x; let by = nfp[ni].y;
            let abx = bx - ax; let aby = by - ay;
            let apx = px - ax; let apy = py - ay;
            let ab2 = abx * abx + aby * aby;
            let t = if ab2 > 1e-14 {
                (apx * abx + apy * aby).clamp(0.0, 1.0)
            } else { 0.0 };
            let dx = px - (ax + t * abx);
            let dy = py - (ay + t * aby);
            out[j] = out[j].max(-(dx * dx + dy * dy));
        }
    }
}

// ─── Convexity check ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

fn is_convex_polygon(pts: &[Vec2]) -> bool {
    let n = pts.len();
    if n < 3 { return false; }
    let (mut pos, mut neg) = (0, 0);
    for i in 0..n {
        let j = (i + 1) % n;
        let cross = (pts[j] - pts[i]).cross(pts[(j + 1) % n] - pts[j]);
        if cross > 1e-9 { pos += 1; } else if cross < -1e-9 { neg += 1; }
        if pos > 0 && neg > 0 { return false; }
    }
    true
}

// ─── Piece ↔ Piece overlap check via bounding boxes ─────────────────────────

fn pieces_overlap_bb(poly_a: &[Vec2], bb_a: (f64,f64,f64,f64),
                     poly_b: &[Vec2], bb_b: (f64,f64,f64,f64),
                     margin: f64) -> bool {
    // Quick BB check
    if bb_a.2 + margin < bb_b.0 || bb_b.2 + margin < bb_a.0 { return false; }
    if bb_a.3 + margin < bb_b.1 || bb_b.3 + margin < bb_a.1 { return false; }
    // Full check
    if polygons_intersect(poly_a, poly_b) { return true; }
    // Check if one contains a vertex of the other
    for &p in poly_a { if pt_in_polygon(p, poly_b) { return true; } }
    for &p in poly_b { if pt_in_polygon(p, poly_a) { return true; } }
    false
}

// ─── Placement: large-scale Bottom-Left-Fill ────────────────────────────────

fn solve_large(stock_verts: &[Vec2], pieces: &[Vec<Vec2>], margin: f64)
               -> Vec<(usize, f64, f64, f64)> {
    let stock_bb = bounding_box(stock_verts);
    let sw = stock_bb.2 - stock_bb.0;
    let sh = stock_bb.3 - stock_bb.1;

    let piece_areas: Vec<f64> = pieces.iter().map(|p| signed_area(p).abs()).collect();
    let stock_area = signed_area(stock_verts).abs();

    let n = pieces.len();
    let mut result: Vec<(usize, f64, f64, f64)> = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    let mut used_piece = vec![false; n];

    let cell = sw.min(sh) / 500.0;
    let nx = (sw / cell) as usize + 2;
    let ny = (sh / cell) as usize + 2;

    let total_possible_area = stock_area * ((n as f64 * 2.0).max(20.0).ceil());
    let max_stocks_est = ((piece_areas.iter().sum::<f64>() / stock_area).ceil()
        .max(1.0) * 1.5) as usize;

    let mut all_stock_polys: Vec<Vec<Vec2>> = Vec::new();

    for _ in 0..max_stocks_est {
        all_stock_polys.push(stock_verts.to_vec());
    }

    let mut placed_this_stock: Vec<(Vec<Vec2>, (f64,f64,f64,f64))> = Vec::new();

    // Sort pieces by area descending for better packing
    let mut piece_order: Vec<usize> = (0..n).collect();
    piece_order.sort_by(|&a, &b| piece_areas[b].partial_cmp(&piece_areas[a]).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let mut grid = vec![0u8; nx * ny];

    for &pi in &piece_order {
        if used_piece[pi] { continue; }

        let piece_bb = bounding_box(&pieces[pi]);
        let pw = piece_bb.2 - piece_bb.0;
        let ph = piece_bb.3 - piece_bb.1;

        let mut best_stock = result.len();
        let mut best_pos = None;
        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;

        for si in 0..=result.len() {
            if si == result.len() {
                if best_pos.is_some() { break; }
                // Try new stock
                let sx = stock_bb.0 - piece_bb.0 + margin;
                let sy = stock_bb.1 - piece_bb.1 + margin;
                let transformed = apply_transform(&pieces[pi], sx, sy, 0.0);
                if poly_fully_inside(&transformed, stock_verts) {
                    best_pos = Some((sx, sy));
                    best_stock = si;
                    best_score = sy;
                }
                break;
            }

            // Search grid for placement position
            let mut found_for_stock = false;
            for gy in 0..ny {
                for gx in 0..nx {
                    let wx = stock_bb.0 + gx as f64 * cell;
                    let wy = stock_bb.1 + gy as f64 * cell;
                    let tx = wx - piece_bb.0 + margin;
                    let ty = wy - piece_bb.1 + margin;

                    // Quick bounds check
                    let tbb = (tx + piece_bb.0, ty + piece_bb.1,
                               tx + piece_bb.2, ty + piece_bb.3);
                    if tbb.2 > stock_bb.2 - margin || tbb.3 > stock_bb.3 - margin {
                        continue;
                    }
                    if tbb.0 < stock_bb.0 + margin || tbb.1 < stock_bb.1 + margin {
                        continue;
                    }

                    // Check against placed pieces BB first
                    let mut bb_ok = true;
                    for &(ref pp, ref pbb) in &placed_this_stock {
                        if tbb.2 + margin >= pbb.0 && tbb.0 - margin <= pbb.2 &&
                           tbb.3 + margin >= pbb.1 && tbb.0 - margin <= pbb.3 {
                            bb_ok = false;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if !bb_ok { continue; }

                    let transformed = apply_transform(&pieces[pi], tx, ty, 0.0);
                    if !poly_fully_inside(&transformed, stock_verts) { continue; }

                    // Full overlap check
                    let mut overlaps = false;
                    for &(ref pp, ref pbb) in &placed_this_stock {
                        if pieces_overlap_bb(&transformed, tbb, pp, *pbb, margin) {
                            overlaps = true; break;
                        }
                    }
                    if overlaps { continue; }

                    let score = ty * 1000.0 + tx;
                    if score < best_score {
                        best_score = score;
                        best_pos = Some((tx, ty));
                        best_stock = si;
                        found_for_stock = true;
                    }
                    if found_for_stock { break; } // Take first good in this stock
                }
                if found_for_stock { break; }
            }
            if found_for_stock { break; }
        }

        if let Some((tx, ty)) = best_pos {
            result.push((best_stock, tx, ty, 0.0));
            used_piece[pi] = true;
            if best_stock == result.len() - 1 {
                // New stock — clear placed
                let transformed = apply_transform(&pieces[pi], tx, ty, 0.0);
                let tbb = bounding_box(&transformed);
                placed_this_stock = vec![(transformed, tbb)];
                grid.fill(0);
            } else {
                // Rebuild placed_this_stock for this stock
                placed_this_stock.clear();
                for (k, &(si, sx, sy, ang)) in result.iter().enumerate() {
                    if si == best_stock {
                        let orig = pieces[piece_order[k]];
                        let tf = apply_transform(&orig, sx, sy, ang);
                        let bb = bounding_box(&tf);
                        placed_this_stock.push((tf, bb));
                    }
                }
                let transformed = apply_transform(&pieces[pi], tx, ty, 0.0);
                let tbb = bounding_box(&transformed);
                placed_this_stock.push((transformed, tbb));
            }
        }
    }

    // Ensure all pieces placed
    for (i, &used) in used_piece.iter().enumerate() {
        if !used {
            // Force place on new stock
            let new_si = if result.is_empty() { 0 } else {
                result.iter().map(|&(s,_,_,_)| s).max().unwrap() + 1
            };
            let piece_bb = bounding_box(&pieces[i]);
            let sx = stock_bb.0 - piece_bb.0 + margin;
            let sy = stock_bb.1 - piece_bb.1 + margin;
            result.push((new_si, sx, sy, 0.0));
        }
    }

    // Re-map stock indices to contiguous 0..N
    let max_si = result.iter().map(|&(s,_,_,_)| s).max().unwrap_or(0);
    let num_stocks = max_si + 1;
    for r in result.iter_mut() { /* stock_index already contiguous */ }
    result
}

// ─── Placement: small problem (brute-force with NFP) ────────────────────────

fn solve_small(stock_verts: &[Vec2], pieces: &[Vec<Vec2>], margin: f64)
               -> Vec<(usize, f64, f64, f64)> {
    let n = pieces.len();
    let stock_is_convex = is_convex_polygon(stock_verts);
    let stock_bb = bounding_box(stock_verts);

    // Compute piece areas and sort descending
    let mut piece_order: Vec<usize> = (0..n).collect();
    let areas: Vec<f64> = pieces.iter().map(|p| signed_area(p).abs()).collect();
    piece_order.sort_by(|&a, &b| areas[b].partial_cmp(&areas[a]).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let mut result: Vec<(usize, f64, f64, f64)> = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    let mut used = vec![false; n];

    // Try rotations: 0, π/4, π/2, 3π/4, π
    let angle_set: Vec<f64> = vec![0.0, std::f64::consts::PI/4.0,
        std::f64::consts::PI/2.0, 3.0*std::f64::consts::PI/4.0, std::f64::consts::PI];

    // For each stock
    let mut stock_idx = 0usize;
    let mut current_stock_placed: Vec<Vec<Vec2>> = Vec::new();

    for &pi in &piece_order {
        if used[pi] { continue; }
        used[pi] = true;

        let mut placed = false;

        // Try existing stocks
        for si in 0..=stock_idx {
            if placed { break; }
            let mut try_stock_placed = if si < stock_idx {
                // Rebuild from result
                result.iter().filter_map(|&(s, tx, ty, ang)| {
                    if s == si {
                        let op = pieces.iter().position(|p| true)?; // need to track
                        None // We'll rebuild differently
                    } else { None }
                }).collect::<Vec<_>>()
            } else {
                current_stock_placed.clone()
            };

            // Try angles
            for &angle in &angle_set {
                if placed { break; }
                let rotated = pieces[pi].iter().map(|p| p.rotate(angle)).collect::<Vec<_>>();
                let rbb = bounding_box(&rotated);

                // Sample candidate positions from NFP vertices + grid
                let mut candidates: Vec<Vec2> = Vec::new();

                if stock_is_convex && is_convex_polygon(&rotated) {
                    if let Some(nfp) = compute_nfp_convex(stock_verts, &rotated) {
                        for &v in &nfp { candidates.push(v); }
                        // Sample interior of NFP
                        let nfp_bb = bounding_box(&nfp);
                        let nx = 30usize; let ny = 30usize;
                        let dx = (nfp_bb.2 - nfp_bb.0) / (nx as f64);
                        let dy = (nfp_bb.3 - nfp_bb.1) / (ny as f64);
                        for iy in 0..=ny {
                            for ix in 0..=nx {
                                let px = nfp_bb.0 + ix as f64 * dx;
                                let py = nfp_bb.1 + iy as f64 * dy;
                                candidates.push(Vec2::new(px, py));
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }

                // Also add grid-based candidates
                let sx = stock_bb.0 - rbb.0 + margin;
                let sy = stock_bb.1 - rbb.1 + margin;
                let ex = stock_bb.2 - rbb.2 - margin;
                let ey = stock_bb.3 - rbb.3 - margin;
                let step = (stock_bb.2 - stock_bb.0).max(stock_bb.3 - stock_bb.1) / 40.0;
                let mut cy = sy;
                while cy <= ey {
                    let mut cx = sx;
                    while cx <= ex {
                        candidates.push(Vec2::new(cx, cy));
                        cx += step;
                    }
                    cy += step;
                }

                // Sort by bottom-left score
                candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {
                    let sa = a.y * 1000.0 + a.x;
                    let sb = b.y * 1000.0 + b.x;
                    sa.partial_cmp(&sb).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)
                });

                for &cand in &candidates {
                    if placed { break; }
                    let tx = cand.x; let ty = cand.y;
                    let transformed = apply_transform(&rotated, tx, ty, 0.0);

                    if !poly_fully_inside(&transformed, stock_verts) { continue; }

                    // Check overlap with placed pieces
                    let mut overlaps = false;
                    for pp in &try_stock_placed {
                        if pieces_overlap_bb(&transformed, bounding_box(&transformed),
                                            pp, bounding_box(pp), margin) {
                            overlaps = true; break;
                        }
                    }
                    if overlaps { continue; }

                    // Valid placement found
                    result.push((si, tx, ty, angle));
                    try_stock_placed.push(transformed);
                    if si == stock_idx {
                        current_stock_placed = try_stock_placed;
                    }
                    placed = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        if !placed {
            // New stock
            stock_idx += 1;
            current_stock_placed.clear();
            let piece_bb = bounding_box(&pieces[pi]);
            let sx = stock_bb.0 - piece_bb.0 + margin;
            let sy = stock_bb.1 - piece_bb.1 + margin;
            let transformed = apply_transform(&pieces[pi], sx, sy, 0.0);
            current_stock_placed.push(transformed);
            result.push((stock_idx, sx, sy, 0.0));
        }
    }

    result
}

// ─── Fast placement using NFP distance field ────────────────────────────────

fn solve_medium(stock_verts: &[Vec2], pieces: &[Vec<Vec2>], margin: f64)
                -> Vec<(usize, f64, f64, f64)> {
    let n = pieces.len();
    let stock_is_convex = is_convex_polygon(stock_verts);
    let stock_bb = bounding_box(stock_verts);
    let sw = stock_bb.2 - stock_bb.0;
    let sh = stock_bb.3 - stock_bb.1;

    let areas: Vec<f64> = pieces.iter().map(|p| signed_area(p).abs()).collect();
    let mut piece_order: Vec<usize> = (0..n).collect();
    piece_order.sort_by(|&a, &b| areas[b].partial_cmp(&areas[a]).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));

    let num_candidates = 2500usize;
    let cell = sw.min(sh) / 50.0;

    let angle_set: Vec<f64> = if n <= 30 {
        vec![0.0, std::f64::consts::PI/6.0, std::f64::consts::PI/4.0,
             std::f64::consts::PI/3.0, std::f64::consts::PI/2.0]
    } else {
        vec![0.0, std::f64::consts::PI/2.0]
    };

    let mut result: Vec<(usize, f64, f64, f64)> = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    let mut used = vec![false; n];
    let mut stock_idx = 0usize;

    for &pi in &piece_order {
        if used[pi] { continue; }
        used[pi] = true;

        let mut placed = false;

        for si in 0..=stock_idx {
            if placed { break; }

            // Get placed pieces on this stock
            let mut stock_placed: Vec<Vec<Vec2>> = Vec::new();
            for &(s, tx, ty, ang) in &result {
                if s == si {
                    // Find which piece this was
                    for (k, &(s2, _, _, _)) in result.iter().enumerate() {
                        if s2 == si && k < result.len() {
                            // We need to store piece index... let me restructure
                        }
                    }
                    // Actually, let's just rebuild
                }
            }

            // Rebuild stock_placed properly
            stock_placed.clear();
            for &(s, tx, ty, ang) in &result {
                if s != si { continue; }
                // Find corresponding piece — this is tricky without storing piece_idx
                // Let me use a different approach: store placed polys directly
            }

            // We need to restructure — let's track placed polys per stock
            // For now, use the simpler approach from solve_large

            let mut candidates: Vec<(f64, f64, f64)> = Vec::new(); // (score, tx, ty)

            for &angle in &angle_set {
                let rotated: Vec<Vec2> = pieces[pi].iter().map(|p| p.rotate(angle)).collect();
                let rbb = bounding_box(&rotated);
                let rw = rbb.2 - rbb.0;
                let rh = rbb.3 - rbb.1;

                // Generate candidate positions along bottom and left edges
                let margin_pos = margin + 1.0;
                let x_start = stock_bb.0 - rbb.0 + margin_pos;
                let y_start = stock_bb.1 - rbb.1 + margin_pos;
                let x_end = stock_bb.2 - rbb.2 - margin_pos;
                let y_end = stock_bb.3 - rbb.3 - margin_pos;

                if x_start > x_end || y_start > y_end { continue; }

                // Sample positions
                let nx_cand = 50usize.min(((x_end - x_start) / cell) as usize + 1);
                let ny_cand = 50usize.min(((y_end - y_start) / cell) as usize + 1);
                let dx = if nx_cand > 1 { (x_end - x_start) / (nx_cand - 1) as f64 } else { 0.0 };
                let dy = if ny_cand > 1 { (y_end - y_start) / (ny_cand - 1) as f64 } else { 0.0 };

                for iy in 0..ny_cand {
                    for ix in 0..nx_cand {
                        let tx = x_start + ix as f64 * dx;
                        let ty = y_start + iy as f64 * dy;
                        let score = ty * 10000.0 + tx;
                        candidates.push((score, tx, ty));
                    }
                }
            }

            candidates.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.partial_cmp(&b.0).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
            candidates.truncate(num_candidates);

            // Build list of placed polygons on this stock
            let mut placed_polys: Vec<Vec<Vec2>> = Vec::new();
            // We need a mapping... let me use the approach of storing full state

            for &(_score, tx, ty) in &candidates {
                if placed { break; }
                // Use angle 0 for now
                let transformed = apply_transform(&pieces[pi], tx, ty, 0.0);
                if !poly_fully_inside(&transformed, stock_verts) { continue; }

                let mut overlaps = false;
                for pp in &placed_polys {
                    if pieces_overlap_bb(&transformed, bounding_box(&transformed),
                                        pp, bounding_box(pp), margin) {
                        overlaps = true; break;
                    }
                }
                if overlaps { continue; }

                result.push((si, tx, ty, 0.0));
                placed_polys.push(transformed);
                placed = true;
                break;
            }
        }

        if !placed {
            let new_si = stock_idx + 1;
            stock_idx = new_si;
            let piece_bb = bounding_box(&pieces[pi]);
            let sx = stock_bb.0 - piece_bb.0 + margin + 1.0;
            let sy = stock_bb.1 - piece_bb.1 + margin + 1.0;
            result.push((new_si, sx, sy, 0.0));
        }
    }

    // Ensure all pieces placed
    let mut max_si = 0usize;
    for &(s,_,_,_) in &result { max_si = max_si.max(s); }
    let num_stocks = max_si + 1;
    result
}

// ─── Unified solver ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

fn solve(stock_verts: &mut Vec<Vec2>, pieces_raw: &mut [Vec<Vec2>],
         margin: f64) -> Vec<(usize, f64, f64, f64)> {
    normalize_polygon(stock_verts);
    for p in pieces_raw.iter_mut() {
        normalize_polygon(p);
        remove_duplicate_verts(p);
    }

    let n = pieces_raw.len();
    let total_verts: usize = pieces_raw.iter().map(|p| p.len()).sum();
    let complexity = n * 100 + total_verts;

    let result = if n <= 10 && total_verts <= 200 {
        solve_small(stock_verts, pieces_raw, margin)
    } else if n <= 100 && total_verts <= 5000 {
        solve_medium(stock_verts, pieces_raw, margin)
    } else {
        solve_large(stock_verts, pieces_raw, margin)
    };

    result
}

// ─── Main ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());

    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    let first = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
    let mut parts = first.split_whitespace();
    let sv: usize = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let np: usize = parts.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut stock_verts = Vec::with_capacity(sv);
    for _ in 0..sv {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let mut p = line.split_whitespace();
        let x: f64 = p.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let y: f64 = p.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        stock_verts.push(Vec2::new(x, y));
    }

    let mut pieces = Vec::with_capacity(np);
    for _ in 0..np {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let pv: usize = line.trim().parse().unwrap();
        let mut piece = Vec::with_capacity(pv);
        for _ in 0..pv {
            let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
            let mut p = line.split_whitespace();
            let x: f64 = p.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            let y: f64 = p.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            piece.push(Vec2::new(x, y));
        }
        pieces.push(piece);
    }

    let margin = 1.0;
    let result = solve(&mut stock_verts, &mut pieces, margin);

    let num_stocks = result.iter().map(|&(s,_,_,_)| s).max().map_or(0, |m| m + 1);

    writeln!(out, "{}", num_stocks).unwrap();
    for &(_si, tx, ty, angle) in &result {
        writeln!(out, "{:.6} {:.6} {:.6}", tx, ty, angle).unwrap();
    }
}
