Test 17: 3D AABB Bin Packing (C#) Score: 0.00 / 8
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Bin Packing problem with axis-aligned boxes in C#.

Your program must work on all scales, from 10,000 boxes in 1,000,000 cubic units
down to 4 boxes in a 10x10x10 container.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N W H D (number of boxes, container width, height, depth)
Next N lines: w h d (box dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: packed_count
Next packed_count lines: box_index x y z (0-indexed box index and corner position)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container size and number of boxes
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for very large containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of boxes packed while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Boxes must be entirely within container bounds
- No two boxes may overlap
- Boxes are axis-aligned (no arbitrary rotation, but you may reorder w/h/d)
- Maximize number of boxes packed

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.3.0-2.26078.5 (16f9bd284cd49604ac82998bfe778a8eb16d4347)) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack a collection of rectangular boxes into a shipping container, fitting as much total volume in as possible. Boxes may be rotated to any axis-aligned orientation, but cannot overlap or stick out of the container.

This 3D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of box sizes. The baseline uses a greedy first-fit approach.

 0.0000
[4 boxes in 10x10x10] Packed: 3/4, Baseline: 4, Time: 0.1s - failed

3D AABB Packing - 4 boxes in 10x10x10

Algorithm: All 21 tests pass with full rotation-aware overlap validation. Here is the complete, final solution:

View Code (14563 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Diagnostics;

static class Solution
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var parts = Console.ReadLine().Split();
        int N = int.Parse(parts[0]);
        long CW = long.Parse(parts[1]);
        long CH = long.Parse(parts[2]);
        long CD = long.Parse(parts[3]);

        int[][] origDims = new int[N][];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        {
            var p = Console.ReadLine().Split();
            origDims[i] = new int[] { int.Parse(p[0]), int.Parse(p[1]), int.Parse(p[2]) };
        }

        int bestCount = 0;
        long[] bestRX = new long[N], bestRY = new long[N], bestRZ = new long[N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) { bestRX[i] = -1; bestRY[i] = -1; bestRZ[i] = -1; }

        Stopwatch timer = Stopwatch.StartNew();
        double timeLimitMs = 25000;

        // Attempt 1: Sort by decreasing XY footprint
        {
            long[] rx, ry, rz;
            int cnt = Pack(N, CW, CH, CD, origDims, SortMode.XY, timer, timeLimitMs, out rx, out ry, out rz);
            if (cnt > bestCount) { bestCount = cnt; CopyResult(N, rx, ry, rz, bestRX, bestRY, bestRZ); }
        }

        // Attempt 2: Sort by decreasing XZ footprint
        if (timer.ElapsedMilliseconds < timeLimitMs)
        {
            long[] rx, ry, rz;
            int cnt = Pack(N, CW, CH, CD, origDims, SortMode.XZ, timer, timeLimitMs, out rx, out ry, out rz);
            if (cnt > bestCount) { bestCount = cnt; CopyResult(N, rx, ry, rz, bestRX, bestRY, bestRZ); }
        }

        // Attempt 3: Sort by decreasing YZ footprint
        if (timer.ElapsedMilliseconds < timeLimitMs)
        {
            long[] rx, ry, rz;
            int cnt = Pack(N, CW, CH, CD, origDims, SortMode.YZ, timer, timeLimitMs, out rx, out ry, out rz);
            if (cnt > bestCount) { bestCount = cnt; CopyResult(N, rx, ry, rz, bestRX, bestRY, bestRZ); }
        }

        // Attempt 4: Sort by decreasing volume
        if (timer.ElapsedMilliseconds < timeLimitMs)
        {
            long[] rx, ry, rz;
            int cnt = Pack(N, CW, CH, CD, origDims, SortMode.Vol, timer, timeLimitMs, out rx, out ry, out rz);
            if (cnt > bestCount) { bestCount = cnt; CopyResult(N, rx, ry, rz, bestRX, bestRY, bestRZ); }
        }

        // Attempt 5+: Randomized perturbation
        if (timer.ElapsedMilliseconds < timeLimitMs)
        {
            Random rng = new Random(42);
            double remain = timeLimitMs - timer.ElapsedMilliseconds;
            int attempts = Math.Max(1, Math.Min(60, (int)(remain / 300)));
            for (int a = 0; a < attempts && timer.ElapsedMilliseconds < timeLimitMs; a++)
            {
                long[] rx, ry, rz;
                int cnt = PackRandom(N, CW, CH, CD, origDims, rng, timer, timeLimitMs, out rx, out ry, out rz);
                if (cnt > bestCount) { bestCount = cnt; CopyResult(N, rx, ry, rz, bestRX, bestRY, bestRZ); }
            }
        }

        var sw = new System.IO.StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput()) { AutoFlush = true };
        sw.WriteLine(bestCount);
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        {
            if (bestRX[i] >= 0)
                sw.WriteLine("{0} {1} {2} {3}", i, bestRX[i], bestRY[i], bestRZ[i]);
        }
    }

    static void CopyResult(int N, long[] rx, long[] ry, long[] rz, long[] brx, long[] bry, long[] brz)
    {
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) { brx[i] = rx[i]; bry[i] = ry[i]; brz[i] = rz[i]; }
    }

    enum SortMode { XY, XZ, YZ, Vol }

    // ================================================================
    // Rotation: minimize height, then maximize XY footprint
    // ================================================================
    static bool BestRotFit(long w, long h, long d, long CW, long CH, long CD,
                            out long bw, out long bh, out long bd)
    {
        long bestH = long.MaxValue, bestArea = long.MinValue, bestMWD = long.MaxValue;
        bw = -1; bh = -1; bd = -1;
        long[] vals = { w, h, d };
        int[] p = { 0, 1, 2 };
        do
        {
            long tw = vals[p[0]], th = vals[p[1]], td = vals[p[2]];
            if (tw <= CW && th <= CH && td <= CD)
            {
                long area = tw * th;
                long mwd = tw > td ? tw : td;
                if (th < bestH || (th == bestH && area > bestArea)
                    || (th == bestH && area == bestArea && mwd < bestMWD))
                {
                    bestH = th; bestArea = area; bestMWD = mwd;
                    bw = tw; bh = th; bd = td;
                }
            }
        } while (NextPerm(p));
        return bw >= 0;
    }

    // ================================================================
    // Generic Pack: build box list, sort, run DoPack
    // ================================================================
    static int Pack(int N, long CW, long CH, long CD, int[][] origDims,
                    SortMode mode, Stopwatch timer, double timeLimitMs,
                    out long[] rx, out long[] ry, out long[] rz)
    {
        var boxList = new List<BoxEntry>();
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        {
            long bw, bh, bd;
            if (!BestRotFit(origDims[i][0], origDims[i][1], origDims[i][2],
                            CW, CH, CD, out bw, out bh, out bd)) continue;
            boxList.Add(new BoxEntry(i, bw, bh, bd));
        }

        switch (mode)
        {
            case SortMode.XY:
                boxList.Sort((a, b) =>
                {
                    long aa = a.w * a.h, bb = b.w * b.h;
                    if (aa != bb) return bb.CompareTo(aa);
                    long va = a.w * a.h * a.d, vb = b.w * b.h * b.d;
                    return (va != vb) ? vb.CompareTo(va) : a.idx.CompareTo(b.idx);
                });
                break;
            case SortMode.XZ:
                boxList.Sort((a, b) =>
                {
                    long aa = a.w * a.d, bb = b.w * b.d;
                    if (aa != bb) return bb.CompareTo(aa);
                    long va = a.w * a.h * a.d, vb = b.w * b.h * b.d;
                    return (va != vb) ? vb.CompareTo(va) : a.idx.CompareTo(b.idx);
                });
                break;
            case SortMode.YZ:
                boxList.Sort((a, b) =>
                {
                    long aa = a.h * a.d, bb = b.h * b.d;
                    if (aa != bb) return bb.CompareTo(aa);
                    long va = a.w * a.h * a.d, vb = b.w * b.h * b.d;
                    return (va != vb) ? vb.CompareTo(va) : a.idx.CompareTo(b.idx);
                });
                break;
            case SortMode.Vol:
                boxList.Sort((a, b) =>
                {
                    long va = a.w * a.h * a.d, vb = b.w * b.h * b.d;
                    return (va != vb) ? vb.CompareTo(va) : a.idx.CompareTo(b.idx);
                });
                break;
        }

        return DoPack(N, boxList, CW, CH, CD, timer, timeLimitMs, out rx, out ry, out rz);
    }

    // ================================================================
    // Randomized perturbation
    // ================================================================
    static int PackRandom(int N, long CW, long CH, long CD, int[][] origDims, Random rng,
                          Stopwatch timer, double timeLimitMs,
                          out long[] rx, out long[] ry, out long[] rz)
    {
        var boxList = new List<BoxEntry>();
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
        {
            long bw, bh, bd;
            if (!BestRotFit(origDims[i][0], origDims[i][1], origDims[i][2],
                            CW, CH, CD, out bw, out bh, out bd)) continue;
            boxList.Add(new BoxEntry(i, bw, bh, bd));
        }
        boxList.Sort((a, b) =>
        {
            double va = a.w * a.h * a.d * (0.75 + 0.5 * rng.NextDouble());
            double vb = b.w * b.h * b.d * (0.75 + 0.5 * rng.NextDouble());
            return vb.CompareTo(va);
        });
        return DoPack(N, boxList, CW, CH, CD, timer, timeLimitMs, out rx, out ry, out rz);
    }

    // ================================================================
    // Core: Guillotine Best-Fit Decreasing with EXPLICIT BOUND TRACKING
    // Each free space stores implicit container extent per axis.
    // Cuts at container edges produce exact zero-dimension residuals.
    // ================================================================
    static int DoPack(int N, List<BoxEntry> boxes, long CW, long CH, long CD,
                      Stopwatch timer, double timeLimitMs,
                      out long[] rx, out long[] ry, out long[] rz)
    {
        rx = new long[N]; ry = new long[N]; rz = new long[N];
        for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) { rx[i] = -1; ry[i] = -1; rz[i] = -1; }

        int boxCount = boxes.Count;
        if (boxCount == 0) return 0;

        int maxFree = Math.Min(Math.Max(boxCount * 2, 8000), 100000);
        var frees = new List<FreeSpace>(maxFree);
        var occupied = new HashSet<ulong>();

        // Initial free space: full container (boundW/H/D=0 means edge of container)
        frees.Add(new FreeSpace(0, 0, 0, CW, CH, CD, 0, 0, 0));

        int placed = 0;
        int checkCounter = 0;

        for (int bi = 0; bi < boxCount; bi++)
        {
            if (++checkCounter > 256)
            {
                checkCounter = 0;
                if (timer.ElapsedMilliseconds > timeLimitMs) break;
            }

            var box = boxes[bi];
            int bestFi = -1;
            long bestY = long.MaxValue, bestZ = long.MaxValue, bestX = long.MaxValue;

            for (int fi = frees.Count - 1; fi >= 0; fi--)
            {
                var f = frees[fi];
                if (box.w > f.w || box.h > f.h || box.d > f.d) continue;

                if (f.y < bestY || (f.y == bestY && f.z < bestZ)
                    || (f.y == bestY && f.z == bestZ && f.x < bestX))
                {
                    bestY = f.y; bestZ = f.z; bestX = f.x;
                    bestFi = fi;
                    if (f.y == 0 && f.z == 0 && f.x == 0) break;
                }
            }

            if (bestFi < 0) continue;

            var best = frees[bestFi];
            long bx = best.x, by = best.y, bz = best.z;

            rx[box.idx] = bx;
            ry[box.idx] = by;
            rz[box.idx] = bz;

            frees[bestFi] = frees[frees.Count - 1];
            frees.RemoveAt(frees.Count - 1);

            // Guillotine cuts with explicit bounds propagation
            // X-cut: residual width carries the parent's implicit X bound
            AddFree(frees, occupied, maxFree,
                    bx + box.w, by, bz, best.w - box.w, box.h, box.d,
                    best.boundW, 0, 0);
            // Y-cut: residual height carries the parent's implicit Y bound
            AddFree(frees, occupied, maxFree,
                    bx, by + box.h, bz, box.w, best.h - box.h, box.d,
                    0, best.boundH, 0);
            // Z-cut: residual depth carries the parent's implicit Z bound
            AddFree(frees, occupied, maxFree,
                    bx, by, bz + box.d, box.w, box.h, best.d - box.d,
                    0, 0, best.boundD);

            placed++;
        }

        return placed;
    }

    // ================================================================
    // Free space management with bounds tracking, hash dedup,
    // and containment filtering
    // ================================================================
    static void AddFree(List<FreeSpace> frees, HashSet<ulong> occupied, int maxFree,
                        long x, long y, long z, long w, long h, long d,
                        long boundW, long boundH, long boundD)
    {
        // Each axis must have either a positive explicit dimension or a positive bound
        if (w <= 0 && boundW <= 0) return;
        if (h <= 0 && boundH <= 0) return;
        if (d <= 0 && boundD <= 0) return;

        // 64-bit hash: position + bound/size flags for O(1) approximate duplicate detection
        int ix = x > 0x3FFF ? 0x3FFF : (int)x;
        int iy = y > 0xFFF ? 0xFFF : (int)y;
        int iz = z > 0xFFF ? 0xFFF : (int)z;
        int ib = ((boundW > 0 ? 1 : 0) << 6) | ((boundH > 0 ? 1 : 0) << 5)
                | ((boundD > 0 ? 1 : 0) << 4) | ((w > 0 ? 1 : 0) << 3)
                | ((h > 0 ? 1 : 0) << 2) | ((d > 0 ? 1 : 0) << 1);
        ulong key = ((ulong)ix << 32) | ((ulong)iy << 20) | ((ulong)iz << 8) | (ulong)ib;
        if (occupied.Contains(key)) return;
        occupied.Add(key);

        // Containment checks: effective extent is max(explicit dim, bound)
        int fc = frees.Count;
        for (int i = fc - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            var e = frees[i];
            long ex2 = e.x + (e.w > 0 ? e.w : e.boundW);
            long ey2 = e.y + (e.h > 0 ? e.h : e.boundH);
            long ez2 = e.z + (e.d > 0 ? e.d : e.boundD);
            long nx2 = x + (w > 0 ? w : boundW);
            long ny2 = y + (h > 0 ? h : boundH);
            long nz2 = z + (d > 0 ? d : boundD);

            if (x <= e.x && y <= e.y && z <= e.z
                && nx2 >= ex2 && ny2 >= ey2 && nz2 >= ez2)
            {
                frees.RemoveAt(i);
                continue;
            }
            if (e.x <= x && e.y <= y && e.z <= z
                && ex2 >= nx2 && ey2 >= ny2 && ez2 >= nz2)
                return;
        }

        if (frees.Count < maxFree)
            frees.Add(new FreeSpace(x, y, z, w, h, d, boundW, boundH, boundD));
    }

    static bool NextPerm(int[] p)
    {
        int n = p.Length;
        int i = n - 2;
        while (i >= 0 && p[i] >= p[i + 1]) i--;
        if (i < 0) return false;
        int j = n - 1;
        while (p[j] <= p[i]) j--;
        int tmp = p[i]; p[i] = p[j]; p[j] = tmp;
        Array.Reverse(p, i + 1, n - i - 1);
        return true;
    }

    struct BoxEntry
    {
        public int idx;
        public long w, h, d;
        public BoxEntry(int idx, long w, long h, long d)
        { this.idx = idx; this.w = w; this.h = h; this.d = d; }
    }

    struct FreeSpace
    {
        public long x, y, z, w, h, d;
        public long boundW, boundH, boundD;
        public FreeSpace(long x, long y, long z, long w, long h, long d,
                          long boundW, long boundH, long boundD)
        {
            this.x = x; this.y = y; this.z = z;
            this.w = w; this.h = h; this.d = d;
            this.boundW = boundW; this.boundH = boundH; this.boundD = boundD;
        }
    }
}

Test 18: Minimum Cut Problem (C++) Score: 0.00 / 9
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a C++ program to solve the Minimum Edge Cut problem.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler information**
MSVC cl 19.50.35725 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to multi-gb.

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ program will be tested with graphs ranging from small toy examples to massive real-world networks.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Problem:**
Given an undirected connected graph, find the minimum number of edges to remove
such that the graph becomes disconnected (split into two non-empty components).

**Input format (stdin):**
- Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
- Lines 2 to M+1: u v (undirected edge between nodes u and v, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
- Line 1: K (number of edges in the minimum cut)
- Lines 2 to K+1: u v (each edge in the cut, in any order)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with this compiler, and run on this environment.
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle graphs even if they don't fit in memory.
4. Complete as fast as possible.
5. Output must be a valid cut that disconnects the graph
6. Minimize the number of cut edges
7. Must handle varying graph sizes efficiently

**Example:**
Input:
```
4 5
0 1
1 2
2 3
3 0
0 2
```

Output (one possible answer):
```
2
0 1
3 0
```

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35725 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different algorithms based on graph scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Find the smallest set of edges to remove from a graph so that it splits into two disconnected pieces. Think of it like finding the weakest point in a network — the fewest cables you’d need to cut to divide it in two.

Subpasses increase the graph size from small networks to very large ones. The AI must implement an efficient algorithm; naïve approaches are far too slow on the bigger inputs.

C++ Code 
// Minimum Edge Cut Solver
// Adaptive: Stoer-Wagner (optimal) or BFS approximation based on graph size
// MSVC cl /std:c++20 /O2

#include <cstdio>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <cstring>
#include <vector>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <algorithm>
#include <random>
#include <chrono>
#include <cassert>

using namespace std;

typedef long long ll;
typedef unsigned long long ull;
typedef unsigned int uint32_t;

// ============================================================
//  Fast parsing utility
// ============================================================

static inline ull parse_ull(const char*& p) {
    while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\r' || *p == '\n') ++p;
    ull v = 0;
    while ((unsigned)(*p - '0') < 10u) { v = v * 10 + (ull)(*p - '0'); ++p; }
    return v;
}

// ============================================================
//  TIER 2: Stoer-Wagner O(N*M) with O(N) adjacency per vertex
//  Returns the global minimum cut (optimal).
// ============================================================

static void solve_exact(
    int N, ll M,
    const char* edge_ptr,        // points to start of edge list in input buffer
    vector<pair<int,int>>& out_cut,
    ll& out_weight)
{
    // Build adjacency: adj[i] = {neighbor -> total edge weight}
    vector<unordered_map<int,int>> adj(N);
    {
        const char* p = edge_ptr;
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(p);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(p);
            adj[u][v]++;
            adj[v][u]++;
        }
    }

    ll  best_weight = LLONG_MAX;
    vector<int> best_side;          // original vertices on side A of best cut
    best_side.reserve(N);

    // super_group[i] = list of original vertices absorbed into super-vertex i
    vector<vector<int>> sg(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) sg[i].push_back(i);

    int n = N;                      // current number of super-vertices

    for (int phase = 0; phase < N - 1; ++phase) {
        // ---- Maximum-Adjacency (MA) ordering ----
        vector<ll> wgt(n, 0);
        vector<bool> in_a(n, false);
        int s = -1, t = -1;

        for (int step = 0; step < n; ++step) {
            // pick the vertex with the largest accumulated weight
            int bv = -1;  ll bw = LLONG_MIN;
            for (int j = 0; j < n; ++j) {
                if (!in_a[j] && wgt[j] > bw) { bw = wgt[j]; bv = j; }
            }
            s = t;  t = bv;
            in_a[bv] = true;
            for (auto& [nb, wt] : adj[bv]) {
                if (!in_a[nb]) wgt[nb] += wt;
            }
        }

        // The s-t cut (A \ {t}, {t}) is a candidate minimum s-t cut.
        ll cw = wgt[t];
        if (cw < best_weight) {
            best_weight = cw;
            best_side.clear();
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (in_a[i] && i != t) {
                    for (int ov : sg[i]) best_side.push_back(ov);
                }
            }
        }
        if (s < 0) break;           // only one vertex left

        // ---- Merge t into s (contract edge s-t) ----
        for (auto& [nb, wt] : adj[t]) {
            if (nb != s) {
                adj[s][nb] += wt;
                adj[nb].erase(t);
                adj[nb][s] = adj[s][nb];
            }
        }
        adj[s].erase(t);
        adj[t].clear();

        // Swap t with the last super-vertex to keep [0..n) dense
        int last = n - 1;
        if (t != last) {
            swap(adj[t], adj[last]);
            swap(sg[t],   sg[last]);
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i == t || i == last) continue;
                auto it = adj[i].find(last);
                if (it != adj[i].end()) {
                    adj[i][t] = it->second;
                    adj[i].erase(it);
                }
            }
        }
        --n;
    }

    // ---- Collect the actual cut edges ----
    unordered_set<int> sa_set(best_side.begin(), best_side.end());
    {
        const char* p = edge_ptr;
        out_cut.clear();
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(p);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(p);
            if (sa_set.count(u) != sa_set.count(v))
                out_cut.push_back({u, v});
        }
    }
    out_weight = (ll)out_cut.size();
}

// ============================================================
//  TIER 3: BFS Approximate Minimum Cut
//  Runs many BFS trials and tries the cut at every BFS level.
//  With enough trials, very likely to find the exact min cut
//  on realistic graphs (especially those with sparse cuts).
// ============================================================

static mt19937_64 g_rng;

static void seed_rng() {
    ull seed = (ull)chrono::steady_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count();
    g_rng.seed(seed);
}

static void solve_approx(
    int N, ll M,
    const char* edge_ptr,
    vector<pair<int,int>>& out_cut,
    ll& out_weight)
{
    // Build simple adjacency list (no weights)
    struct Edge { int to, idx; };
    vector<vector<Edge>> adj(N);
    {
        const char* p = edge_ptr;
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(p);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(p);
            adj[u].push_back({v, (int)i});
            adj[v].push_back({u, (int)i});
        }
    }

    // Number of random BFS trials
    int trials = (int)min(200LL, max(80LL, N / 5000 + 80));
    ll best_cv = LLONG_MAX;
    vector<bool> best_side(N, false);

    for (int tr = 0; tr < trials; ++tr) {
        int start = (int)(g_rng() % (uint32_t)N);

        vector<int> depth(N, -1);
        vector<int> ord;  ord.reserve(N);
        ord.push_back(start);
        depth[start] = 0;

        for (int qi = 0; qi < (int)ord.size(); ++qi) {
            int v = ord[qi];
            int d = depth[v];
            for (auto& e : adj[v]) {
                if (depth[e.to] < 0) {
                    depth[e.to] = d + 1;
                    ord.push_back(e.to);
                }
            }
        }
        if ((int)ord.size() < N) continue;   // graph already disconnected

        int max_d = 0;
        for (int v : ord) if (depth[v] > max_d) max_d = depth[v];

        // Try every BFS depth as the cut boundary
        // Count crossing edges only for edges crossing the depth-d boundary
        for (int d = 1; d <= max_d; ++d) {
            ll cv = 0;
            for (int qi = 0; qi < (int)ord.size(); ++qi) {
                int v = ord[qi];
                if (depth[v] == d - 1) {
                    for (auto& e : adj[v]) {
                        if (depth[e.to] >= d) ++cv;
                    }
                }
            }
            if (cv < best_cv) {
                best_cv = cv;
                for (int v = 0; v < N; ++v)
                    best_side[v] = (depth[v] < d);
            }
        }
    }

    // ---- Collect cut edges ----
    {
        const char* p = edge_ptr;
        out_cut.clear();
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(p);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(p);
            if (best_side[u] != best_side[v])
                out_cut.push_back({u, v});
        }
    }
    out_weight = (ll)out_cut.size();
}

// ============================================================
//  Validation: verify the cut is valid (disconnects the graph)
// ============================================================

static bool validate_cut(int N, ll M,
                         const vector<pair<int,int>>& edges,
                         const vector<pair<int,int>>& cut)
{
    // Build set of cut edges
    unordered_set<ll> cut_set;
    cut_set.reserve(cut.size() * 2);
    for (auto& [u, v] : cut) {
        if (u <= v) cut_set.insert((ll)u * N + v);
        else        cut_set.insert((ll)v * N + u);
    }

    // Build adjacency without cut edges
    vector<vector<int>> adj(N);
    adj.reserve(N);
    for (auto& [u, v] : edges) {
        ll key = (u <= v) ? ((ll)u * N + v) : ((ll)v * N + u);
        if (cut_set.count(key)) continue;
        adj[u].push_back(v);
        adj[v].push_back(u);
    }

    // BFS from vertex 0
    vector<bool> vis(N, false);
    vector<int> q;  q.reserve(N);
    q.push_back(0);
    vis[0] = true;
    int qi = 0;
    while (qi < (int)q.size()) {
        int v = q[qi++];
        for (int nb : adj[v]) {
            if (!vis[nb]) { vis[nb] = true; q.push_back(nb); }
        }
    }

    // Check: some vertex must be unreachable
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        if (!vis[i]) return true;     // disconnected -> valid cut
    }
    return false;                     // still connected -> invalid
}

// ============================================================
//  MAIN
// ============================================================

int main() {
    seed_rng();

    // ---- Read all input into a growable buffer ----
    size_t buf_cap = (size_t)1 << 22;    // 4 MB initial
    char* buf = (char*)malloc(buf_cap);
    if (!buf) { fprintf(stderr, "malloc failed\n"); return 1; }

    size_t total = 0;
    size_t nr;
    while ((nr = fread(buf + total, 1, buf_cap - total - 1, stdin)) > 0) {
        total += nr;
        if (total >= buf_cap - 1) {
            buf_cap = buf_cap * 2;
            buf = (char*)realloc(buf, buf_cap);
            if (!buf) { fprintf(stderr, "realloc failed\n"); return 1; }
        }
    }
    buf[total] = '\0';

    const char* p = buf;
    ull Nu = parse_ull(p);
    ull Mu = parse_ull(p);
    int N = (int)Nu;
    ll  M = (ll)Mu;

    const char* edge_ptr = p;   // save: first edge starts here

    // ---- Decide algorithm tier ----
    // Stoer-Wagner O(N*M): exact but expensive for huge graphs.
    // Use exact when: N <= 2500, or estimated work 2*M*N <= 6*10^11.
    // Otherwise fall back to BFS approximation.
    bool use_exact = (N <= 2500) ||
                     (2LL * M * N <= 600000000000LL);

    vector<pair<int,int>> cut_edges;
    ll cut_value = 0;

    if (use_exact) {
        solve_exact(N, M, edge_ptr, cut_edges, cut_value);
    } else {
        solve_approx(N, M, edge_ptr, cut_edges, cut_value);
    }

    // ---- Output ----
    {
        char obuf[1 << 18];
        setvbuf(stdout, obuf, _IOFBF, sizeof(obuf));
        fprintf(stdout, "%lld\n", (ll)cut_edges.size());
        for (auto& [u, v] : cut_edges)
            fprintf(stdout, "%d %d\n", u, v);
        fflush(stdout);
    }

    // ---- Validation (safety check) ----
    {
        const char* vp = edge_ptr;
        vector<pair<int,int>> all_edges;
        all_edges.reserve((size_t)M);
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(vp);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(vp);
            all_edges.push_back({u, v});
        }
        if (!validate_cut(N, M, all_edges, cut_edges)) {
            fprintf(stderr, "ERROR: cut validation failed\n");
            // Should never happen
            return 1;
        }
    }

    free(buf);
    return 0;
}

C++ Code 
// Minimum Edge Cut Solver
// Adaptive: Stoer-Wagner (optimal) or BFS approximation based on graph size
// MSVC cl /std:c++20 /O2

#include <cstdio>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <cstring>
#include <vector>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <algorithm>
#include <random>
#include <chrono>
#include <cassert>

using namespace std;

typedef long long ll;
typedef unsigned long long ull;
typedef unsigned int uint32_t;

// ============================================================
//  Fast parsing utility
// ============================================================

static inline ull parse_ull(const char*& p) {
    while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\r' || *p == '\n') ++p;
    ull v = 0;
    while ((unsigned)(*p - '0') < 10u) { v = v * 10 + (ull)(*p - '0'); ++p; }
    return v;
}

// ============================================================
//  TIER 2: Stoer-Wagner O(N*M) with O(N) adjacency per vertex
//  Returns the global minimum cut (optimal).
// ============================================================

static void solve_exact(
    int N, ll M,
    const char* edge_ptr,        // points to start of edge list in input buffer
    vector<pair<int,int>>& out_cut,
    ll& out_weight)
{
    // Build adjacency: adj[i] = {neighbor -> total edge weight}
    vector<unordered_map<int,int>> adj(N);
    {
        const char* p = edge_ptr;
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(p);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(p);
            adj[u][v]++;
            adj[v][u]++;
        }
    }

    ll  best_weight = LLONG_MAX;
    vector<int> best_side;          // original vertices on side A of best cut
    best_side.reserve(N);

    // super_group[i] = list of original vertices absorbed into super-vertex i
    vector<vector<int>> sg(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) sg[i].push_back(i);

    int n = N;                      // current number of super-vertices

    for (int phase = 0; phase < N - 1; ++phase) {
        // ---- Maximum-Adjacency (MA) ordering ----
        vector<ll> wgt(n, 0);
        vector<bool> in_a(n, false);
        int s = -1, t = -1;

        for (int step = 0; step < n; ++step) {
            // pick the vertex with the largest accumulated weight
            int bv = -1;  ll bw = LLONG_MIN;
            for (int j = 0; j < n; ++j) {
                if (!in_a[j] && wgt[j] > bw) { bw = wgt[j]; bv = j; }
            }
            s = t;  t = bv;
            in_a[bv] = true;
            for (auto& [nb, wt] : adj[bv]) {
                if (!in_a[nb]) wgt[nb] += wt;
            }
        }

        // The s-t cut (A \ {t}, {t}) is a candidate minimum s-t cut.
        ll cw = wgt[t];
        if (cw < best_weight) {
            best_weight = cw;
            best_side.clear();
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (in_a[i] && i != t) {
                    for (int ov : sg[i]) best_side.push_back(ov);
                }
            }
        }
        if (s < 0) break;           // only one vertex left

        // ---- Merge t into s (contract edge s-t) ----
        for (auto& [nb, wt] : adj[t]) {
            if (nb != s) {
                adj[s][nb] += wt;
                adj[nb].erase(t);
                adj[nb][s] = adj[s][nb];
            }
        }
        adj[s].erase(t);
        adj[t].clear();

        // Swap t with the last super-vertex to keep [0..n) dense
        int last = n - 1;
        if (t != last) {
            swap(adj[t], adj[last]);
            swap(sg[t],   sg[last]);
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (i == t || i == last) continue;
                auto it = adj[i].find(last);
                if (it != adj[i].end()) {
                    adj[i][t] = it->second;
                    adj[i].erase(it);
                }
            }
        }
        --n;
    }

    // ---- Collect the actual cut edges ----
    unordered_set<int> sa_set(best_side.begin(), best_side.end());
    {
        const char* p = edge_ptr;
        out_cut.clear();
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(p);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(p);
            if (sa_set.count(u) != sa_set.count(v))
                out_cut.push_back({u, v});
        }
    }
    out_weight = (ll)out_cut.size();
}

// ============================================================
//  TIER 3: BFS Approximate Minimum Cut
//  Runs many BFS trials and tries the cut at every BFS level.
//  With enough trials, very likely to find the exact min cut
//  on realistic graphs (especially those with sparse cuts).
// ============================================================

static mt19937_64 g_rng;

static void seed_rng() {
    ull seed = (ull)chrono::steady_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count();
    g_rng.seed(seed);
}

static void solve_approx(
    int N, ll M,
    const char* edge_ptr,
    vector<pair<int,int>>& out_cut,
    ll& out_weight)
{
    // Build simple adjacency list (no weights)
    struct Edge { int to, idx; };
    vector<vector<Edge>> adj(N);
    {
        const char* p = edge_ptr;
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(p);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(p);
            adj[u].push_back({v, (int)i});
            adj[v].push_back({u, (int)i});
        }
    }

    // Number of random BFS trials
    int trials = (int)min(200LL, max(80LL, N / 5000 + 80));
    ll best_cv = LLONG_MAX;
    vector<bool> best_side(N, false);

    for (int tr = 0; tr < trials; ++tr) {
        int start = (int)(g_rng() % (uint32_t)N);

        vector<int> depth(N, -1);
        vector<int> ord;  ord.reserve(N);
        ord.push_back(start);
        depth[start] = 0;

        for (int qi = 0; qi < (int)ord.size(); ++qi) {
            int v = ord[qi];
            int d = depth[v];
            for (auto& e : adj[v]) {
                if (depth[e.to] < 0) {
                    depth[e.to] = d + 1;
                    ord.push_back(e.to);
                }
            }
        }
        if ((int)ord.size() < N) continue;   // graph already disconnected

        int max_d = 0;
        for (int v : ord) if (depth[v] > max_d) max_d = depth[v];

        // Try every BFS depth as the cut boundary
        // Count crossing edges only for edges crossing the depth-d boundary
        for (int d = 1; d <= max_d; ++d) {
            ll cv = 0;
            for (int qi = 0; qi < (int)ord.size(); ++qi) {
                int v = ord[qi];
                if (depth[v] == d - 1) {
                    for (auto& e : adj[v]) {
                        if (depth[e.to] >= d) ++cv;
                    }
                }
            }
            if (cv < best_cv) {
                best_cv = cv;
                for (int v = 0; v < N; ++v)
                    best_side[v] = (depth[v] < d);
            }
        }
    }

    // ---- Collect cut edges ----
    {
        const char* p = edge_ptr;
        out_cut.clear();
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(p);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(p);
            if (best_side[u] != best_side[v])
                out_cut.push_back({u, v});
        }
    }
    out_weight = (ll)out_cut.size();
}

// ============================================================
//  Validation: verify the cut is valid (disconnects the graph)
// ============================================================

static bool validate_cut(int N, ll M,
                         const vector<pair<int,int>>& edges,
                         const vector<pair<int,int>>& cut)
{
    // Build set of cut edges
    unordered_set<ll> cut_set;
    cut_set.reserve(cut.size() * 2);
    for (auto& [u, v] : cut) {
        if (u <= v) cut_set.insert((ll)u * N + v);
        else        cut_set.insert((ll)v * N + u);
    }

    // Build adjacency without cut edges
    vector<vector<int>> adj(N);
    adj.reserve(N);
    for (auto& [u, v] : edges) {
        ll key = (u <= v) ? ((ll)u * N + v) : ((ll)v * N + u);
        if (cut_set.count(key)) continue;
        adj[u].push_back(v);
        adj[v].push_back(u);
    }

    // BFS from vertex 0
    vector<bool> vis(N, false);
    vector<int> q;  q.reserve(N);
    q.push_back(0);
    vis[0] = true;
    int qi = 0;
    while (qi < (int)q.size()) {
        int v = q[qi++];
        for (int nb : adj[v]) {
            if (!vis[nb]) { vis[nb] = true; q.push_back(nb); }
        }
    }

    // Check: some vertex must be unreachable
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        if (!vis[i]) return true;     // disconnected -> valid cut
    }
    return false;                     // still connected -> invalid
}

// ============================================================
//  MAIN
// ============================================================

int main() {
    seed_rng();

    // ---- Read all input into a growable buffer ----
    size_t buf_cap = (size_t)1 << 22;    // 4 MB initial
    char* buf = (char*)malloc(buf_cap);
    if (!buf) { fprintf(stderr, "malloc failed\n"); return 1; }

    size_t total = 0;
    size_t nr;
    while ((nr = fread(buf + total, 1, buf_cap - total - 1, stdin)) > 0) {
        total += nr;
        if (total >= buf_cap - 1) {
            buf_cap = buf_cap * 2;
            buf = (char*)realloc(buf, buf_cap);
            if (!buf) { fprintf(stderr, "realloc failed\n"); return 1; }
        }
    }
    buf[total] = '\0';

    const char* p = buf;
    ull Nu = parse_ull(p);
    ull Mu = parse_ull(p);
    int N = (int)Nu;
    ll  M = (ll)Mu;

    const char* edge_ptr = p;   // save: first edge starts here

    // ---- Decide algorithm tier ----
    // Stoer-Wagner O(N*M): exact but expensive for huge graphs.
    // Use exact when: N <= 2500, or estimated work 2*M*N <= 6*10^11.
    // Otherwise fall back to BFS approximation.
    bool use_exact = (N <= 2500) ||
                     (2LL * M * N <= 600000000000LL);

    vector<pair<int,int>> cut_edges;
    ll cut_value = 0;

    if (use_exact) {
        solve_exact(N, M, edge_ptr, cut_edges, cut_value);
    } else {
        solve_approx(N, M, edge_ptr, cut_edges, cut_value);
    }

    // ---- Output ----
    {
        char obuf[1 << 18];
        setvbuf(stdout, obuf, _IOFBF, sizeof(obuf));
        fprintf(stdout, "%lld\n", (ll)cut_edges.size());
        for (auto& [u, v] : cut_edges)
            fprintf(stdout, "%d %d\n", u, v);
        fflush(stdout);
    }

    // ---- Validation (safety check) ----
    {
        const char* vp = edge_ptr;
        vector<pair<int,int>> all_edges;
        all_edges.reserve((size_t)M);
        for (ll i = 0; i < M; ++i) {
            int u = (int)parse_ull(vp);
            int v = (int)parse_ull(vp);
            all_edges.push_back({u, v});
        }
        if (!validate_cut(N, M, all_edges, cut_edges)) {
            fprintf(stderr, "ERROR: cut validation failed\n");
            // Should never happen
            return 1;
        }
    }

    free(buf);
    return 0;
}

Test 19: 3D Voxel Mining (C#) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a C# program to solve a mining problem in a simplified 3D voxel space.

**Constraints:**
1. Can only dig dirt that is exposed from above or a side.
2. Dug dirt must be dumped somewhere - at a location not blocking future digging
3. Maximum uphill slope - 1 Z voxel per X/Y voxel.
4. Dumped dirt becomes a new obstacle (plan carefully!)
5. Goal is to get valuable ore to a processing plant located at x=0, y=0
6. Ground is stable and solid, and you can dig under any voxel without fear of it collapsing
so long as it's connected to the earth. If you disconnect a voxel(s), it will collapse.

The voxel grid size has 10x more precision in Z than X/Y. So 10x10x100 is a cube.

**Input header format (stdin - space delimited. Ends with newline):**
```
XVoxelCount YVoxelCount ZVoxelCount XBlockSize YBlockSize ZBlockSize
```
Counts are human readable integers. BlockSize are human readable decimal numbers.

After the newline, voxels then follow in bytes, like this python psuedocode:
```
for x in range(XVoxelCount):
for y in range(YVoxelCount):
for z in range(ZVoxelCount):
d = readByte(stdin)
massInTonnes = d % 16
valuePerTonne = d // 16
addVoxel(d)
```

Each peice of dirt needs to be moved to its new location - you need to trace the
path in X and Y (4-connected / manhatten steps) and ensure that the path doesn't climb uphill
or downhill more than 1 Z voxel for every X/Y voxel.

**Output format (stdout / newline seperated):**
```
M (number of operations)
N digX digY digZ stepX1 stepY1 stepX2 stepY2 (N steps) destX destY (for each operation)
```

If dumping valuable ore at the processing plant, the voxel ceases to exist. If a voxel is
dumped elsewhere, it is dumped on top of the ground, raising the ground by 1 voxel. It can
be dug again and moved later if required.

Every unit of mass requires $1 of fuel to move one voxel in X or Y when flat, or $5 of fuel
to go up a 10% slope. Downhill is free.

Getting valuable ore to your processing plant pays $1000 per unit of mass per unit of value.
The most valuable ore (#15) pays $15,000 per ton, so the most a single dump truck load can gross
is $225,000.

(Dumping junk ore at the processing plant causes damage to the plant, costing $1000 per ton.)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. World size could be anything from 10x10x10 to 5000x5000x5000, your code should handle both extremes.
4. Your code should complete in under 5 minutes
5. Maximize profit.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.3.0-2.26078.5 (16f9bd284cd49604ac82998bfe778a8eb16d4347)) | max LangVersion 14


Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Plan an open-pit mine in a 3D block model. Each block has a value (ore worth money, waste costs money to move) and you can only dig a block once the blocks above it have been removed. The goal is to maximise profit by choosing which blocks to extract and where to dump the waste.

The AI must balance revenue against haulage costs, respect slope constraints for road access, and avoid dumping waste where it blocks future digging. Subpasses increase the pit size and complexity.

 0.0000
[10×10 tiny pit (10 KB)] Compilation failed:
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmp333a9ce_.cs(169,34): warning CS8632: The annotation for nullable reference types should only be used in code within a '#nullable' annotations context.
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmp333a9ce_.cs(28,41): error CS1503: Argument 1: cannot convert from 'byte[]' to 'char[]'
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmp333a9ce_.cs(123,26): error CS1061: '(int x, int y, int z, int mass, int val, double cx, double cy, double cz)' does not contain a definition for 'm' and no accessible extension method 'm' accepting a first argument of type '(int x, int y, int z, int mass, int val, double cx, double cy, double cz)' could be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

3D Voxel Mining - 10×10 tiny pit (10 KB)

Strategy: Looking at this problem, I need to: 1. Parse the voxel grid and identify ore worth mining 2. Plan efficient pathfinding respecting the slope constraint 3. Manage dirt disposal without blocking future paths 4. Maximize profit by selecting optimal ore and minimizing fuel costs Let me think through the key algorithms: voxel representation, slope-aware pathfinding (BFS with Z-bound tracking), adapti...

View C# Code (9964 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var header = Console.ReadLine()!.Split();
        int XC = int.Parse(header[0]);
        int YC = int.Parse(header[1]);
        int ZC = int.Parse(header[2]);
        double XS = double.Parse(header[3]);
        double YS = double.Parse(header[4]);
        double ZS = double.Parse(header[5]);

        bool big = (long)XC * YC * ZC > 500_000L;

        // Read voxel data as raw bytes
        byte[] raw;
        if (big)
        {
            raw = new byte[XC * YC * ZC];
            int total = raw.Length, off = 0;
            while (off < total)
            {
                int n = Console.In.Read(raw, off, total - off);
                if (n <= 0) break;
                off += n;
            }
        }
        else
        {
            raw = new byte[Console.In.Read()];
        }

        // ---------------------------------------------------------------
        // 1. Extract surface voxels, identify valuable ore, compute costs
        // ---------------------------------------------------------------
        var surf = new List<(int x, int y, int z, int mass, int val, double cx, double cy, double cz)>();

        for (int x = 0; x < XC; x++)
        {
            for (int y = 0; y < YC; y++)
            {
                for (int z = 0; z < ZC; z++)
                {
                    int idx = (x * YC + y) * ZC + z;
                    if (raw[idx] == 0) continue;

                    bool exposed = false;
                    if (z == ZC - 1) exposed = true;
                    else if (raw[idx + 1] == 0) exposed = true;
                    if (!exposed && x > 0)
                    {
                        int n = ((x - 1) * YC + y) * ZC + z;
                        if (n >= 0 && raw[n] == 0) exposed = true;
                    }
                    if (!exposed && x < XC - 1)
                    {
                        int n = ((x + 1) * YC + y) * ZC + z;
                        if (n < raw.Length && raw[n] == 0) exposed = true;
                    }
                    if (!exposed && y > 0)
                    {
                        int n = (x * YC + (y - 1)) * ZC + z;
                        if (n >= 0 && raw[n] == 0) exposed = true;
                    }
                    if (!exposed && y < YC - 1)
                    {
                        int n = (x * YC + (y + 1)) * ZC + z;
                        if (n < raw.Length && raw[n] == 0) exposed = true;
                    }

                    if (exposed)
                    {
                        int m = raw[idx] & 0xF;
                        int v = raw[idx] >> 4;
                        if (m > 0)
                        {
                            surf.Add((x, y, z, m, v,
                                (x + 0.5) * XS, (y + 0.5) * YS,
                                (z + 0.5) * ZS));
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Distance to plant (used for fuel cost approximation)
        surf.Sort((a, b) =>
        {
            double da = a.cx * a.cx + a.cy * a.cy;
            double db = b.cx * b.cx + b.cy * b.cy;
            return da.CompareTo(db);
        });

        int W = 0, H = 0;
        foreach (var s in surf) { if (s.x + 1 > W) W = s.x + 1; if (s.y + 1 > H) H = s.y + 1; }
        if (W == 0) W = 1;
        if (H == 0) H = 1;

        // Estimate fuel per manhattan step per tonne
        double est = surf.Count > 0 ? surf.Count > 3
            ? Math.Sqrt(surf[surf.Count / 2].cx * surf[surf.Count / 2].cx +
                        surf[surf.Count / 2].cy * surf[surf.Count / 2].cy) * ZS : ZS * 5
            : ZS * 3;
        double baseF = 2.0, slopeF = 2.0;
        if (est > 1) { baseF = Math.Max(0.01, ZS / est); slopeF = baseF * 4; }

        // ---------------------------------------------------------------
        // 2. Greedy selection of profitable ore
        // ---------------------------------------------------------------
        var ops = new List<(int x, int y, int z, int m, int v, List<(int dx, int dy)> p)>();
        var dels = new List<(int dx, int dy)>();
        var rev = new List<(int x, int y)>();

        foreach (var s in surf)
        {
            if (s.val < 2 || ops.Count >= 9000) continue;
            int worth = s.val * 1000;
            int cost = s.m * (int)Math.Ceiling(baseF + slopeF * 1.8);
            if (worth <= cost) continue;

            int npx = s.x / 2, npy = s.y / 2;
            if (npx < 0 || npy < 0 || npx >= W || npy >= H) continue;
            if (dels.Contains((npx, npy))) continue;

            bool ok = true;
            foreach (var d in dels)
                if (Math.Abs(d.dx - npx) <= 1 && Math.Abs(d.dy - npy) <= 1) { ok = false; break; }
            if (!ok) continue;

            var path = FindPath(s.x, s.y, W, H, dels);
            if (path == null) continue;

            ops.Add((s.x, s.y, s.z, s.m, s.v, path));
            dels.Add((npx, npy));
            rev.Add((s.x, s.y));

            if (dels.Count >= 12000) break;
        }

        // ---------------------------------------------------------------
        // 3. Build output
        // ---------------------------------------------------------------
        var sb = new StringBuilder();
        sb.Append(ops.Count);

        for (int i = 0; i < ops.Count; i++)
        {
            var o = ops[i];
            var p = o.p;
            sb.AppendLine();
            sb.Append(p.Count + 2);
            sb.Append(' ').Append(o.x).Append(' ').Append(o.y).Append(' ').Append(o.z);
            foreach (var st in p)
                sb.Append(' ').Append(st.dx).Append(' ').Append(st.dy);
            sb.Append(" 0 0");
        }

        Console.Write(sb.ToString());
    }

    // ---------------------------------------------------------------
    // BFS pathfinding with slope-safe routing (Z bound tracking)
    // ---------------------------------------------------------------
    static List<(int dx, int dy)>? FindPath(int sx, int sy, int W, int H,
        List<(int dx, int dy)> blocked)
    {
        int[] dirs = { -1, 0, 1, 0, 0, -1, 0, 1 };
        int cap = 25000;

        // Primary BFS: prefer routes toward plant (lower Manhattan distance)
        var q = new Queue<(int x, int y, int lo, int hi)>();
        q.Enqueue((sx, sy, 0, cap));

        var vis = new HashSet<long>();
        vis.Add(Pack(sx, sy, 0, cap));

        var par = new Dictionary<long, (int px, int py, int plo, int phi, int dir)>();
        var bl = new HashSet<(int, int)>(blocked);
        int gx = 0, gy = 0;

        while (q.Count > 0)
        {
            var c = q.Dequeue();
            if (c.x == gx && c.y == gy)
            {
                var r = new List<(int dx, int dy)>();
                int cx = gx, cy = gy, clo = 0, chi = cap;
                long ck = Pack(cx, cy, clo, chi);
                while (ck != -1L && par.TryGetValue(ck, out var pp))
                {
                    r.Add((pp.px, pp.py));
                    cx = pp.px; cy = pp.py;
                    clo = pp.plo; chi = pp.phi;
                    ck = Pack(cx, cy, clo, chi);
                }
                r.Reverse();
                return r;
            }

            for (int d = 0; d < 4; d++)
            {
                int nx = c.x + dirs[d * 2];
                int ny = c.y + dirs[d * 2 + 1];
                if (nx < -3 || nx > W + 3 || ny < -3 || ny > H + 3) continue;
                if (bl.Contains((nx, ny))) continue;

                int nlo = Math.Max(0, c.lo - 1);
                int nhi = Math.Min(cap, c.hi + 1);
                if (nhi <= nlo) continue;

                long nk = Pack(nx, ny, nlo, nhi);
                if (vis.Add(nk))
                {
                    q.Enqueue((nx, ny, nlo, nhi));
                    par[nk] = (c.x, c.y, c.lo, c.hi, d);
                }
            }
        }

        // Fallback BFS: any Manhattan-decreasing path
        vis.Clear();
        par.Clear();
        q.Clear();
        q.Enqueue((sx, sy, 0, cap));
        vis.Add(Pack(sx, sy, 0, cap));

        while (q.Count > 0)
        {
            var c = q.Dequeue();
            if (c.x == gx && c.y == gy)
            {
                var r = new List<(int dx, int dy)>();
                int cx = gx, cy = gy, clo = 0, chi = cap;
                long ck = Pack(cx, cy, clo, chi);
                while (ck != -1L && par.TryGetValue(ck, out var pp))
                {
                    r.Add((pp.px, pp.py));
                    cx = pp.px; cy = pp.py;
                    clo = pp.plo; chi = pp.phi;
                    ck = Pack(cx, cy, clo, chi);
                }
                r.Reverse();
                return r;
            }

            for (int d = 0; d < 4; d++)
            {
                int nx = c.x + dirs[d * 2];
                int ny = c.y + dirs[d * 2 + 1];
                if (nx < -3 || nx > W + 3 || ny < -3 || ny > H + 3) continue;
                if (bl.Contains((nx, ny))) continue;

                int cd = Math.Abs(c.x) + Math.Abs(c.y);
                int nd = Math.Abs(nx) + Math.Abs(ny);
                if (nd > cd) continue;

                int nlo = Math.Max(0, c.lo - 1);
                int nhi = Math.Min(cap, c.hi + 1);
                if (nhi <= nlo) continue;

                long nk = Pack(nx, ny, nlo, nhi);
                if (vis.Add(nk))
                {
                    q.Enqueue((nx, ny, nlo, nhi));
                    par[nk] = (c.x, c.y, c.lo, c.hi, d);
                }
            }
        }

        return null;
    }

    static long Pack(int x, int y, int lo, int hi)
    {
        if (x == -1 && y == -1) return -1L;
        return ((long)x & 0x7FFFFFFFL) |
               (((long)y & 0x7FFFFFFFL) << 31) |
               (((long)(hi & 0xFFFF) & 0xFFFFL) << 62);
    }
}

Test 20: Drillhole Data Validation (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a Rust program to validate drillhole assay data.


**The Challenge:**
Your Rust program will be tested with drillhole datasets ranging from small exploration projects to
massive mining surveys. The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales from 10 holes to
200,000 holes.

**Problem:**
Given drillhole data from a mining exploration project, identify samples that likely contain typos or
measurement errors. These outliers don't correlate well with spatially neighboring data points.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler information**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

**Drillhole Data:**
- Each hole has a 3D path: start point (x,y,z) + direction vector + length
- Samples are taken at intervals along each hole
- Each sample has measurements for multiple properties

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_holes num_properties
property_name_1 property_name_2 ...
hole_id start_x start_y start_z dir_x dir_y dir_z length num_samples
depth prop1 prop2 ...
depth prop1 prop2 ...
etc.
hole_id start_x start_y start_z dir_x dir_y dir_z length num_samples
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
M (number of suspect entries found)
hole_id sample_idx property_name confidence (for each suspect, sorted by confidence desc)
```

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc and only the standard rust library.
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle up to thousands of holes with thousands of samples each
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Report at least the most obvious outliers
6. Confidence score should reflect how anomalous the value is
7. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on dataset scale.

The program should be robust enough to handle edge cases like missing data, duplicate entries, and varying data distributions.
 Prompt Changes:

The AI must compare each measurement against its spatial neighbours: a reading that disagrees with nearby drillholes is suspect. Subpasses increase the number of drillholes and properties, requiring efficient spatial reasoning at scale.

Test 21: N-Dimensional Snake Game (C++) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play an N-Dimensional Snake Game.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY dimensional complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2D games (eg 8x8), few obstacles, basic pathfinding
- **Medium**: 3D-4D games (eg 6x6x6 to 8x8x8x8), moderate obstacles, intermediate planning
- **Large**: 6D-8D games (eg 4x4x4x4x4x4 to 6x6x6x6x6x6x6x6), many obstacles, complex pathfinding
- **Extreme**: 10D-20D games (eg 3x3x3... to 4x4x4...)

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ snake player will be tested with games ranging from simple 2D boards to massive 20D hypercubes.
The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL dimensionalities.

**Problem:**
Like classic Nokia snake, but generalized to arbitrary dimensions. The snake occupies a series of
connected integer grid points in N-D space, moves one step per turn along one axis (+1 or -1),
grows when eating food, and dies if it hits an obstacle or itself.

**Input format (stdin, whitespace / newline seperated):**
```
D max_turns (D = number of dimensions, max_turns = turn limit)
bounds (D integers: size of space in each dimension)
snake_length start_pos (length, then D integers for head position)
F (number of food items)
F lines: food positions (D integers each)
O (number of obstacles)
O lines: obstacle positions (D integers each)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Each turn, output: axis direction
- axis: 0 to D-1 (which dimension to move along)
- direction: -1 or +1 (which way to move)

**Game ends when:**
- Snake eats all food (win)
- Snake hits obstacle or itself (lose)
- Max turns reached

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35725 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:

Play the classic Snake game, but in more than two dimensions. The snake moves through an N-dimensional grid, eating food to grow while avoiding its own body and the walls. In higher dimensions the grid is enormous but self-collision becomes far trickier to reason about.

Subpasses increase the number of dimensions, making the state space explode. The AI must write a strategy that plans a safe path to food without boxing itself in.

 0.0000
[2D 20x20, 5 food, 10 obstacles] Food: 0/5, Turns: 0, End: Compilation error: Compilation failed:
tmp8gq3z_5y.cpp
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmp8gq3z_5y.cpp(1): fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: 'bits/stdc++.h': No such file or directory
, Time: 0.00s

N-Dimensional Snake - 2D 20x20, 5 food, 10 obstacles

Approach: Here's my complete N-Dimensional Snake solver:

View C++ Code (5935 chars)
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
using VI = vector<int>;
using LL = long long;

int D;                    // Number of dimensions
VI BD;                    // Board size per dimension
vector<LL> DIM;           // DIM[d] = 1LL << (3*d)

// Position hashing: 3 bits per dimension interleaved
// Supports coordinates 0-7 per dim (21 dimensions in 63 bits)
static LL hashPos(const VI& p) {
    LL h = 0;
    for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d)
        h |= (LL)p[d] << (3 * d);
    return h;
}

static bool inBounds(LL h) {
    for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d)
        if ((int)((h >> (3 * d)) & 7) >= BD[d]) return false;
    return true;
}

// BFS on N-D bitmask grid.
// Returns {visited bitmask, target_reached, farthest_cell}
static tuple<LL, bool, LL> bfs(LL blocked, LL start, LL target) {
    LL vis = blocked | (1LL << start);
    queue<LL> q;
    q.push(start);
    bool reached = (target >= 0 && start == target);
    LL farthest = start;
    while (!q.empty()) {
        LL c = q.front(); q.pop();
        farthest = c;
        if (target >= 0 && c == target) { reached = true; break; }
        for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d) {
            LL n = c ^ DIM[d];
            if ((vis >> n) & 1) continue;
            if (!inBounds(n)) continue;
            vis |= 1LL << n;
            q.push(n);
        }
    }
    return {vis, reached, farthest};
}

static void processTurn(LL vol) {
    string line;
    if (!getline(cin, line)) return;

    istringstream hs(line);
    int snake_len;
    hs >> snake_len;
    VI head_p(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) hs >> head_p[i];
    LL head_h = hashPos(head_p);

    vector<LL> body;
    body.push_back(head_h);
    for (int b = 1; b < snake_len; ++b) {
        getline(cin, line);
        istringstream ss(line);
        VI p(D);
        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) ss >> p[i];
        body.push_back(hashPos(p));
    }

    getline(cin, line);
    int nf = 0;
    { istringstream ss(line); ss >> nf; }
    vector<LL> food(nf);
    for (int i = 0; i < nf; ++i) {
        getline(cin, line);
        istringstream ss(line);
        VI p(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) ss >> p[j];
        food[i] = hashPos(p);
    }

    getline(cin, line);
    int no = 0;
    { istringstream ss(line); ss >> no; }
    LL obs_mask = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < no; ++i) {
        getline(cin, line);
        istringstream ss(line);
        VI p(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) ss >> p[j];
        obs_mask |= 1LL << hashPos(p);
    }

    LL body_mask = obs_mask;
    for (LL b : body) body_mask |= 1LL << b;

    LL body_no_tail = obs_mask;
    for (int i = 0; i + 1 < (int)body.size(); ++i)
        body_no_tail |= 1LL << body[i];

    LL tail_h = body.back();

    struct Move { int axis, dir; LL nh; };
    vector<Move> moves;
    for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d) {
        for (int dd : {-1, 1}) {
            int nc = head_p[d] + dd;
            if (nc < 0 || nc >= BD[d]) continue;
            LL nh = head_h ^ DIM[d];
            if ((body_mask >> nh) & 1) continue;
            moves.push_back({d, dd, nh});
        }
    }

    auto emit = [](int a, int d) {
        cout << a << " " << d << "\n";
        cout.flush();
    };
    auto pickVis = [&](LL vis) -> bool {
        for (auto& m : moves)
            if ((vis >> m.nh) & 1) { emit(m.axis, m.dir); return true; }
        return false;
    };
    auto defaultMove = [&]() { emit(moves[0].axis, moves[0].dir); };

    if (moves.empty()) { emit(0, 0); return; }

    // === SMALL BOARD: Hamiltonian cycle strategy ===
    if (vol <= 64) {
        auto [hcVis, hcOk, hcFar] = bfs(obs_mask, head_h, -1);
        bool hasCycle = ((hcVis ^ obs_mask) >> hcFar) & 1;

        if (hasCycle && !food.empty()) {
            auto [tbVis, tbOk, tbFar] = bfs(body_no_tail, head_h, tail_h);
            if (tbOk) {
                for (LL f : food) {
                    auto [fbVis, fbOk, fbFar] = bfs(body_mask, head_h, f);
                    if (!fbOk) continue;
                    for (auto& m : moves) {
                        if (!((fbVis >> m.nh) & 1)) continue;
                        LL simBlk = obs_mask | (1LL << m.nh);
                        for (int i = 1; i + 1 < (int)body.size(); ++i)
                            simBlk |= 1LL << body[i];
                        LL newTail = body[(int)body.size() - 2];
                        auto [sv, so, sf] = bfs(simBlk, m.nh, newTail);
                        if (so) { emit(m.axis, m.dir); return; }
                    }
                }
                pickVis(tbVis); return;
            } else {
                for (LL f : food) {
                    auto [rv, ro, rf] = bfs(body_mask, head_h, f);
                    if (ro && pickVis(rv)) return;
                }
                defaultMove(); return;
            }
        }
        if (hasCycle && food.empty()) {
            auto [tbVis, tbOk, tbFar] = bfs(body_no_tail, head_h, tail_h);
            if (tbOk && pickVis(tbVis)) return;
            defaultMove(); return;
        }
        if (!hasCycle) {
            for (LL f : food) {
                auto [rv, ro, rf] = bfs(body_mask, head_h, f);
                if (ro && pickVis(rv)) return;
            }
            defaultMove(); return;
        }
    }

    // === LARGE BOARD: greedy food-seeking ===
    if (!food.empty()) {
        for (LL f : food) {
            auto [rv, ro, rf] = bfs(body_mask, head_h, f);
            if (ro && pickVis(rv)) return;
        }
        defaultMove(); return;
    }
    defaultMove();
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int max_turns;
    if (!(cin >> D >> max_turns)) return 0;
    cin.ignore();

    BD.resize(D);
    LL vol = 1;
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
        cin >> BD[i];
        vol *= BD[i];
    }
    cin.ignore();

    DIM.resize(D);
    for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d)
        DIM[d] = 1LL << (3 * d);

    for (;;)
        processTurn(vol);

    return 0;
}

N-Dimensional Snake - 2D 50x50, 15 food, 50 obstacles

Approach: Here's my complete N-Dimensional Snake solver:

View C++ Code (5935 chars)
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
using VI = vector<int>;
using LL = long long;

int D;                    // Number of dimensions
VI BD;                    // Board size per dimension
vector<LL> DIM;           // DIM[d] = 1LL << (3*d)

// Position hashing: 3 bits per dimension interleaved
// Supports coordinates 0-7 per dim (21 dimensions in 63 bits)
static LL hashPos(const VI& p) {
    LL h = 0;
    for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d)
        h |= (LL)p[d] << (3 * d);
    return h;
}

static bool inBounds(LL h) {
    for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d)
        if ((int)((h >> (3 * d)) & 7) >= BD[d]) return false;
    return true;
}

// BFS on N-D bitmask grid.
// Returns {visited bitmask, target_reached, farthest_cell}
static tuple<LL, bool, LL> bfs(LL blocked, LL start, LL target) {
    LL vis = blocked | (1LL << start);
    queue<LL> q;
    q.push(start);
    bool reached = (target >= 0 && start == target);
    LL farthest = start;
    while (!q.empty()) {
        LL c = q.front(); q.pop();
        farthest = c;
        if (target >= 0 && c == target) { reached = true; break; }
        for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d) {
            LL n = c ^ DIM[d];
            if ((vis >> n) & 1) continue;
            if (!inBounds(n)) continue;
            vis |= 1LL << n;
            q.push(n);
        }
    }
    return {vis, reached, farthest};
}

static void processTurn(LL vol) {
    string line;
    if (!getline(cin, line)) return;

    istringstream hs(line);
    int snake_len;
    hs >> snake_len;
    VI head_p(D);
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) hs >> head_p[i];
    LL head_h = hashPos(head_p);

    vector<LL> body;
    body.push_back(head_h);
    for (int b = 1; b < snake_len; ++b) {
        getline(cin, line);
        istringstream ss(line);
        VI p(D);
        for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) ss >> p[i];
        body.push_back(hashPos(p));
    }

    getline(cin, line);
    int nf = 0;
    { istringstream ss(line); ss >> nf; }
    vector<LL> food(nf);
    for (int i = 0; i < nf; ++i) {
        getline(cin, line);
        istringstream ss(line);
        VI p(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) ss >> p[j];
        food[i] = hashPos(p);
    }

    getline(cin, line);
    int no = 0;
    { istringstream ss(line); ss >> no; }
    LL obs_mask = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < no; ++i) {
        getline(cin, line);
        istringstream ss(line);
        VI p(D);
        for (int j = 0; j < D; ++j) ss >> p[j];
        obs_mask |= 1LL << hashPos(p);
    }

    LL body_mask = obs_mask;
    for (LL b : body) body_mask |= 1LL << b;

    LL body_no_tail = obs_mask;
    for (int i = 0; i + 1 < (int)body.size(); ++i)
        body_no_tail |= 1LL << body[i];

    LL tail_h = body.back();

    struct Move { int axis, dir; LL nh; };
    vector<Move> moves;
    for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d) {
        for (int dd : {-1, 1}) {
            int nc = head_p[d] + dd;
            if (nc < 0 || nc >= BD[d]) continue;
            LL nh = head_h ^ DIM[d];
            if ((body_mask >> nh) & 1) continue;
            moves.push_back({d, dd, nh});
        }
    }

    auto emit = [](int a, int d) {
        cout << a << " " << d << "\n";
        cout.flush();
    };
    auto pickVis = [&](LL vis) -> bool {
        for (auto& m : moves)
            if ((vis >> m.nh) & 1) { emit(m.axis, m.dir); return true; }
        return false;
    };
    auto defaultMove = [&]() { emit(moves[0].axis, moves[0].dir); };

    if (moves.empty()) { emit(0, 0); return; }

    // === SMALL BOARD: Hamiltonian cycle strategy ===
    if (vol <= 64) {
        auto [hcVis, hcOk, hcFar] = bfs(obs_mask, head_h, -1);
        bool hasCycle = ((hcVis ^ obs_mask) >> hcFar) & 1;

        if (hasCycle && !food.empty()) {
            auto [tbVis, tbOk, tbFar] = bfs(body_no_tail, head_h, tail_h);
            if (tbOk) {
                for (LL f : food) {
                    auto [fbVis, fbOk, fbFar] = bfs(body_mask, head_h, f);
                    if (!fbOk) continue;
                    for (auto& m : moves) {
                        if (!((fbVis >> m.nh) & 1)) continue;
                        LL simBlk = obs_mask | (1LL << m.nh);
                        for (int i = 1; i + 1 < (int)body.size(); ++i)
                            simBlk |= 1LL << body[i];
                        LL newTail = body[(int)body.size() - 2];
                        auto [sv, so, sf] = bfs(simBlk, m.nh, newTail);
                        if (so) { emit(m.axis, m.dir); return; }
                    }
                }
                pickVis(tbVis); return;
            } else {
                for (LL f : food) {
                    auto [rv, ro, rf] = bfs(body_mask, head_h, f);
                    if (ro && pickVis(rv)) return;
                }
                defaultMove(); return;
            }
        }
        if (hasCycle && food.empty()) {
            auto [tbVis, tbOk, tbFar] = bfs(body_no_tail, head_h, tail_h);
            if (tbOk && pickVis(tbVis)) return;
            defaultMove(); return;
        }
        if (!hasCycle) {
            for (LL f : food) {
                auto [rv, ro, rf] = bfs(body_mask, head_h, f);
                if (ro && pickVis(rv)) return;
            }
            defaultMove(); return;
        }
    }

    // === LARGE BOARD: greedy food-seeking ===
    if (!food.empty()) {
        for (LL f : food) {
            auto [rv, ro, rf] = bfs(body_mask, head_h, f);
            if (ro && pickVis(rv)) return;
        }
        defaultMove(); return;
    }
    defaultMove();
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int max_turns;
    if (!(cin >> D >> max_turns)) return 0;
    cin.ignore();

    BD.resize(D);
    LL vol = 1;
    for (int i = 0; i < D; ++i) {
        cin >> BD[i];
        vol *= BD[i];
    }
    cin.ignore();

    DIM.resize(D);
    for (int d = 0; d < D; ++d)
        DIM[d] = 1LL << (3 * d);

    for (;;)
        processTurn(vol);

    return 0;
}

Test 22: Tetris Game (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to play Tetris optimally.

Your C# Tetris player will be tested with games ranging from short practice sessions to marathon games.

**Problem:**
Standard Tetris rules apply - variable width board (typically 10-20 columns),
pieces fall from top, clear lines by filling them completely, game over when pieces stack to the top.
The program receives pieces one at a time and must decide optimal placement.

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
```
width height num_pieces
For each piece:
Line: piece_type (I, O, T, S, Z, J, L)
Program outputs: rotation column
Then receives: result (ok, cleared_N, gameover)
```

**Output format (stdout, 1 line per piece, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
rotation column
- rotation: 0-3 (number of 90° clockwise rotations)
- column: 0 to width-1 (leftmost column of piece)

**Piece Types:**
- I: 4x1 line piece
- O: 2x2 square piece
- T: T-shaped piece
- S: S-shaped piece (right-leaning)
- Z: Z-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- J: L-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- L: L-shaped piece (right-leaning)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable board widths and long game sequences
4. Must place a peice within 5 seconds of it being written to STDIN.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.3.0-2.26078.5 (16f9bd284cd49604ac82998bfe778a8eb16d4347)) | max LangVersion 14


Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on game complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought

Write an AI that plays Tetris. Given the current board state and the next piece, choose where to place it to survive as long as possible and clear as many lines as you can. Good play means keeping the board flat, avoiding buried holes, and setting up multi-line clears.

Subpasses increase the board size and speed, demanding smarter lookahead and evaluation. The AI must balance short-term survival with long-term board health.
