View C++ Code (15959 chars)
#include <cstdio>
#include <cstring>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <algorithm>
#include <random>
#include <chrono>
#include <cstdint>

#ifdef _MSC_VER
#include <intrin.h>
#endif

using namespace std;

// ========================== FAST FILE I/O ==========================
static const int IO_BUF_SZ = 1 << 20;
static char in_buf[IO_BUF_SZ], out_buf[IO_BUF_SZ];
static int in_pos = 0, in_len = 0, out_pos = 0;
static FILE* fin_ptr = stdin;
static FILE* fout_ptr = stdout;

static void refill_in() {
    in_len = (int)fread(in_buf, 1, IO_BUF_SZ, fin_ptr);
    in_pos = 0;
}
static void flush_out() {
    if (out_pos > 0) { fwrite(out_buf, 1, out_pos, fout_ptr); out_pos = 0; }
}
static void skip_ws() {
    for (;;) {
        if (in_pos < in_len) {
            char c = in_buf[in_pos];
            if (c == ' ' || c == '\t' || c == '\r' || c == '\n' || c == ',') in_pos++;
            else return;
        } else { refill_in(); if (in_len == 0) return; }
    }
}
static bool read_int(int& val) {
    skip_ws();
    if (in_pos >= in_len) return false;
    val = 0;
    bool neg = false;
    if (in_buf[in_pos] == '-') { neg = true; in_pos++; }
    else if (in_buf[in_pos] == '+') { in_pos++; }
    bool ok = false;
    while (in_pos < in_len) {
        char c = in_buf[in_pos];
        if (c >= '0' && c <= '9') { val = val * 10 + (c - '0'); in_pos++; ok = true; }
        else break;
    }
    if (neg && ok) val = -val;
    return ok;
}
static void write_int(int val) {
    if (out_pos > IO_BUF_SZ - 20) flush_out();
    char tmp[20]; int tlen = 0;
    if (val < 0) { out_buf[out_pos++] = '-'; val = -val; }
    if (val == 0) tmp[tlen++] = '0';
    else { while (val > 0) { tmp[tlen++] = (char)('0' + (val % 10)); val /= 10; } }
    for (int i = tlen - 1; i >= 0; i--) out_buf[out_pos++] = tmp[i];
}
static void write_char(char c) {
    if (out_pos >= IO_BUF_SZ - 1) flush_out();
    out_buf[out_pos++] = c;
}

// ========================== GRAPH ==========================
struct Graph {
    int n, m, k;
    vector<vector<int>> adj;
    vector<int> deg;

    void init(int _n, int _m, int _k) {
        n = _n; m = _m; k = _k;
        adj.resize(n);
        deg.assign(n, 0);
    }
    void add_edge(int u, int v) {
        if (u == v) return;
        adj[u].push_back(v);
        adj[v].push_back(u);
    }
    void finalize() {
        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
            sort(adj[v].begin(), adj[v].end());
            adj[v].erase(unique(adj[v].begin(), adj[v].end()), adj[v].end());
            deg[v] = (int)adj[v].size();
        }
    }
    bool is_valid(const vector<int>& col) const {
        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
            for (int u : adj[v]) {
                if (u > v && col[u] == col[v]) return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
};

// ========================== BACKTRACKING DSATUR (n <= 60) ==========================
// Exact backtracking with DSATUR vertex selection heuristic.
// Tracks colored-neighbor color counts per vertex (ncc[v][c])
// and saturation degree (number of distinct neighbor colors).
// Picks most constrained vertex first, tries available colors.
class BacktrackDSATUR {
    const Graph& g;
    int k, n;
    long long explored;
    const long long MAX_EXP = 500000000LL;
    chrono::steady_clock::time_point t0;
    double tlim;
    vector<vector<int>> ncc;
    vector<int> sat;
    vector<int> col;
public:
    BacktrackDSATUR(const Graph& gr) : g(gr), k(gr.k), n(gr.n), explored(0), tlim(60.0) {}
    bool solve(vector<int>& result) {
        ncc.assign(n, vector<int>(k, 0));
        sat.assign(n, 0);
        col.assign(n, -1);
        explored = 0;
        t0 = chrono::steady_clock::now();
        bool ok = bt(n);
        result = col;
        return ok;
    }
private:
    bool bt(int remaining) {
        if (++explored > MAX_EXP) return false;
        if (remaining == 0) return true;
        if ((explored & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {
            if (chrono::duration<double>(chrono::steady_clock::now() - t0).count() > tlim)
                return false;
        }
        int bv = -1, bs = -1, bd = -1;
        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
            if (col[v] >= 0) continue;
            if (sat[v] > bs || (sat[v] == bs && g.deg[v] > bd)) {
                bv = v; bs = sat[v]; bd = g.deg[v];
            }
        }
        if (bv < 0) return true;
        for (int c = 0; c < k; c++) {
            if (ncc[bv][c] > 0) continue;
            col[bv] = c;
            vector<int> ch;
            for (int u : g.adj[bv]) {
                if (col[u] >= 0) continue;
                if (ncc[u][c]++ == 0) sat[u]++;
                ch.push_back(u);
            }
            if (bt(remaining - 1)) return true;
            col[bv] = -1;
            for (int u : ch) {
                if (--ncc[u][c] == 0) sat[u]--;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
};

// ========================== DSATUR GREEDY with bucket PQ ==========================
// DSATUR (Brélaz, 1979): picks uncolored vertex with maximum saturation degree
// (most distinct colors among its neighbors), breaking ties by degree.
// Uses uint64_t bitmask per vertex for forbidden colors, enabling O(1) smallest-color
// lookup via count-trailing-zeros. Bucket-based priority queue groups vertices
// by saturation level for efficient max-saturation selection.
// Supports k <= 64 colors.
class DSATURGreedy {
    const Graph& g;
    int k, n;
public:
    DSATURGreedy(const Graph& gr) : g(gr), k(gr.k), n(gr.n) {}

    bool solve(vector<int>& result) {
        result.assign(n, -1);
        if (n == 0) return true;

        vector<uint64_t> forbidden(n, 0);
        vector<int> sat(n, 0);
        vector<bool> done(n, false);

        uint64_t kmask = (k >= 64) ? ~0ULL : ((1ULL << k) - 1);
        int max_sat = min(k, n);
        vector<vector<int>> buckets(max_sat + 1);
        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) buckets[0].push_back(v);

        for (int step = 0; step < n; step++) {
            int cs = 0;
            while (cs <= max_sat && buckets[cs].empty()) cs++;
            if (cs > max_sat) break;

            auto& bk = buckets[cs];
            int bv = -1, bd = -1, bi = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < (int)bk.size(); i++) {
                if (!done[bk[i]] && g.deg[bk[i]] > bd) {
                    bv = bk[i]; bd = g.deg[bk[i]]; bi = i;
                }
            }
            if (bv < 0) break;

            bk[bi] = bk.back();
            bk.pop_back();
            done[bv] = true;

            uint64_t avail = (~forbidden[bv]) & kmask;
            if (avail == 0) return false;

            int chosen;
#if defined(_MSC_VER)
            unsigned long idx;
            _BitScanForward64(&idx, avail);
            chosen = (int)idx;
#else
            chosen = __builtin_ctzll(avail);
#endif

            result[bv] = chosen;
            uint64_t bit = 1ULL << chosen;

            for (int u : g.adj[bv]) {
                if (done[u]) continue;
                if (!(forbidden[u] & bit)) {
                    forbidden[u] |= bit;
                    int os = sat[u];
                    int ns = ++sat[u];
                    auto& ob = buckets[os];
                    for (int i = 0; i < (int)ob.size(); i++) {
                        if (ob[i] == u) {
                            ob[i] = ob.back();
                            ob.pop_back();
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                    if (ns <= max_sat) buckets[ns].push_back(u);
                }
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
};

// ========================== BFS LEVEL GREEDY ==========================
// BFS from arbitrary start vertices, processes nodes level-by-level.
// Excellent for bipartite and tree-like structures (guaranteed 2-coloring
// of bipartite graphs). Uses mark/unmark of neighbor colors for O(degree)
// per vertex with O(k) state size.
class BFSGreedy {
    const Graph& g;
    int k, n;
public:
    BFSGreedy(const Graph& gr) : g(gr), k(gr.k), n(gr.n) {}

    bool solve(vector<int>& result) {
        result.assign(n, -1);
        if (n == 0) return true;

        vector<int> order;
        order.reserve(n);
        vector<bool> visited(n, false);

        for (int s = 0; s < n; s++) {
            if (visited[s]) continue;
            queue<int> q;
            q.push(s);
            visited[s] = true;
            while (!q.empty()) {
                int v = q.front(); q.pop();
                order.push_back(v);
                for (int u : g.adj[v]) {
                    if (!visited[u]) {
                        visited[u] = true;
                        q.push(u);
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        vector<bool> used(k, false);
        for (int v : order) {
            for (int u : g.adj[v]) {
                if (result[u] >= 0 && result[u] < k) used[result[u]] = true;
            }
            int c = 0;
            while (c < k && used[c]) c++;
            for (int u : g.adj[v]) {
                if (result[u] >= 0 && result[u] < k) used[result[u]] = false;
            }
            result[v] = (c < k) ? c : 0;
        }
        return true;
    }
};

// ========================== RANDOM GREEDY ==========================
// Multiple attempts with random vertex orderings, plus a degree-ordered
// deterministic fallback. Validated after each successful complete coloring.
class RandomGreedy {
    const Graph& g;
    int k, n;
public:
    RandomGreedy(const Graph& gr) : g(gr), k(gr.k), n(gr.n) {}

    bool solve(vector<int>& result, int attempts = 50) {
        result.assign(n, -1);
        if (n == 0) return true;
        mt19937 rng(42);
        vector<int> order(n);
        iota(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);
        vector<bool> used(k, false);

        for (int a = 0; a < attempts; a++) {
            shuffle(order.begin(), order.end(), rng);
            fill(result.begin(), result.end(), -1);
            bool ok = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < n && ok; i++) {
                int v = order[i];
                for (int u : g.adj[v]) {
                    if (result[u] >= 0 && result[u] < k) used[result[u]] = true;
                }
                int c = 0;
                while (c < k && used[c]) c++;
                for (int u : g.adj[v]) {
                    if (result[u] >= 0 && result[u] < k) used[result[u]] = false;
                }
                if (c >= k) { ok = false; break; }
                result[v] = c;
            }
            if (ok && g.is_valid(result)) return true;
        }

        // Degree-ordered deterministic fallback
        fill(result.begin(), result.end(), -1);
        iota(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);
        sort(order.begin(), order.end(), [&](int a, int b) { return g.deg[a] > g.deg[b]; });
        bool ok = true;
        for (int i = 0; i < n && ok; i++) {
            int v = order[i];
            for (int u : g.adj[v]) {
                if (result[u] >= 0 && result[u] < k) used[result[u]] = true;
            }
            int c = 0;
            while (c < k && used[c]) c++;
            for (int u : g.adj[v]) {
                if (result[u] >= 0 && result[u] < k) used[result[u]] = false;
            }
            if (c >= k) { ok = false; break; }
            result[v] = c;
        }
        return ok;
    }
};

// ========================== LOCAL SEARCH ==========================
// Iteratively picks a random conflicting vertex and recolors it
// to minimize (ideally eliminate) its conflicts.
// Recomputes conflict set after each move.
bool local_search_fix(const Graph& g, vector<int>& col, int k, double tlimit) {
    int n = g.n;
    auto t0 = chrono::steady_clock::now();

    vector<int> conf;
    conf.reserve(n);
    for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
        for (int u : g.adj[v]) {
            if (u > v && col[v] == col[u] && col[v] >= 0) {
                conf.push_back(v);
                break;
            }
        }
    }
    if (conf.empty()) return true;

    mt19937 rng(77777);
    int iters = 0;
    const int MAX_IT = (int)min((long long)n * 500LL, 10000000LL);

    while (!conf.empty() && iters < MAX_IT) {
        if (++iters % 2000 == 0) {
            if (chrono::duration<double>(chrono::steady_clock::now() - t0).count() > tlimit) break;
        }

        uniform_int_distribution<int> dist(0, (int)conf.size() - 1);
        int v = conf[dist(rng)];

        int best_c = -1, best_bad = n + 1;
        for (int c = 0; c < k; c++) {
            int bad = 0;
            for (int u : g.adj[v]) {
                if (col[u] == c) bad++;
            }
            if (bad == 0) { best_c = c; break; }
            if (bad < best_bad) { best_bad = bad; best_c = c; }
        }

        col[v] = best_c;

        conf.clear();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (col[i] < 0) continue;
            for (int u : g.adj[i]) {
                if (u > i && col[i] == col[u]) {
                    conf.push_back(i);
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return conf.empty();
}

// ========================== MAIN ==========================
int main() {
    fin_ptr = stdin;
    fout_ptr = stdout;

    int n, m, k;
    if (!read_int(n) || !read_int(m) || !read_int(k)) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error reading input\n");
        return 1;
    }
    fprintf(stderr, "n=%d m=%d k=%d\n", n, m, k);
    if (n == 0) { flush_out(); return 0; }

    Graph g;
    g.init(n, m, k);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
        int u, v;
        if (!read_int(u) || !read_int(v)) break;
        g.add_edge(u, v);
    }
    g.finalize();

    int max_deg = 0;
    long long total_deg = 0;
    for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
        max_deg = max(max_deg, g.deg[v]);
        total_deg += g.deg[v];
    }
    fprintf(stderr, "max_deg=%d avg_deg=%.1f\n", max_deg, total_deg * 1.0 / max(n, 1));

    vector<int> color;
    bool ok = false;

    // Strategy 1: Exact backtracking for very small graphs (n <= 60)
    if (n <= 60) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Strategy: Backtrack DSATUR\n");
        BacktrackDSATUR solver(g);
        ok = solver.solve(color);
        if (ok) ok = g.is_valid(color);
        fprintf(stderr, "Backtrack: %s\n", ok ? "OK" : "FAIL");
    }

    // Strategy 2: DSATUR greedy for medium-large graphs (n <= 300K, k <= 64)
    if (!ok && n <= 300000) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Strategy: DSATUR Greedy\n");
        DSATURGreedy solver(g);
        ok = solver.solve(color);
        if (ok) ok = g.is_valid(color);
        fprintf(stderr, "DSATUR: %s\n", ok ? "OK" : "FAIL");
    }

    // Strategy 3: BFS level greedy (handles bipartite-like structures well)
    if (!ok) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Strategy: BFS Greedy\n");
        BFSGreedy solver(g);
        ok = solver.solve(color);
        if (ok) ok = g.is_valid(color);
        fprintf(stderr, "BFS: %s\n", ok ? "OK" : "FAIL");
    }

    // Strategy 4: Random greedy with degree-ordered fallback
    if (!ok) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Strategy: Random Greedy\n");
        RandomGreedy solver(g);
        ok = solver.solve(color, 60);
        if (ok) ok = g.is_valid(color);
        fprintf(stderr, "Random: %s\n", ok ? "OK" : "FAIL");
    }

    // Strategy 5: Local search to resolve residual conflicts
    if (!ok) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Strategy: Local Search\n");
        if (local_search_fix(g, color, k, 120.0)) {
            ok = g.is_valid(color);
        }
        fprintf(stderr, "LocalSearch: %s\n", ok ? "OK" : "FAIL");
    }

    if (ok) {
        ok = g.is_valid(color);
        if (!ok) fprintf(stderr, "WARNING: validation failed\n");
    }
    if (!ok) fprintf(stderr, "WARNING: no valid coloring found\n");

    // Output
    for (int v = 0; v < n; v++) {
        if (v > 0) write_char(' ');
        write_int(color[v] >= 0 ? color[v] : 0);
    }
    write_char('\n');
    flush_out();

    fprintf(stderr, "Done. ok=%d\n", (int)ok);
    return 0;
}

Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Minimum Vertex Cover problem.

Your code may see graphs with up to 2M vertices and 15M edges.

**Problem:**
Find the minimum set of vertices such that every edge has at least one endpoint in the set.
This is NP-Hard and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
vertex_count
vertex_1 vertex_2 ... vertex_k (vertices in the cover, space-separated)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All edges are covered by selected vertices
- Vertex set size is minimized (or near-minimized for large graphs)
- Output format is correct

**Failure Criteria:**
- Not all edges are covered
- Timeout without solution
- Invalid output format

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Be aware that default warnings are enabled and will cause a compilation failure,
so ensure that you write warning-free code.

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.

Test 29: Maximum Clique (C#) Score: 0.00 / 18
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the Maximum Clique problem, which is NP Hard.

Your C# maximum clique finder will be tested with graphs ranging from sparse to very dense instances.
The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL graph complexities, from 20 vertices to 1 million.

**Problem:**
Find the largest complete subgraph (clique) in an undirected graph.
A clique is a set of vertices where every pair of vertices is connected by an edge.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
clique_size
vertex_1 vertex_2 ... vertex_k (vertices in the maximum clique, space-separated)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All vertices in output form a complete subgraph
- No larger clique exists in the graph
- Output format is correct

**Failure Criteria:**
- Output vertices don't form a clique
- Larger clique exists
- Timeout without solution
- Invalid output format

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable graph sizes and densities
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying graph complexities efficiently

Hints:
- You may wish to drop to a hueristic if time is running out for partial score.
- Use threads to process in parallel.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (csc.exe 5.3.0-2.26078.5 (16f9bd284cd49604ac82998bfe778a8eb16d4347)) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.

Find the largest group of nodes in a graph where every pair is directly connected. Think of finding the biggest circle of friends where everyone knows everyone else — no strangers allowed.

This is one of the hardest problems in computer science (NP-hard). The test uses planted instances with a known answer for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the graph size and density.

Compilation error: Compilation failed:
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmptu8mpstz.cs(139,42): error CS0023: Operator '!' cannot be applied to operand of type 'int'
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmptu8mpstz.cs(147,40)

Maximum Clique - 20 vertices, dense

Approach: Looking at this problem, I need to handle graphs from 20 to 1M vertices across all densities within 5 minutes. I'll implement a multi-strategy approach: 1. **Large/medium graphs**: Heuristic greedy coloring with restarts (max–saturation), peeling, and bit-parallel search 2. **Small graphs**: Exact Tomita branch-and-bound for guaranteed optimality 3. **Timeout safety**: Return best heuristic resul...

View C# Code (14425 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text;
using System.Threading;

class Program
{
    static int n, m;
    static List<int>[] adj, adjAll;
    static int[] degree;
    static long startMs;
    static readonly int timeLimitMs = 260000; // ~4.3 minutes safety margin

    static void Main()
    {
        new Thread(Run, 256 * 1024 * 1024).Start();
    }

    static void Run()
    {
        var sr = new StreamReader(Console.OpenStandardInput(), Encoding.ASCII, false, 1 << 18);
        string line;
        while ((line = sr.ReadLine()) != null)
        {
            line = line.Trim();
            if (line.Length > 0 && line[0] != '#') break;
        }
        var p = line.Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        n = int.Parse(p[0]); m = int.Parse(p[1]);

        adj = new List<int>[n];
        adjAll = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { adj[i] = new List<int>(); adjAll[i] = new List<int>(); }

        degree = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            p = sr.ReadLine().Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
            int u = int.Parse(p[0]), v = int.Parse(p[1]);
            if (u == v) continue;
            adj[u].Add(v);
            adj[v].Add(u);
            degree[u]++; degree[v]++;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { adjAll[i].Add(i); adjAll[i].AddRange(adj[i]); }

        startMs = (long)Stopwatch.GetTimestamp() / 10000L;
        var best = Heuristic();

        if (n <= 5000)
        {
            var seeds = MakeSeeds(300);
            int ub = UpperBound();
            if (best.Count < ub) BestFirstSearch(seeds);
            if (n <= 500 && best.Count < ub && Elapsed() < timeLimitMs - 15000)
                ExactSearch(seeds);
        }

        var sb = new StringBuilder();
        sb.Append(best.Count);
        foreach (var v in best) { sb.Append(' '); sb.Append(v); }
        Console.WriteLine(sb.ToString());
    }

    // === TIMER ===
    static long Elapsed() => (long)Stopwatch.GetTimestamp() / 10000L - startMs;
    static bool TimeLeft(long ms) => Elapsed() < ms;

    // === HEURISTIC: Greedy Max-Saturation with Restarts ===
    static List<int> Heuristic()
    {
        var rng = new Random(42);
        var best = new List<int>();
        for (int r = 0; r < 5000 && TimeLeft(timeLimitMs - 8000); r++)
        {
            var c = GreedySat(rng);
            if (c.Count > best.Count) best = c;
            if (best.Count * (best.Count - 1) / 2 == m) break;
            if (r > 0 && r % 50 == 0 && best.Count > 15 && n > 500)
            { var e = TryExtend(best, rng); if (e.Count > best.Count) best = e; }
        }
        return best;
    }

    static List<int> GreedySat(Random rng)
    {
        int sz = Math.Min(n, 60000);
        var idx = new int[sz];
        for (int i = 0; i < sz; i++) idx[i] = i;
        Shuffle(idx, 0, sz, rng);

        var inC = new bool[n];
        var clique = new List<int>();
        int remaining = sz, actCount = 0;

        while (remaining > 0)
        {
            int best = -1, bs = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < sz; i++)
            {
                if (inC[idx[i]] || !Active(idx[i], inC, ref actCount)) continue;
                int s = SatDeg(idx[i], inC);
                if (s > bs || (s == bs && rng.Next(100) < 50)) { bs = s; best = idx[i]; }
            }
            if (best < 0) break;
            inC[best] = true; clique.Add(best); remaining--;
        }
        return clique;
    }

    static void Shuffle(int[] a, int s, int len, Random r)
    {
        for (int i = len - 1; i > s; i--)
        { int j = r.Next(i + 1); var t = a[i]; a[i] = a[j]; a[j] = t; }
    }

    static bool Active(int v, bool[] inC, ref int c) { c++; return !inC[v]; }

    static int SatDeg(int v, bool[] inC)
    {
        int cnt = 0;
        foreach (int u in adj[v]) if (inC[u]) cnt++;
        return cnt;
    }

    static List<int> TryExtend(List<int> baseC, Random rng)
    {
        var inC = new bool[n];
        foreach (var v in baseC) inC[v] = true;
        var candidates = new List<int>();
        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++)
        {
            if (inC[v]) continue;
            bool ok = true;
            foreach (var u in baseC) if (!adjAll[u].BinarySearch(v)) { ok = false; break; }
            if (ok) candidates.Add(v);
        }
        Shuffle(candidates, rng);
        var ext = new List<int>(baseC);
        foreach (var v in candidates)
        {
            bool ok = true;
            foreach (var u in ext) if (!adjAll[u].BinarySearch(v)) { ok = false; break; }
            if (ok) ext.Add(v);
        }
        return ext;
    }

    // === UPPER BOUND (Greedy Coloring) ===
    static int UpperBound()
    {
        int[] order = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) order[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(order, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));
        var used = new bool[n];
        int mx = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            int v = order[i]; used[v] = true;
            var nCol = new bool[n];
            foreach (int u in adj[v]) if (used[u]) nCol[u] = true;
            int c = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
            {
                if (!nCol[order[j]]) { nCol[order[j]] = true; c++; }
                if (c + (n - i) <= mx) break;
            }
            if (c > mx) mx = c;
        }
        return mx;
    }

    // === SEED ORDERING ===
    static int[] MakeSeeds(int max)
    {
        if (n <= max) { int[] a = new int[n]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) a[i] = i; return a; }
        int k = Math.Min(n, max);
        var ord = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) ord[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(ord, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));
        int[] seeds = new int[k];
        for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) seeds[i] = ord[i];
        return seeds;
    }

    // === BEST-FIRST SEARCH ===
    static void BestFirstSearch(int[] seeds)
    {
        int[] col = new int[n];
        int[] ci = new int[n];
        var st = new State[n];
        int globalBest = HeuristicGreedyCol(seeds);
        long deadline = startMs + timeLimitMs - 12000;

        for (int s = 0; s < seeds.Length; s++)
        {
            if (!TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) break;
            int startV = seeds[s];

            // BFS coloring from startV
            var reach = adjAll[startV];
            var order = new int[reach.Count];
            int pos = 0;
            foreach (int v in reach) if (v != startV) order[pos++] = v;
            Array.Sort(order, 0, pos, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));

            var used = new bool[n]; used[startV] = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < pos; i++)
            {
                int v = order[i]; used[v] = true;
                int mx = 0;
                var nc = new bool[n];
                foreach (int u in adjAll[v])
                {
                    int uu = u < 0 ? ~u : u;
                    if (uu != v && used[uu]) nc[uu] = true;
                }
                for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
                {
                    if (!nc[order[j]]) { nc[order[j]] = true; mx++; }
                    if (mx + (pos - i) <= globalBest) break;
                }
                col[v] = mx;
            }

            // Greedy initial clique
            var init = new List<int>(); init.Add(startV);
            foreach (int v in order) if (col[v] >= globalBest) init.Add(v);
            int depth = init.Count;
            if (depth <= globalBest) continue;
            for (int i = 0; i < depth; i++) { st[i].v = init[i]; st[i].ci = col[init[i]]; }
            globalBest = depth;
            RecBFS(st, ref globalBest, col, ci, pos, order, depth, deadline);
        }
    }

    static int RecBFS(State[] st, ref int globalBest, int[] col, int[] ci,
                      int candSz, int[] cand, int depth, long deadline)
    {
        if (depth <= globalBest) return 0;
        if (depth == n || candSz == 0) { globalBest = depth; return depth; }

        // Prune
        int mx = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < candSz; i++) if (col[cand[i]] > mx) mx = col[cand[i]];
        if (depth + mx <= globalBest) return 0;

        int ub = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < candSz; i++) if (col[cand[i]] + depth > globalBest) ub++;
        if (ub > 35 || candSz > 8000)
        {
            // Greedy only
            for (int i = 0; i < candSz; i++)
            {
                int v = cand[i];
                if (col[v] + depth <= globalBest) continue;
                st[depth].v = v; st[depth].ci = 0;
                int r = RecBFS(st, ref globalBest, col, ci, 0, cand, depth + 1, deadline);
                if (r > globalBest) globalBest = r;
            }
            return globalBest;
        }

        // Full expansion
        var nc = new List<int>(ub);
        for (int i = 0; i < candSz; i++) if (col[cand[i]] + depth > globalBest) nc.Add(cand[i]);

        int iL = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < nc.Count; i++) { col[nc[i]] += depth; if (col[nc[i]] > globalBest) ci[iL++] = i; }

        int improved = 0;
        for (int ii = iL - 1; ii >= 0 && improved < 8; ii--)
        {
            int idx = ci[ii], v = nc[idx];
            st[depth].v = v; st[depth].ci = col[v];
            int r = RecBFS(st, ref globalBest, col, ci, ii, nc, depth + 1, deadline);
            if (r > globalBest) { globalBest = r; improved++; }
            if (!TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) break;
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < nc.Count; i++) col[nc[i]] -= depth;
        return globalBest;
    }

    static int HeuristicGreedyCol(int[] seeds)
    {
        if (n == 0) return 0;
        int bestV = seeds[0];
        for (int i = 1; i < seeds.Length; i++) if (degree[seeds[i]] > degree[bestV]) bestV = seeds[i];

        var cand = new int[n];
        int pos = 0;
        foreach (int v in adjAll[bestV]) cand[pos++] = v;
        Array.Sort(cand, 0, pos, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));

        var used = new bool[n]; used[bestV] = true;
        int[] col = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < pos; i++)
        {
            int v = cand[i]; used[v] = true;
            int mx = 0;
            var nc = new bool[n];
            foreach (int u in adjAll[v])
            {
                int uu = u < 0 ? ~u : u;
                if (uu != v && used[uu]) nc[uu] = true;
            }
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
            {
                if (!nc[cand[j]]) { nc[cand[j]] = true; mx++; }
                if (mx + (pos - i) <= mx) break;
            }
            col[v] = mx;
        }

        int best = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < pos; i++) if (col[cand[i]] >= best) best = col[cand[i]] + 1;
        return best;
    }

    // === EXACT SEARCH (Tomita-style for small graphs) ===
    static void ExactSearch(int[] seeds)
    {
        var col = new int[n];
        int[] best = new int[n]; int bestSz = 0;
        var st = new int[n];
        int explored = 0;
        long deadline = startMs + timeLimitMs - 5000;

        for (int s = 0; s < seeds.Length; s++)
        {
            if (!TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) break;
            int startV = seeds[s];
            var reach = adjAll[startV];
            var order = new int[reach.Count];
            int pos = 0;
            foreach (int v in reach) if (v != startV) order[pos++] = v;
            Array.Sort(order, 0, pos, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));

            var used = new bool[n]; used[startV] = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < pos; i++)
            {
                int v = order[i]; used[v] = true;
                int mx = 0;
                var nc = new bool[n];
                foreach (int u in adjAll[v])
                {
                    int uu = u < 0 ? ~u : u;
                    if (uu != v && used[uu]) nc[uu] = true;
                }
                for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
                {
                    if (!nc[order[j]]) { nc[order[j]] = true; mx++; }
                }
                col[v] = mx;
            }

            st[0] = startV;
            RecExact(st, ref bestSz, best, col, order, pos, 1, ref explored, deadline);
            explored++;
            if (!TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) break;
        }
    }

    static void RecExact(int[] st, ref int bestSz, int[] best, int[] col,
                          int[] cand, int candSz, int depth, ref int explored, long deadline)
    {
        if (depth > bestSz) { bestSz = depth; Array.Copy(st, best, depth); }

        if (candSz == 0 || depth + col[cand[0]] <= bestSz) return;
        if ((explored & 0xFFFF) == 0 && !TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) return;

        int iL = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < candSz && depth + col[cand[i]] > bestSz; i++) iL++;

        for (int ii = iL - 1; ii >= 0; ii--)
        {
            int v = cand[ii];
            st[depth] = v;
            var nc = new List<int>();
            for (int i = 0; i < ii; i++) if (adjAll[v].BinarySearch(cand[i]) >= 0) nc.Add(cand[i]);

            int[] ncArr = nc.ToArray();
            ColorBFS(ncArr, col, ref bestSz, depth);
            int ncSz = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < ncArr.Length && depth + 1 + col[ncArr[i]] > bestSz; i++) ncSz++;
            RecExact(st, ref bestSz, best, col, ncArr, ncSz, depth + 1, ref explored, deadline);
            explored++;
        }
    }

    static void ColorBFS(int[] verts, int[] col, ref int bestSz, int depth)
    {
        if (verts.Length == 0) return;
        Array.Sort(verts);
        int k = verts.Length;

        for (int i = k - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            int v = verts[i];
            int maxC = 0;
            for (int j = i + 1; j < k; j++)
            {
                if (adjAll[v].BinarySearch(verts[j]) >= 0)
                {
                    int c = col[verts[j]];
                    if (c > maxC) maxC = c;
                }
            }
            col[v] = maxC + 1;
            if (col[v] + depth <= bestSz) return;
        }
    }

    // === UTILITIES ===
    static void Shuffle<T>(List<T> list, Random rng)
    {
        for (int i = list.Count - 1; i > 0; i--)
        {
            int j = rng.Next(i + 1);
            var t = list[i]; list[i] = list[j]; list[j] = t;
        }
    }

    struct State { public int v, ci; }
}

Maximum Clique - 40 vertices, medium density

Approach: Looking at this problem, I need to handle graphs from 20 to 1M vertices across all densities within 5 minutes. I'll implement a multi-strategy approach: 1. **Large/medium graphs**: Heuristic greedy coloring with restarts (max–saturation), peeling, and bit-parallel search 2. **Small graphs**: Exact Tomita branch-and-bound for guaranteed optimality 3. **Timeout safety**: Return best heuristic resul...

View C# Code (14425 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text;
using System.Threading;

class Program
{
    static int n, m;
    static List<int>[] adj, adjAll;
    static int[] degree;
    static long startMs;
    static readonly int timeLimitMs = 260000; // ~4.3 minutes safety margin

    static void Main()
    {
        new Thread(Run, 256 * 1024 * 1024).Start();
    }

    static void Run()
    {
        var sr = new StreamReader(Console.OpenStandardInput(), Encoding.ASCII, false, 1 << 18);
        string line;
        while ((line = sr.ReadLine()) != null)
        {
            line = line.Trim();
            if (line.Length > 0 && line[0] != '#') break;
        }
        var p = line.Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        n = int.Parse(p[0]); m = int.Parse(p[1]);

        adj = new List<int>[n];
        adjAll = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { adj[i] = new List<int>(); adjAll[i] = new List<int>(); }

        degree = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            p = sr.ReadLine().Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
            int u = int.Parse(p[0]), v = int.Parse(p[1]);
            if (u == v) continue;
            adj[u].Add(v);
            adj[v].Add(u);
            degree[u]++; degree[v]++;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { adjAll[i].Add(i); adjAll[i].AddRange(adj[i]); }

        startMs = (long)Stopwatch.GetTimestamp() / 10000L;
        var best = Heuristic();

        if (n <= 5000)
        {
            var seeds = MakeSeeds(300);
            int ub = UpperBound();
            if (best.Count < ub) BestFirstSearch(seeds);
            if (n <= 500 && best.Count < ub && Elapsed() < timeLimitMs - 15000)
                ExactSearch(seeds);
        }

        var sb = new StringBuilder();
        sb.Append(best.Count);
        foreach (var v in best) { sb.Append(' '); sb.Append(v); }
        Console.WriteLine(sb.ToString());
    }

    // === TIMER ===
    static long Elapsed() => (long)Stopwatch.GetTimestamp() / 10000L - startMs;
    static bool TimeLeft(long ms) => Elapsed() < ms;

    // === HEURISTIC: Greedy Max-Saturation with Restarts ===
    static List<int> Heuristic()
    {
        var rng = new Random(42);
        var best = new List<int>();
        for (int r = 0; r < 5000 && TimeLeft(timeLimitMs - 8000); r++)
        {
            var c = GreedySat(rng);
            if (c.Count > best.Count) best = c;
            if (best.Count * (best.Count - 1) / 2 == m) break;
            if (r > 0 && r % 50 == 0 && best.Count > 15 && n > 500)
            { var e = TryExtend(best, rng); if (e.Count > best.Count) best = e; }
        }
        return best;
    }

    static List<int> GreedySat(Random rng)
    {
        int sz = Math.Min(n, 60000);
        var idx = new int[sz];
        for (int i = 0; i < sz; i++) idx[i] = i;
        Shuffle(idx, 0, sz, rng);

        var inC = new bool[n];
        var clique = new List<int>();
        int remaining = sz, actCount = 0;

        while (remaining > 0)
        {
            int best = -1, bs = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < sz; i++)
            {
                if (inC[idx[i]] || !Active(idx[i], inC, ref actCount)) continue;
                int s = SatDeg(idx[i], inC);
                if (s > bs || (s == bs && rng.Next(100) < 50)) { bs = s; best = idx[i]; }
            }
            if (best < 0) break;
            inC[best] = true; clique.Add(best); remaining--;
        }
        return clique;
    }

    static void Shuffle(int[] a, int s, int len, Random r)
    {
        for (int i = len - 1; i > s; i--)
        { int j = r.Next(i + 1); var t = a[i]; a[i] = a[j]; a[j] = t; }
    }

    static bool Active(int v, bool[] inC, ref int c) { c++; return !inC[v]; }

    static int SatDeg(int v, bool[] inC)
    {
        int cnt = 0;
        foreach (int u in adj[v]) if (inC[u]) cnt++;
        return cnt;
    }

    static List<int> TryExtend(List<int> baseC, Random rng)
    {
        var inC = new bool[n];
        foreach (var v in baseC) inC[v] = true;
        var candidates = new List<int>();
        for (int v = 0; v < n; v++)
        {
            if (inC[v]) continue;
            bool ok = true;
            foreach (var u in baseC) if (!adjAll[u].BinarySearch(v)) { ok = false; break; }
            if (ok) candidates.Add(v);
        }
        Shuffle(candidates, rng);
        var ext = new List<int>(baseC);
        foreach (var v in candidates)
        {
            bool ok = true;
            foreach (var u in ext) if (!adjAll[u].BinarySearch(v)) { ok = false; break; }
            if (ok) ext.Add(v);
        }
        return ext;
    }

    // === UPPER BOUND (Greedy Coloring) ===
    static int UpperBound()
    {
        int[] order = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) order[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(order, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));
        var used = new bool[n];
        int mx = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            int v = order[i]; used[v] = true;
            var nCol = new bool[n];
            foreach (int u in adj[v]) if (used[u]) nCol[u] = true;
            int c = 0;
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
            {
                if (!nCol[order[j]]) { nCol[order[j]] = true; c++; }
                if (c + (n - i) <= mx) break;
            }
            if (c > mx) mx = c;
        }
        return mx;
    }

    // === SEED ORDERING ===
    static int[] MakeSeeds(int max)
    {
        if (n <= max) { int[] a = new int[n]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) a[i] = i; return a; }
        int k = Math.Min(n, max);
        var ord = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) ord[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(ord, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));
        int[] seeds = new int[k];
        for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) seeds[i] = ord[i];
        return seeds;
    }

    // === BEST-FIRST SEARCH ===
    static void BestFirstSearch(int[] seeds)
    {
        int[] col = new int[n];
        int[] ci = new int[n];
        var st = new State[n];
        int globalBest = HeuristicGreedyCol(seeds);
        long deadline = startMs + timeLimitMs - 12000;

        for (int s = 0; s < seeds.Length; s++)
        {
            if (!TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) break;
            int startV = seeds[s];

            // BFS coloring from startV
            var reach = adjAll[startV];
            var order = new int[reach.Count];
            int pos = 0;
            foreach (int v in reach) if (v != startV) order[pos++] = v;
            Array.Sort(order, 0, pos, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));

            var used = new bool[n]; used[startV] = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < pos; i++)
            {
                int v = order[i]; used[v] = true;
                int mx = 0;
                var nc = new bool[n];
                foreach (int u in adjAll[v])
                {
                    int uu = u < 0 ? ~u : u;
                    if (uu != v && used[uu]) nc[uu] = true;
                }
                for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
                {
                    if (!nc[order[j]]) { nc[order[j]] = true; mx++; }
                    if (mx + (pos - i) <= globalBest) break;
                }
                col[v] = mx;
            }

            // Greedy initial clique
            var init = new List<int>(); init.Add(startV);
            foreach (int v in order) if (col[v] >= globalBest) init.Add(v);
            int depth = init.Count;
            if (depth <= globalBest) continue;
            for (int i = 0; i < depth; i++) { st[i].v = init[i]; st[i].ci = col[init[i]]; }
            globalBest = depth;
            RecBFS(st, ref globalBest, col, ci, pos, order, depth, deadline);
        }
    }

    static int RecBFS(State[] st, ref int globalBest, int[] col, int[] ci,
                      int candSz, int[] cand, int depth, long deadline)
    {
        if (depth <= globalBest) return 0;
        if (depth == n || candSz == 0) { globalBest = depth; return depth; }

        // Prune
        int mx = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < candSz; i++) if (col[cand[i]] > mx) mx = col[cand[i]];
        if (depth + mx <= globalBest) return 0;

        int ub = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < candSz; i++) if (col[cand[i]] + depth > globalBest) ub++;
        if (ub > 35 || candSz > 8000)
        {
            // Greedy only
            for (int i = 0; i < candSz; i++)
            {
                int v = cand[i];
                if (col[v] + depth <= globalBest) continue;
                st[depth].v = v; st[depth].ci = 0;
                int r = RecBFS(st, ref globalBest, col, ci, 0, cand, depth + 1, deadline);
                if (r > globalBest) globalBest = r;
            }
            return globalBest;
        }

        // Full expansion
        var nc = new List<int>(ub);
        for (int i = 0; i < candSz; i++) if (col[cand[i]] + depth > globalBest) nc.Add(cand[i]);

        int iL = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < nc.Count; i++) { col[nc[i]] += depth; if (col[nc[i]] > globalBest) ci[iL++] = i; }

        int improved = 0;
        for (int ii = iL - 1; ii >= 0 && improved < 8; ii--)
        {
            int idx = ci[ii], v = nc[idx];
            st[depth].v = v; st[depth].ci = col[v];
            int r = RecBFS(st, ref globalBest, col, ci, ii, nc, depth + 1, deadline);
            if (r > globalBest) { globalBest = r; improved++; }
            if (!TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) break;
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < nc.Count; i++) col[nc[i]] -= depth;
        return globalBest;
    }

    static int HeuristicGreedyCol(int[] seeds)
    {
        if (n == 0) return 0;
        int bestV = seeds[0];
        for (int i = 1; i < seeds.Length; i++) if (degree[seeds[i]] > degree[bestV]) bestV = seeds[i];

        var cand = new int[n];
        int pos = 0;
        foreach (int v in adjAll[bestV]) cand[pos++] = v;
        Array.Sort(cand, 0, pos, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));

        var used = new bool[n]; used[bestV] = true;
        int[] col = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < pos; i++)
        {
            int v = cand[i]; used[v] = true;
            int mx = 0;
            var nc = new bool[n];
            foreach (int u in adjAll[v])
            {
                int uu = u < 0 ? ~u : u;
                if (uu != v && used[uu]) nc[uu] = true;
            }
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
            {
                if (!nc[cand[j]]) { nc[cand[j]] = true; mx++; }
                if (mx + (pos - i) <= mx) break;
            }
            col[v] = mx;
        }

        int best = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < pos; i++) if (col[cand[i]] >= best) best = col[cand[i]] + 1;
        return best;
    }

    // === EXACT SEARCH (Tomita-style for small graphs) ===
    static void ExactSearch(int[] seeds)
    {
        var col = new int[n];
        int[] best = new int[n]; int bestSz = 0;
        var st = new int[n];
        int explored = 0;
        long deadline = startMs + timeLimitMs - 5000;

        for (int s = 0; s < seeds.Length; s++)
        {
            if (!TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) break;
            int startV = seeds[s];
            var reach = adjAll[startV];
            var order = new int[reach.Count];
            int pos = 0;
            foreach (int v in reach) if (v != startV) order[pos++] = v;
            Array.Sort(order, 0, pos, (a, b) => degree[b].CompareTo(degree[a]));

            var used = new bool[n]; used[startV] = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < pos; i++)
            {
                int v = order[i]; used[v] = true;
                int mx = 0;
                var nc = new bool[n];
                foreach (int u in adjAll[v])
                {
                    int uu = u < 0 ? ~u : u;
                    if (uu != v && used[uu]) nc[uu] = true;
                }
                for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)
                {
                    if (!nc[order[j]]) { nc[order[j]] = true; mx++; }
                }
                col[v] = mx;
            }

            st[0] = startV;
            RecExact(st, ref bestSz, best, col, order, pos, 1, ref explored, deadline);
            explored++;
            if (!TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) break;
        }
    }

    static void RecExact(int[] st, ref int bestSz, int[] best, int[] col,
                          int[] cand, int candSz, int depth, ref int explored, long deadline)
    {
        if (depth > bestSz) { bestSz = depth; Array.Copy(st, best, depth); }

        if (candSz == 0 || depth + col[cand[0]] <= bestSz) return;
        if ((explored & 0xFFFF) == 0 && !TimeLeft(deadline - startMs)) return;

        int iL = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < candSz && depth + col[cand[i]] > bestSz; i++) iL++;

        for (int ii = iL - 1; ii >= 0; ii--)
        {
            int v = cand[ii];
            st[depth] = v;
            var nc = new List<int>();
            for (int i = 0; i < ii; i++) if (adjAll[v].BinarySearch(cand[i]) >= 0) nc.Add(cand[i]);

            int[] ncArr = nc.ToArray();
            ColorBFS(ncArr, col, ref bestSz, depth);
            int ncSz = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < ncArr.Length && depth + 1 + col[ncArr[i]] > bestSz; i++) ncSz++;
            RecExact(st, ref bestSz, best, col, ncArr, ncSz, depth + 1, ref explored, deadline);
            explored++;
        }
    }

    static void ColorBFS(int[] verts, int[] col, ref int bestSz, int depth)
    {
        if (verts.Length == 0) return;
        Array.Sort(verts);
        int k = verts.Length;

        for (int i = k - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            int v = verts[i];
            int maxC = 0;
            for (int j = i + 1; j < k; j++)
            {
                if (adjAll[v].BinarySearch(verts[j]) >= 0)
                {
                    int c = col[verts[j]];
                    if (c > maxC) maxC = c;
                }
            }
            col[v] = maxC + 1;
            if (col[v] + depth <= bestSz) return;
        }
    }

    // === UTILITIES ===
    static void Shuffle<T>(List<T> list, Random rng)
    {
        for (int i = list.Count - 1; i > 0; i--)
        {
            int j = rng.Next(i + 1);
            var t = list[i]; list[i] = list[j]; list[j] = t;
        }
    }

    struct State { public int v, ci; }
}

Test 30: 3-SAT Solver (Python) Score: 0.00 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Python code to solve the 3-SAT (Boolean Satisfiability) problem, which is NP-Complete.

**What is 3-SAT?**
You have N boolean variables (x1, x2, ..., xN), each of which is either True or False.
You are given M "clauses". Each clause is a constraint containing exactly 3 "literals".
A literal is either:
- A positive integer (e.g. 5) meaning "variable 5 must be True", or
- A negative integer (e.g. -5) meaning "variable 5 must be False".

A clause is **satisfied** if ANY of its 3 literals evaluates to True.
The formula is **satisfiable** if ALL clauses can be satisfied simultaneously
by some assignment of True/False to the variables.

**Example:**
Variables: x1, x2, x3
Clause "1 -2 3" means: x1=True OR x2=False OR x3=True.
If x1=True, x2=True, x3=False → clause satisfied (x1=True is enough).

Your task: find an assignment of True/False to every variable that satisfies
every clause. All test instances are guaranteed to be satisfiable.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_variables num_clauses
lit1 lit2 lit3
lit1 lit2 lit3
...
```
Variables are 1-indexed. Negative literals mean negation.

**Output format (stdout):**
```
SAT
v1 v2 v3 ... vN
```
Where each vi is 1 (True) or 0 (False) for variable i, space-separated.

**Grading:**
- Output "SAT" on the first line, then the assignment on the second line.
- Every clause must be satisfied by your assignment.
- Timeouts, crashes, wrong format, or unsatisfied clauses all score 0.

**Requirements:**
1. Read from stdin, write to stdout.
2. Handle up to 20M variables and 85M clauses.
3. Complete within 5 minutes.

Write complete, runnable Python code.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a logic puzzle made of true/false variables — where each rule says “at least one of these three things must be true” and all rules must hold simultaneously — find an assignment that satisfies every rule.

This is the classic 3-SAT problem, one of the first problems proven to be NP-complete. Every test instance has a hidden satisfying assignment the AI must discover. Subpasses increase the number of variables and clauses.

 0.0000
[20 vars, 85 clauses] 9/85 clauses unsatisfied, 0.06s

3-SAT Solver - 20 vars, 85 clauses

Approach: Looking at this problem, I need a robust SAT solver that can handle massive instances (up to 20M variables, 85M clauses). I'll implement a **CDCL (Conflict-Driven Clause Learning)** solver with **VSIDS branching**, **two-watched literals**, **restarts**, and **phase saving** — all the key components of a modern SAT solver.

View Python Code (13766 chars)
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
3-SAT Solver — CDCL with VSIDS, watched literals, restarts, phase saving.
Memory-optimized for up to 20M variables and 85M clauses.
"""

import sys
import os

# ─────────────────────────── I/O helpers ───────────────────────────

def fast_readints():
    """Read all ints from stdin efficiently, splitting by any whitespace."""
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
    return list(map(int, data.split()))


# ─────────────────────────── Solver ────────────────────────────────

class CDCLSolver:
    """
    CDCL SAT solver.
      • Trail:   assignment stack  (trail[i] = literal that propagated at level i)
      • Level:   trail_pos_to_level[i] = decision level of trail position i
      • Vidx:    var_to_trail_pos[v] = index in trail (or -1)
      • Reason:  var_reason[v] = clause index that implied v  (None if decision)
      • Watches: two-watched-literal scheme per clause (list of literals)
      • VSIDS:   heap-based activity branching
    """

    def __init__(self, nvars, clauses):
        self.nv = nvars
        self.nc = len(clauses)

        # ── assignments ──
        self.lit_val  = [0] * (2 * nvars + 2)   # 1 = true, -1 = false, 0 = undef
        self.trail    = []                       # literals assigned in order
        self.trail_lim = []                      # trail index at each new dl
        self.v2tp     = [-1] * (nvars + 1)      # var  → trail position
        self.vreason  = [None] * (nvars + 1)     # var  → clause idx or None
        self.dl       = 0                        # current decision level

        # ── clause storage (compact) ──
        self.c_lits = []
        self.c_sz   = []
        self.c_lrn  = []                         # True for learned clauses
        for c in clauses:
            self.c_lits.append(c)
            self.c_sz.append(len(c))
            self.c_lrn.append(False)

        # ── watched-literal occurrence lists ──
        #  watch_map[lit] = list of (clause_index, other_lit, third_lit)
        self.wm = {}
        for ci, cl in enumerate(clauses):
            assert cl[0] != 0
            lit0, lit1 = cl[0], cl[1]
            lit2 = cl[2] if len(cl) == 3 else None
            for w in (lit0, lit1):
                self.wm.setdefault(w, []).append((ci, lit1 if w == lit0 else lit0, lit2))

        # ── VSIDS branching heap ──
        self._heap   = list(range(1, nvars + 1))
        self._hpos   = [0] * (nvars + 1)
        self._act    = [0.0] * (nvars + 1)
        for i in range(nvars, 0, -1):
            self._hpos[i] = i - 1
        for i in range(nvars):
            self._heap[i] = i + 1
            self._hpos[i + 1] = i
        self._heap_n = nvars

        # bump variables that appear frequently
        for cl in clauses:
            for l in cl:
                v = abs(l)
                self._bump(v, 1.0)

        # ── phase saving ──
        self._phase = [False] * (nvars + 1)

        # ── restart / conflict counters ──
        self._nconfl = 0
        self._lim    = 100
        self._limfac = 1.5
        self._vc     = 0.0

        self._v_dec = [False] * (nvars + 1)

    # ──────── VSIDS heap ────────

    def _heap_siftup(self, i):
        a = self._act
        h = self._heap
        hp = self._hpos
        v = h[i]
        ai = a[v]
        n = self._heap_n
        while True:
            j = i
            l = 2 * i + 1
            r = l + 1
            if l < n and a[h[l]] > ai:
                j = l
            if r < n and a[h[r]] > a[h[j]]:
                j = r
            if j == i:
                break
            h[i] = h[j]; hp[h[j]] = i
            i = j
        h[i] = v; hp[v] = i

    def _heap_siftdown(self, i):
        a = self._act
        h = self._heap
        hp = self._hpos
        v = h[i]
        ai = a[v]
        while i > 0:
            p = (i - 1) >> 1
            if a[h[p]] >= ai:
                break
            h[i] = h[p]; hp[h[p]] = i
            i = p
        h[i] = v; hp[v] = i

    def _heap_pop(self):
        h = self._heap; hp = self._hpos
        top = h[0]
        last = h[self._heap_n - 1]
        self._heap_n -= 1
        if self._heap_n:
            h[0] = last; hp[last] = 0
            self._heap_siftup(0)
        hp[top] = -1
        return top

    def _bump(self, v, inc):
        self._act[v] += inc
        p = self._hpos[v]
        if p >= 0 and p < self._heap_n:
            self._heap_siftdown(p)

    def _pick(self):
        while self._heap_n:
            v = self._heap[0]
            if v <= self.nv and self.v2tp[v] == -1:
                self._heap_pop()
                return v
            self._heap_pop()
        return 0

    # ──────── enqueue (BCP) ────────

    def _enqueue(self, lit, reason=None):
        v = abs(lit)
        self.lit_val[lit]  = 1
        self.lit_val[-lit] = -1
        self.v2tp[v]  = len(self.trail)
        self.vreason[v] = reason
        self.trail.append(lit)
        self.trail_lim.append(self.dl)

    # ──────── propagate ────────

    def _propagate(self):
        """
        Boolean constraint propagation using two-watched literals.
        Returns (ok, clause_index)  — clause_index is the conflicting clause,
        or None when no conflict.
        """
        lv = self.lit_val
        wm = self.wm
        csz = self.c_sz
        cl = self.c_lits
        trail = self.trail
        q = len(trail)
        dl = self.dl
        enf = self._enqueue

        while q < len(trail):
            unit_lit = trail[q]
            q += 1
            falsified_lit = -unit_lit

            wl = wm.get(falsified_lit)
            if wl is None:
                continue

            # swap out the watch list so we can modify it in-place
            wm[falsified_lit] = []
            j = 0
            ok = True
            for ci, other, third in wl:
                # other already true  →  clause satisfied, keep watch
                if lv[other] == 1:
                    wm[falsified_lit].append((ci, other, third))
                    j += 1
                    continue

                # try to find a replacement literal for the falsified position
                found = False
                if third is not None:
                    if lv[third] != -1:
                        found = True
                        # watch falsified_lit → third
                        wm.setdefault(third, []).append((ci, other, falsified_lit))
                        j += 1
                        continue
                else:
                    # binary clause — no replacement possible
                    pass

                if not found:
                    # falsified_lit must stay watched
                    # re-evaluate: other may have become true since start
                    if lv[other] == 1:
                        wm[falsified_lit].append((ci, other, third))
                        j += 1
                        continue

                    if lv[other] == 0:
                        # other is undef  →  unit propagation
                        enf(other, ci)
                        wm[falsified_lit].append((ci, other, third))
                        j += 1
                    else:
                        # conflict: other is false and third is false
                        wm[falsified_lit].append((ci, other, third))
                        # put remaining watches back
                        for k in range(j + 1, len(wl)):
                            wm[falsified_lit].append(wl[k])
                        return (False, ci)
                    # continue (trail may have grown, next iteration will process it)

            # trim to j
            if j < len(wm[falsified_lit]):
                del wm[falsified_lit][j:]

        return (True, None)

    # ──────── analyze ────────

    def _analyze(self, confl):
        """
        Build a learned clause by 1-UIP.
        Returns (learned_clause, asserting_literal, backtrack_level).
        """
        seen = [False] * (self.nv + 1)
        counter = 0
        idx = len(self.trail) - 1
        cl = self.c_lits
        p = None
        learned = []
        lv = self.lit_val
        tr = self.trail
        vtp = self.v2tp
        vr = self.vreason
        tlim = self.trail_lim
        self._nconfl += 1
        bump = self._bump
        vc = self._vc

        while counter > 1 or (p is not None and self.dl == tlim[vtp[abs(p)]]):
            # resolve
            for lit in cl[confl]:
                v = abs(lit)
                if seen[v]:
                    continue
                seen[v] = True
                pvtp = vtp[v]
                if pvtp >= 0 and tlim[pvtp] == self.dl:
                    counter += 1
                elif pvtp >= 0:
                    learned.append(-lit)          # store falsified
                    bump(v, vc)
            # find next literal on trail at current dl with a reason
            while idx >= 0:
                v = abs(tr[idx])
                idx -= 1
                if seen[v] and tlim[vtp[v]] == self.dl:
                    break
            p = tr[vtp[v]]
            seen[v] = False
            counter -= 1
            confl = vr[v]

        learned.append(-p)
        # learned now contains falsified lits + one dl literal (the asserting one)
        # sort: dl-literal first
        v_ap = abs(p)
        i = 0
        for k in range(len(learned)):
            if abs(learned[k]) == v_ap:
                i = k; break
        learned[0], learned[i] = learned[i], learned[0]

        bt = 0
        for k in range(1, len(learned)):
            vv = abs(learned[k])
            bt = max(bt, tlim[vtp[vv]])

        return learned, p, bt

    # ──────── backtrack ────────

    def _backtrack(self, bt):
        lv = self.lit_val
        vtp = self.v2tp
        vr = self.vreason
        tlim = self.trail_lim
        trail = self.trail
        ph = self._phase
        vd = self._v_dec
        hp = self._hpos
        hn = self._heap_n
        a = self._act
        selfdl = self.dl

        while len(trail) > 0 and tlim[-1] > bt:
            p = trail.pop()
            tlim.pop()
            v = abs(p)
            lv[p] = 0; lv[-p] = 0
            vtp[v] = -1; vr[v] = None
            ph[v] = (p > 0)
            # reinsert into heap
            if hp[v] < 0:
                hp[v] = hn; hn += 1
                if hn > len(self._heap):
                    self._heap.append(v)
                else:
                    self._heap[hn - 1] = v
                self._heap_siftdown(hn - 1)
            vd[v] = False
        self._heap_n = hn
        self.dl = bt

    # ──────── add learned clause ────────

    def _addlearned(self, learned):
        n = len(learned)
        idx = len(self.c_lits)
        self.c_lits.append(learned)
        self.c_sz.append(n)
        self.c_lrn.append(True)

        # watch the asserting literal and one other
        w0 = learned[0]
        w1 = learned[1] if n > 1 else -learned[0]   # shouldn't happen n=1

        t = learned[2] if n > 2 else None
        self.wm.setdefault(w0, []).append((idx, w1, t))
        if n > 1:
            self.wm.setdefault(w1, []).append((idx, w0, t))

        # bump
        vc = self._vc
        for l in learned:
            self._bump(abs(l), vc)

        # decay
        self._vc = max(1e-6, self._vc * 0.95)

    # ──────── main solve ────────

    def solve(self):
        # initial propagation (handles unit and binary clauses)
        for ci, cl in enumerate(self.c_lits):
            if self.c_sz[ci] == 1:
                l = cl[0]
                if self.lit_val[-l] == 1:
                    return False
                if self.lit_val[l] == 0:
                    self._enqueue(l, ci)

        ok, _ = self._propagate()
        if not ok:
            return False

        while True:
            # restart check
            if self._nconfl >= self._lim:
                self._lim = int(self._lim * self._limfac)
                if self._lim > 300000:
                    self._limfac = 1.1
                self._backtrack(0)

            v = self._pick()
            if v == 0:
                return True

            self.dl += 1
            lit = v if self._phase[v] else -v
            self._v_dec[v] = True
            self._enqueue(lit, None)

            while True:
                ok, confl = self._propagate()
                if ok:
                    break
                if self.dl == 0:
                    return False
                learned, p, bt = self._analyze(confl)
                self._addlearned(learned)
                self._backtrack(bt)
                self._enqueue(p, len(self.c_lits) - 1)

    def model(self):
        vtp = self.v2tp
        tr = self.trail
        ph = self._phase
        r = []
        for v in range(1, self.nv + 1):
            if vtp[v] >= 0:
                r.append(1 if tr[vtp[v]] > 0 else 0)
            else:
                r.append(1 if ph[v] else 0)
        return r


# ─────────────────────────── main ──────────────────────────────────

def main():
    data = fast_readints()
    if not data:
        return
    nv = data[0]
    nc = data[1]

    # parse clauses
    pos = 2
    clauses = []
    for _ in range(nc):
        a = data[pos]; b = data[pos+1]; c = data[pos+2]; pos += 3
        # unit clause?
        if a != 0 and (b == 0 or c == 0):
            if b == 0:
                clauses.append([a])
            else:
                clauses.append([a, b])
        else:
            clauses.append([a, b, c])

    solver = CDCLSolver(nv, clauses)
    if solver.solve():
        sys.stdout.write("SAT\n")
        sys.stdout.write(" ".join(map(str, solver.model())) + "\n")
    else:
        sys.stdout.write("UNSAT\n")
        sys.stdout.flush()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Test 31: Subset Sum (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Subset Sum problem.

Your Rust subset sum solver will be tested with instances ranging from simple to very
complex sets. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL problem complexities.

Given a set of integers and a target value, determine if there exists a subset
that sums to exactly the target value. Simple sets (<100 numbers) you can probably
brute force, however this code will also need to run on sets with millions of
numbers. This is NP-Complete and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances:
additionally; threading, memory optimization, vectorization and parallelization are all
strongly suggested.

**Input format (stdin, space seperated decimal integers):**
```
num_numbers target
num1 num2 ... numN
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
YES or NO
[index1 index2 ... indexK] (only if YES, 0-indexed indices of subset elements)
```

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect set complexity and choose appropriate algorithm
- Use efficient data structures: bitsets, hash sets for DP
- Implement adaptive quality vs speed tradeoffs
- For very large sets, focus on approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty set, zero target, all numbers add to target.
- Use fast I/O as the input size can approach 1gb.

**Success Criteria:**
- Correctly determine if subset sum exists
- If YES, provide valid subset indices
- Complete within time limit

**Failure Criteria:**
- Incorrect subset sum determination
- Invalid subset indices

**Requirements:**

2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable set sizes and target values
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying subset sum complexities efficiently

so ensure that you write warning-free code.

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt

View chain of thought

Given a list of numbers and a target total, find a subset of those numbers that adds up to exactly the target. It sounds simple, but with large lists the number of possible subsets is astronomical.

This is a classic NP-hard problem. The test uses planted instances with a known solution for strict pass/fail grading. Subpasses increase the list size and target magnitude.

error[E0425]: cannot find value `target` in this scope

|
471 | return brute_force_find(&used_values, target

Subset Sum - 20 numbers, small values
