#![allow(dead_code)]

use std::io::{self, Write, BufWriter};
use std::time::Instant;

fn main() {
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());
    let start = Instant::now();

    let input = read_stdin();
    let mut tokens = input.split_ascii_whitespace();

    let num_numbers: usize = tokens.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let target: i128 = tokens.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    if num_numbers == 0 {
        if target == 0 {
            writeln!(out, "YES").unwrap();
            writeln!(out, "[]").unwrap();
        } else {
            writeln!(out, "NO").unwrap();
        }
        return;
    }

    let numbers: Vec<i128> = tokens.take(num_numbers).map(|t| t.parse().unwrap()).collect();

    let has_neg = numbers.iter().any(|&x| x < 0);
    let min_val = *numbers.iter().min().unwrap();
    let max_val = *numbers.iter().max().unwrap();

    let result = if has_neg {
        solve_with_negatives(&numbers, target, min_val, max_val)
    } else {
        solve_nonnegative(&numbers, target)
    };

    match result {
        Some(indices) => {
            let idxs: Vec<usize> = indices.into_iter().collect();
            let sum: i128 = idxs.iter().map(|&i| numbers[i]).sum();
            assert_eq!(sum, target, "VERIFICATION FAILED: sum={} target={}", sum, target);
            writeln!(out, "YES").unwrap();
            writeln!(out, "{}", fmt_indices(&idxs)).unwrap();
        }
        None => {
            writeln!(out, "NO").unwrap();
        }
    }

    eprintln!("Time: {:?}", start.elapsed());
}

fn fmt_indices(idxs: &[usize]) -> String {
    let mut s = String::with_capacity(idxs.len() * 8);
    s.push('[');
    for (i, &idx) in idxs.iter().enumerate() {
        if i > 0 {
            s.push(' ');
        }
        s.push_str(&idx.to_string());
    }
    s.push(']');
    s
}

fn read_stdin() -> Vec<u8> {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut buf = Vec::new();
    stdin.lock().read_to_end(&mut buf).unwrap();
    buf
}

fn solve_nonnegative(numbers: &[i128], target: i128) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    if target == 0 {
        return Some(vec![]);
    }
    if target < 0 {
        return None;
    }

    let n = numbers.len();
    let target = target as u128;

    // Filter out zeros, track their count
    let nonzero: Vec<(usize, i128)> = numbers
        .iter()
        .enumerate()
        .filter(|(_, &v)| v != 0)
        .map(|(i, v)| (i, v))
        .collect();
    let nnz = nonzero.len();
    let mut zero_indices: Vec<usize> = numbers
        .iter()
        .enumerate()
        .filter(|(_, &v)| v == 0)
        .map(|(i, _)| i)
        .collect();

    if nnz == 0 {
        return if target == 0 { Some(zero_indices) } else { None };
    }

    let total_sum: u128 = nonzero.iter().map(|&(_, v)| v as u128).sum();
    if total_sum < target {
        return None;
    }

    // Tiny sets: brute force via bitset enumeration
    if nnz <= 30 {
        let result = brute_force_nn(&nonzero, target);
        if let Some(mut idxs) = result {
            idxs.extend(zero_indices.drain(..));
            return Some(idxs);
        }
        return None;
    }

    // Filter out values larger than target
    let filtered: Vec<(usize, u128)> = nonzero
        .into_iter()
        .filter(|&(_, v)| v as u128 <= target)
        .map(|(i, v)| (i, v as u128))
        .collect();

    let total_filtered: u128 = filtered.iter().map(|&(_, v)| v).sum();
    if total_filtered < target {
        return None;
    }

    // Use only up to 1M values (sorted by size for better pruning)
    let mut use_vals = filtered;
    if use_vals.len() > 1_000_000 {
        use_vals.sort_by_key(|&(_, v)| v);
        use_vals.truncate(1_000_000);
    }
    let n_use = use_vals.len();

    // Compute working range: shrink from both ends based on prefix sums
    let prefix: Vec<u128> = std::iter::once(0u128)
        .chain(use_vals.iter().scan(0u128, |acc, &(_, v)| {
            *acc += v;
            Some(*acc)
        }))
        .collect();
    let suffix: Vec<u128> = std::iter::once(0u128)
        .chain(use_vals.iter().rev().scan(0u128, |acc, &(_, v)| {
            *acc += v;
            Some(*acc)
        }))
        .collect();
    let suffix: Vec<u128> = suffix.into_iter().rev().collect();

    let mut lo = 0u128;
    let mut hi = target;
    for i in 0..n_use {
        if prefix[i] > target {
            break;
        }
        let rem = target - prefix[i];
        if rem <= suffix[i + 1] {
            lo = prefix[i];
            break;
        }
    }
    for i in (0..n_use).rev() {
        if suffix[i] > target {
            continue;
        }
        let rem = target - suffix[i];
        if rem <= prefix[i] {
            hi = suffix[i];
            break;
        }
    }

    let dp_size = (hi - lo) as usize + 1;

    // Bitset DP if range is feasible (up to ~16 billion bits = ~2GB)
    if dp_size <= 16_000_000_000usize {
        let result = bitset_dp(&use_vals, lo, hi, target, dp_size);
        if let Some(mut idxs) = result {
            idxs.extend(zero_indices.drain(..));
            return Some(idxs);
        }
        return None;
    }

    // Range too large for bitset: try randomized sampling
    eprintln!(
        "Bitset too large ({} bits), falling back to randomized sampling",
        dp_size
    );
    let result = randomized_subset_sum(&use_vals, target, 5_000_000);
    if let Some(mut idxs) = result {
        idxs.extend(zero_indices.drain(..));
        return Some(idxs);
    }
    None
}

fn brute_force_nn(nonzero: &[(usize, i128)], target: u128) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    let n = nonzero.len();
    if n <= 25 {
        for mask in 0u32..(1u32 << n) {
            let mut sum = 0u128;
            for i in 0..n {
                if (mask >> i) & 1 == 1 {
                    sum += nonzero[i].1 as u128;
                }
            }
            if sum == target {
                let idxs: Vec<usize> = (0..n)
                    .filter(|&i| (mask >> i) & 1 == 1)
                    .map(|i| nonzero[i].0)
                    .collect();
                return Some(idxs);
            }
        }
    } else {
        for mask in 0u64..(1u64 << n) {
            let mut sum = 0u128;
            for i in 0..n {
                if (mask >> i) & 1 == 1 {
                    sum += nonzero[i].1 as u128;
                }
            }
            if sum == target {
                let idxs: Vec<usize> = (0..n)
                    .filter(|&i| (mask >> i) & 1 == 1)
                    .map(|i| nonzero[i].0)
                    .collect();
                return Some(idxs);
            }
        }
    }
    None
}

fn bitset_dp(
    use_vals: &[(usize, u128)],
    lo: u128,
    hi: u128,
    target: u128,
    dp_size: usize,
) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    let n = use_vals.len();
    let target_offset = target - lo;

    // dp: bit j set = sum (lo + j) is reachable
    let mut dp: Vec<u64> = vec![0u64; (dp_size + 63) / 64];
    dp[0] = 1; // lo is reachable (empty subset)

    let target_block = (target_offset / 64) as usize;
    let target_bit = (target_offset % 64) as u32;

    let found_block = target_block;
    let found_bit = target_bit;

    // Count usable upper blocks for parallel search
    let max_sum = hi - lo;
    let num_full_blocks = dp.len();
    let usable_upper = ((n as u64) * 64).min(num_full_blocks as u64) as usize;
    let usable_lower = num_full_blocks.saturating_sub(usable_upper);

    let mut found_at_step = n; // default: not found early

    // Process each value
    for val_idx in 0..n {
        let val = use_vals[val_idx].1;
        let shift_blocks = (val / 64) as usize;
        let shift_bits = (val % 64) as u32;

        // Check if target is already reached
        if dp[target_block] & (1u64 << target_bit) != 0 {
            found_at_step = val_idx;
            break;
        }

        // Core bitset update: dp |= dp << val
        if shift_bits == 0 {
            // Pure block shift: can be parallelized
            if usable_upper > 0 && usable_lower > 0 {
                let upper_len = usable_upper.min(num_full_blocks);
                let lower_len = usable_lower.min(num_full_blocks);
                // Upper half: dp[i] |= dp[i - shift_blocks] for i in shift_blocks..upper_end
                let upper_end = shift_blocks + lower_len;
                if shift_blocks < upper_end && upper_end <= num_full_blocks {
                    let block_shift = shift_blocks;
                    let dp_ptr = dp.as_mut_ptr();
                    let dp_len = num_full_blocks;
                    let range_start = block_shift;
                    let range_end = upper_end;
                    // SAFETY: indices are in bounds
                    unsafe {
                        for i in range_start..range_end {
                            let src = *dp_ptr.add(i - block_shift);
                            *dp_ptr.add(i) |= src;
                        }
                    }
                }
                // Lower half: sequential
                for i in upper_end..num_full_blocks {
                    dp[i] |= dp[i - shift_blocks];
                }
            } else {
                // Fully sequential
                for i in shift_blocks..num_full_blocks {
                    dp[i] |= dp[i - shift_blocks];
                }
            }
        } else {
            // Bit shift with carry: must be sequential
            for i in shift_blocks..num_full_blocks {
                let (low, high) = shift_u128(dp[i - shift_blocks], shift_bits);
                dp[i] |= low;
                if i + shift_blocks + 1 < num_full_blocks {
                    dp[i + shift_blocks + 1] |= high;
                }
            }
        }

        // Check target after this value
        if dp[target_block] & (1u64 << target_bit) != 0 {
            found_at_step = val_idx + 1;
            break;
        }
    }

    // Final check
    if dp[target_block] & (1u64 << found_bit) == 0 {
        return None;
    }

    // Backtrack to find the subset
    backtrack_bitset(use_vals, lo, dp_size, found_at_step, target_block, found_bit)
}

fn shift_u128(val: u64, shift: u32) -> (u64, u64) {
    let wide = val as u128;
    let result = wide << shift;
    (result as u64, (result >> 64) as u64)
}

fn backtrack_bitset(
    use_vals: &[(usize, u128)],
    lo: u128,
    dp_size: usize,
    found_at_step: usize,
    target_block: usize,
    target_bit: u32,
) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    // Forward pass: track which values are definitely NOT in the solution
    let mut mask: Vec<u64> = vec![0u64; (dp_size + 63) / 64];

    let mut cur_block = target_block;
    let mut cur_bit = target_bit;
    let num_steps = use_vals.len();

    for val_idx in (0..num_steps).reverse() {
        let val = use_vals[val_idx].1;

        // Check if removing val still reaches cur
        if val <= cur_block as u128 * 64 + cur_bit as u128 {
            let rem_block = ((cur_block as u128 * 64 + cur_bit as u128 - val) / 64) as usize;
            let rem_bit = ((cur_block as u128 * 64 + cur_bit as u128 - val) % 64) as u32;
            if rem_block < mask.len() && mask[rem_block] & (1u64 << rem_bit) != 0 {
                // cur is reachable without val → val is NOT in the subset
            }
        }

        // Compute shifted mask for val
        let shift_blocks = (val / 64) as usize;
        let shift_bits = (val % 64) as u32;

        if shift_bits == 0 {
            for i in (shift_blocks..mask.len()).rev() {
                mask[i] |= mask[i - shift_blocks];
            }
        } else {
            for i in (shift_blocks..mask.len()).rev() {
                let (low, high) = shift_u128(mask[i - shift_blocks], shift_bits);
                mask[i] |= low;
                if i + shift_blocks + 1 < mask.len() {
                    mask[i + shift_blocks + 1] |= high;
                }
            }
        }

        // Set the reachable sums using only values up to val_idx
        mask[cur_block] |= 1u64 << cur_bit;
    }

    // mask now shows which sums are reachable using any subset of values 0..num_steps
    // BUT we need to be more careful: find the specific subset

    // Alternative: use a proper DP table for backtracking
    // Build a compressed DP: for each step, store which NEW sums were added
    // Actually, let's just do a targeted search from found values

    // Collect candidate values: those that could be in the subset
    let mut candidates: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();
    let mut cur_sum = target_block as u128 * 64 + cur_bit as u128 + lo;

    // Forward: find which values we need
    // For each value from 0..found_at_step, check if removing it keeps us on a valid path
    // We need a "used only values up to i" DP

    // Let's rebuild DP more carefully for backtracking
    // dp_forward[i] = reachable sums using only values[0..i]
    // dp_backward[i] = reachable sums using only values[i..n]

    // For efficiency, we'll store the DP for the first found_at_step values
    // and backtrack through them

    // Simpler approach: greedy backtracking using the mask
    // For each value (in reverse), if target - val is in the mask for earlier values, try removing it

    // Actually, let's do a proper recursive/iterative backtracking
    // We know found_at_step values can form the target
    // Try to find a minimal subset among them

    // Build forward DP table for backtracking
    let steps = found_at_step.min(use_vals.len());
    let reachable = vec![0u64; (dp_size + 63) / 64];
    // We'll track cumulative DP and for each value, check if target - val was reachable before

    // Build prefix DP states
    let mut prefix_dp: Vec<u64> = vec![0u64; (dp_size + 63) / 64];
    prefix_dp[0] = 1; // lo reachable

    let mut value_used = vec![false; steps];

    let mut cur = target_block as u128 * 64 + cur_bit as u128 + lo;

    for val_idx in (0..steps).rev() {
        let val = use_vals[val_idx].1;
        if val <= cur {
            let rem = cur - val;
            let rem_idx = (rem - lo) as usize;
            let rem_block = rem_idx / 64;
            let rem_bit = rem_idx % 64;
            // Check if rem was reachable using only values 0..val_idx
            // We need the DP state at step val_idx
            // Since we don't store all states, use mask heuristic
            // Actually, let's check the cumulative mask
            // The mask after step val_idx includes all sums reachable with values 0..=val_idx
            // But we need sums reachable with values 0..val_idx-1
            // This is tricky without storing all states

            // Let's use a different approach: rebuild DP and track
            value_used[val_idx] = true;
            cur = rem;
        }
    }

    // This greedy approach may not work correctly. Let's do a proper backtracking
    // using a fresh DP table for the candidate values.

    // Collect used values (reverse of value_used to get candidates)
    let mut used_values: Vec<(usize, u128)> = Vec::new();
    for val_idx in 0..steps {
        if value_used[val_idx] {
            used_values.push(use_vals[val_idx]);
        }
    }

    if used_values.len() <= 40 {
        // Brute force among candidates
        return brute_force_find(&used_values, target - lo);
    }

    // For larger candidate sets, redo bitset with tracking
    backtrack_bitset_full(used_values, target - lo)
}

fn brute_force_find(candidates: &[(usize, u128)], target: u128) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    let n = candidates.len();
    if n <= 25 {
        for mask in 0u32..(1u32 << n) {
            let mut sum = 0u128;
            for i in 0..n {
                if (mask >> i) & 1 == 1 {
                    sum += candidates[i].1;
                }
            }
            if sum == target {
                return Some((0..n).filter(|&i| (mask >> i) & 1 == 1).map(|i| candidates[i].0).collect());
            }
        }
    } else {
        for mask in 0u64..(1u64 << n) {
            let mut sum = 0u128;
            for i in 0..n {
                if (mask >> i) & 1 == 1 {
                    sum += candidates[i].1;
                }
            }
            if sum == target {
                return Some((0..n).filter(|&i| (mask >> i) & 1 == 1).map(|i| candidates[i].0).collect());
            }
        }
    }
    None
}

fn backtrack_bitset_full(candidates: Vec<(usize, u128)>, target: u128) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    let n = candidates.len();
    let total: u128 = candidates.iter().map(|&(_, v)| v).sum();

    // Build prefix sums
    let mut prefix = vec![0u128; n + 1];
    for i in 0..n {
        prefix[i + 1] = prefix[i] + candidates[i].1;
    }

    // Recursive backtracking with pruning
    let mut result = Vec::new();
    let found = backtrack_search(
        &candidates,
        &prefix,
        0,
        target,
        &mut result,
    );
    if found {
        Some(result)
    } else {
        None
    }
}

fn backtrack_search(
    candidates: &[(usize, u128)],
    prefix: &[u128],
    start: usize,
    remaining: u128,
    result: &mut Vec<usize>,
) -> bool {
    if remaining == 0 {
        return true;
    }
    if start >= candidates.len() {
        return false;
    }

    let suffix_sum = prefix[prefix.len() - 1] - prefix[start];
    if suffix_sum < remaining {
        return false;
    }
    if candidates[start].1 > remaining && candidates.iter().skip(start).all(|&(_, v)| v >= remaining) {
        // All remaining values too large
        return false;
    }

    for i in start..candidates.len() {
        let val = candidates[i].1;
        if val > remaining {
            continue;
        }
        let new_rem = remaining - val;
        // Check if new_rem is achievable with remaining values
        let new_suffix = prefix[prefix.len() - 1] - prefix[i + 1];
        if new_suffix < new_rem {
            continue;
        }
        result.push(candidates[i].0);
        if backtrack_search(candidates, prefix, i + 1, new_rem, result) {
            return true;
        }
        result.pop();
    }
    false
}

fn solve_with_negatives(
    numbers: &[i128],
    target: i128,
    min_val: i128,
    max_val: i128,
) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    if numbers.len() == 0 {
        return if target == 0 { Some(vec![]) } else { None };
    }

    // If all numbers are negative, negate everything
    let all_negative = max_val < 0;
    if all_negative {
        let negated: Vec<i128> = numbers.iter().map(|&x| -x).collect();
        let neg_target = -target;
        let result = solve_nonnegative(&negated, neg_target)?;
        Some(result)
    } else if min_val >= 0 {
        return solve_nonnegative(numbers, target);
    }

    let n = numbers.len();

    // Split into positives and negatives
    let mut pos: Vec<(usize, i128)> = Vec::new();
    let mut neg: Vec<(usize, i128)> = Vec::new();
    let mut zeros: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();

    for (i, &v) in numbers.iter().enumerate() {
        if v > 0 {
            pos.push((i, v));
        } else if v < 0 {
            neg.push((i, -v)); // store as positive for easier computation
        } else {
            zeros.push(i);
        }
    }

    pos.sort_by_key(|&(_, v)| v);
    neg.sort_by_key(|&(_, v)| v);

    // Meet-in-the-middle for mixed sign (only feasible for small n)
    if n <= 50 {
        let half = n / 2;

        // Enumerate all subsets of first half
        let mut first_half = Vec::new();
        for mask in 0u64..(1u64 << half) {
            let mut sum: i128 = 0;
            for i in 0..half {
                if (mask >> i) & 1 == 1 {
                    sum += numbers[i];
                }
            }
            first_half.push((sum, mask));
        }

        // Sort first half by sum
        first_half.sort_by_key(|&(s, _)| s);

        // Enumerate second half, binary search for complement
        let second_half_n = n - half;
        for mask in 0u64..(1u64 << second_half_n) {
            let mut sum: i128 = 0;
            for i in 0..second_half_n {
                if (mask >> i) & 1 == 1 {
                    sum += numbers[half + i];
                }
            }
            let needed = target - sum;
            match first_half.binary_search_by_key(&needed, |&(s, _)| s) {
                Ok(idx) => {
                    let (_, mask1) = first_half[idx];
                    let mut indices = Vec::new();
                    for i in 0..half {
                        if (mask1 >> i) & 1 == 1 {
                            indices.push(i);
                        }
                    }
                    for i in 0..second_half_n {
                        if (mask >> i) & 1 == 1 {
                            indices.push(half + i);
                        }
                    }
                    indices.extend(zeros.iter().copied());
                    return Some(indices);
                }
                Err(_) => continue,
            }
        }
        return None;
    }

    // Larger set with negatives: try offset bitset DP
    let pos_sum: i128 = pos.iter().map(|&(_, v)| v).sum();
    let neg_sum: i128 = neg.iter().map(|&(_, v)| v).sum();

    // Convert to non-negative problem: shift everything by neg_sum
    // Each element becomes (value + neg_sum)
    // New target = target + k * neg_sum for some k (number of negative elements used)
    // This isn't directly applicable, but we can bound the range

    // Better: use offset bitset
    // dp[s] = reachable sum s, where s ranges from neg_sum to pos_sum
    let range_min = neg_sum; // most negative achievable sum
    let range_max = pos_sum; // most positive achievable sum

    if target < range_min || target > range_max {
        return None;
    }

    let offset = range_min;
    let range = (range_max - range_min) as usize;
    let target_idx = (target - offset) as usize;

    if range > 16_000_000_000usize {
        // Too large for bitset, try randomized
        eprintln!("Negative bitset range too large: {}", range);
        let pos_only_target = target;
        // Try: find subset of positives = target, or
        // find subset of positives + some negatives = target
        // Random sampling
        return randomized_mixed(&pos, &neg, &zeros, target);
    }

    // Bitset DP with offset
    let dp_size = range + 1;
    let mut dp: Vec<u64> = vec![0u64; (dp_size + 63) / 64];
    let zero_idx = (0i128 - offset) as usize;
    let zero_block = zero_idx / 64;
    let zero_bit = zero_idx % 64;
    dp[zero_block] |= 1u64 << zero_bit;

    let target_block = target_idx / 64;
    let target_bit = target_idx % 64;

    // Process positive values (forward shift)
    for &(_, val) in &pos {
        if dp[target_block] & (1u64 << target_bit) != 0 {
            break;
        }
        let val = val as u128;
        let shift_blocks = (val / 64) as usize;
        let shift_bits = (val % 64) as u32;
        if shift_bits == 0 {
            for i in (shift_blocks..dp.len()).rev() {
                dp[i] |= dp[i - shift_blocks];
            }
        } else {
            for i in (shift_blocks..dp.len()).rev() {
                let (low, high) = shift_u128(dp[i - shift_blocks], shift_bits);
                dp[i] |= low;
                if i + shift_blocks + 1 < dp.len() {
                    dp[i + shift_blocks + 1] |= high;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // Process negative values (backward shift)
    for &(_, val) in &neg {
        if dp[target_block] & (1u64 << target_bit) != 0 {
            break;
        }
        let val = val as u128;
        let shift_blocks = (val / 64) as usize;
        let shift_bits = (val % 64) as u32;
        if shift_bits == 0 {
            for i in 0..(dp.len() - shift_blocks) {
                dp[i] |= dp[i + shift_blocks];
            }
        } else {
            // Right shift by val: for each block, shift right and OR carry from higher block
            for i in 0..(dp.len().saturating_sub(shift_blocks)) {
                if i + shift_blocks + 1 < dp.len() {
                    let high_bits = dp[i + shift_blocks + 1] << (64 - shift_bits);
                    dp[i] |= (dp[i + shift_blocks] >> shift_bits) | high_bits;
                } else {
                    dp[i] |= dp[i + shift_blocks] >> shift_bits;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    if dp[target_block] & (1u64 << target_bit) == 0 {
        return None;
    }

    // Backtrack for mixed signs
    // Rebuild DP step by step and track which values are used
    let all_vals: Vec<(usize, i128, bool)> = pos
        .iter()
        .map(|&(i, v)| (i, v, true))
        .chain(neg.iter().map(|&(i, v)| (i, -(v as i128), false)))
        .collect();
    let n_all = all_vals.len();

    // Build prefix DP states
    let mut states: Vec<Vec<u64>> = Vec::with_capacity(n_all + 1);
    states.push(dp.clone()); // final state

    // Reverse the DP to find which values contribute
    // We need to rebuild forward and check at each step
    // Actually, let's rebuild forward and store states at key points

    // Simpler: greedy backtracking
    let mut current_dp = vec![0u64; (dp_size + 63) / 64];
    current_dp[zero_block] |= 1u64 << zero_bit;

    let mut result_indices: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();
    let mut cur_target = target_idx;

    for val_idx in 0..n_all {
        let (orig_idx, val, is_pos) = all_vals[val_idx];
        let abs_val = val.abs() as u128;

        // Check if cur_target is already reachable without this value
        if current_dp[cur_target / 64] & (1u64 << (cur_target % 64)) != 0 {
            // Already reachable, skip this value
            continue;
        }

        // Try including this value
        if is_pos {
            if cur_target >= abs_val as usize {
                let rem = cur_target - abs_val as usize;
                if current_dp[rem / 64] & (1u64 << (rem % 64)) != 0 {
                    result_indices.push(orig_idx);
                    cur_target = rem;
                }
            }
        } else {
            let rem = cur_target + abs_val as usize;
            if rem < dp_size && current_dp[rem / 64] & (1u64 << (rem % 64)) != 0 {
                result_indices.push(orig_idx);
                cur_target = rem;
            }
        }

        // Update current_dp with this value
        if is_pos {
            let shift_blocks = (abs_val / 64) as usize;
            let shift_bits = (abs_val % 64) as u32;
            if shift_bits == 0 {
                for i in (shift_blocks..current_dp.len()).rev() {
                    current_dp[i] |= current_dp[i - shift_blocks];
                }
            } else {
                for i in (shift_blocks..current_dp.len()).rev() {
                    let (low, high) = shift_u128(current_dp[i - shift_blocks], shift_bits);
                    current_dp[i] |= low;
                    if i + shift_blocks + 1 < current_dp.len() {
                        current_dp[i + shift_blocks + 1] |= high;
                    }
                }
            }
        } else {
            let shift_blocks = (abs_val / 64) as usize;
            let shift_bits = (abs_val % 64) as u32;
            if shift_bits == 0 {
                for i in 0..(current_dp.len() - shift_blocks) {
                    current_dp[i] |= current_dp[i + shift_blocks];
                }
            } else {
                for i in 0..(current_dp.len().saturating_sub(shift_blocks)) {
                    if i + shift_blocks + 1 < current_dp.len() {
                        let high_bits = current_dp[i + shift_blocks + 1] << (64 - shift_bits);
                        current_dp[i] |= (current_dp[i + shift_blocks] >> shift_bits) | high_bits;
                    } else {
                        current_dp[i] |= current_dp[i + shift_blocks] >> shift_bits;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        if cur_target == zero_idx {
            break;
        }
    }

    if cur_target != zero_idx {
        // Greedy failed, try full backtracking on a reduced set
        // This shouldn't happen often
        result_indices.clear();
        // Fall back to randomized
        return randomized_mixed(&pos, &neg, &zeros, target);
    }

    result_indices.extend(zeros.iter().copied());
    Some(result_indices)
}

fn randomized_mixed(
    pos: &[(usize, i128)],
    neg: &[(usize, i128)],
    zeros: &[usize],
    target: i128,
) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    use std::collections::HashSet;

    let mut rng: u64 = 123456789;
    let next_rand = |rng: &mut u64| -> u64 {
        *rng = rng.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);
        *rng
    };

    let pos_sum: i128 = pos.iter().map(|&(_, v)| v).sum();

    for _ in 0..20_000_000 {
        // Random subset of positives
        let mut pos_sum_selected: i128 = 0;
        let mut selected_pos: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();
        for &(idx, val) in pos {
            if next_rand(&mut rng) % 4 == 0 {
                pos_sum_selected += val;
                selected_pos.push(idx);
            }
        }

        let needed_neg = pos_sum_selected - target;
        if needed_neg <= 0 {
            if needed_neg == 0 {
                let mut result = selected_pos;
                result.extend(zeros.iter().copied());
                return Some(result);
            }
            continue;
        }

        // Try to find subset of negatives summing to needed_neg
        // Negatives stored as positive values
        let neg_needed = needed_neg;
        let mut neg_sum: i128 = 0;
        let mut selected_neg: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();
        for &(idx, val) in neg {
            if next_rand(&mut rng) % 4 == 0 && neg_sum + val <= neg_needed {
                neg_sum += val;
                selected_neg.push(idx);
            }
        }

        if neg_sum == neg_needed {
            let mut result = selected_pos;
            result.extend(selected_neg);
            result.extend(zeros.iter().copied());
            return Some(result);
        }
    }

    None
}

fn randomized_subset_sum(
    vals: &[(usize, u128)],
    target: u128,
    iterations: usize,
) -> Option<Vec<usize>> {
    let mut rng: u64 = 987654321;
    let next_rand = |rng: &mut u64| -> u64 {
        *rng = rng.wrapping_mul(6364136223846793005).wrapping_add(1442695040888963407);
        *rng
    };

    // Try random inclusion with varying density
    for iter in 0..iterations {
        let density = match iter % 5 {
            0 => 1,
            1 => 2,
            2 => 3,
            3 => 5,
            _ => 10,
        };

        let mut sum: u128 = 0;
        let mut selected: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();
        for &(idx, val) in vals {
            if next_rand(&mut rng) % density == 0 {
                if sum + val <= target {
                    sum += val;
                    selected.push(idx);
                }
            }
        }

        if sum == target {
            return Some(selected);
        }

        // Try adding remaining values one by one
        if sum < target {
            for &(idx, val) in vals {
                if sum + val <= target {
                    sum += val;
                    selected.push(idx);
                    if sum == target {
                        return Some(selected);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    None
}

Test 32: Steiner Tree (C++) Score: 0.00 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the Steiner Tree problem (NP-Hard).

**Problem:**
Given a weighted undirected graph and a set of terminal vertices, find a minimum-weight
tree that connects all terminals. The tree may include non-terminal (Steiner) vertices.

**Input (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges num_terminals
u v weight (one edge per line, 0-indexed)
terminal_1 terminal_2 ... terminal_K
```

**Output (stdout):**
```
total_weight edge_count
u1 v1
u2 v2
...
```
Edges listed must form a connected, acyclic subgraph that includes all terminals.


**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: AMD64 Family 23 Model 8 Stepping 2, AuthenticAMD
Core count: 64
Memory: 127.87 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35725 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

**Hints (not required):**
- Exact DP (Dreyfus-Wagner) for small terminal sets.
- MST / shortest-path heuristics for large graphs.
- Use adjacency lists and fast I/O.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Connect a set of important nodes in a weighted graph using the cheapest possible tree of edges. Unlike a minimum spanning tree, the solution may pass through intermediate nodes that aren’t in the required set if doing so saves cost — like building a road network that connects key cities via junctions.

This is NP-hard. The test uses planted instances with a known optimal tree for strict grading. Subpasses increase the graph size.

Steiner Tree - 20V, 5 terminals

Approach: Here is the complete, self-contained C++20 solution:

#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

#ifdef _MSC_VER
#pragma comment(linker, "/STACK:268435456")
#endif

using int64 = long long;

// ================================================================
//  Fast I/O
// ================================================================
struct FastIO {
    char ib[1 << 20], ob[1 << 18];
    int iL = 0, iR = 0, oP = 0;
    FILE *fin, *fout;

    FastIO(FILE *_fin = stdin, FILE *_fout = stdout) : fin(_fin), fout(_fout) {}

    inline void flush() {
        if (oP > 0) { fwrite(ob, 1, oP, fout); oP = 0; }
    }
    ~FastIO() { flush(); }

    inline int gc() {
        if (iL >= iR) {
            iL = 0;
            iR = (int)fread(ib, 1, sizeof ib, fin);
            if (iL >= iR) return -1;
        }
        return ib[iL++];
    }

    inline void pc(int c) {
        if (oP >= (int)sizeof ob) { fwrite(ob, 1, oP, fout); oP = 0; }
        ob[oP++] = c;
    }

    inline void write(const char *s) {
        while (*s) pc(*s++);
    }

    // Read unsigned integer
    inline int64 readi() {
        int c = gc();
        while (c != -1 && c != '-' && !(c >= '0' && c <= '9')) c = gc();
        int64 s = 1, v = 0;
        if (c == '-') { s = -1; c = gc(); }
        for (; c >= '0' && c <= '9'; c = gc()) v = v * 10 + (c - '0');
        return s * v;
    }

    // Write integer
    inline void writei(int64 x) {
        if (x < 0) { pc('-'); x = -x; }
        char buf[24]; int n = 0;
        do { buf[n++] = char('0' + x % 10); x /= 10; } while (x);
        while (n--) pc(buf[n]);
    }
} io;

// ================================================================
//  Graph
// ================================================================
struct Graph {
    int n, m;
    vector<vector<pair<int, int>>> adj;       // adjacency list
    vector<tuple<int, int, int64>> edges;     // {u, v, w}
    vector<int> terminals;
    int K;

    void read_input() {
        n = (int)io.readi();
        m = (int)io.readi();
        K = (int)io.readi();

        adj.assign(n, {});
        edges.reserve(m);
        terminals.reserve(K);

        for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
            int u = (int)io.readi();
            int v = (int)io.readi();
            int64 w = io.readi();
            adj[u].emplace_back(v, (int)i);
            adj[v].emplace_back(u, (int)i);
            edges.emplace_back(u, v, w);
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < K; ++i) {
            terminals.push_back((int)io.readi());
        }
    }
};

// ================================================================
//  Dijkstra from single source
// ================================================================
struct DijkstraResult {
    vector<int64> dist;
    vector<int> parent_vertex;
    vector<int> parent_edge;
};

DijkstraResult dijkstra(const Graph &G, int src) {
    int n = G.n, m = G.m;
    DijkstraResult res;
    res.dist.assign(n, INT64_MAX);
    res.parent_vertex.assign(n, -1);
    res.parent_edge.assign(n, -1);
    res.dist[src] = 0;

    using pq_type = tuple<int64, int>;
    priority_queue<pq_type, vector<pq_type>, greater<pq_type>> pq;
    pq.emplace(0, src);

    while (!pq.empty()) {
        auto [d, u] = pq.top(); pq.pop();
        if (d != res.dist[u]) continue;
        for (auto [v, ei] : G.adj[u]) {
            int64 w = get<2>(G.edges[ei]);
            int64 nd = d + w;
            if (nd < res.dist[v]) {
                res.dist[v] = nd;
                res.parent_vertex[v] = u;
                res.parent_edge[v] = ei;
                pq.emplace(nd, v);
            }
        }
    }
    return res;
}

// ================================================================
//  Extract shortest-path subgraph edges between src and dst
// ================================================================
void extract_sp_edges(const Graph &G, int src, int dst,
                      const vector<int> &parent_vertex,
                      const vector<int> &parent_edge,
                      vector<bool> &in_subgraph)
{
    for (int v = dst; v != src; ) {
        int u = parent_vertex[v];
        if (u < 0) break;       // unreachable
        in_subgraph[parent_edge[v]] = true;
        v = u;
    }
}

// ================================================================
//  Build MST from a set of edges (Kruskal)
// ================================================================
struct DSU {
    vector<int> p, rnk;
    DSU(int n) : p(n, -1), rnk(n, 0) {}
    int find(int x) { return p[x] < 0 ? x : p[x] = find(p[x]); }
    bool unite(int a, int b) {
        a = find(a); b = find(b);
        if (a == b) return false;
        if (rnk[a] < rnk[b]) swap(a, b);
        p[b] = a;
        if (rnk[a] == rnk[b]) ++rnk[a];
        return true;
    }
};

vector<int> build_mst(const Graph &G, const vector<bool> &candidate) {
    DSU dsu(G.n);
    // Gather (weight, edge_index) for all candidate edges
    vector<pair<int64, int>> ev;
    ev.reserve(G.m);
    for (int i = 0; i < G.m; ++i)
        if (candidate[i]) ev.emplace_back(get<2>(G.edges[i]), i);
    sort(ev.begin(), ev.end());

    vector<int> tree;
    for (auto [w, ei] : ev) {
        auto [u, v, wt] = G.edges[ei];
        if (dsu.unite(u, v)) tree.push_back(ei);
    }
    return tree;
}

// ================================================================
//  Connectivity check via BFS
// ================================================================
bool is_connected(const Graph &G, int start, const vector<bool> &active_edge) {
    int n = G.n;
    vector<bool> vis(n, false);
    queue<int> q;
    vis[start] = true;
    q.push(start);
    int count = 1;
    while (!q.empty()) {
        int u = q.front(); q.pop();
        for (auto [v, ei] : G.adj[u]) {
            if (active_edge[ei] && !vis[v]) {
                vis[v] = true;
                ++count;
                q.push(v);
            }
        }
    }
    // Also check the reverse (in case start is isolated)
    if (count == 1) {
        for (int u = 0; u < n; ++u) {
            for (auto [v, ei] : G.adj[u]) {
                if (active_edge[ei]) {
                    // Start BFS from u instead
                    fill(vis.begin(), vis.end(), false);
                    vis[u] = true;
                    q = queue<int>();
                    q.push(u);
                    count = 1;
                    while (!q.empty()) {
                        int x = q.front(); q.pop();
                        for (auto [y, ej] : G.adj[x]) {
                            if (active_edge[ej] && !vis[y]) {
                                vis[y] = true;
                                ++count;
                                q.push(y);
                            }
                        }
                    }
                    return count == n;
                }
            }
        }
        return n == 1;   // trivially connected
    }
    return count == n;
}

// ================================================================
//  Dreyfus-Wagner Exact DP  (optimal for K ≤ ~18)
// ================================================================
struct DWResult {
    int64 weight;
    vector<int> edge_list;   // MST edge indices
};

DWResult dreyfus_wagner(const Graph &G) {
    int n = G.n, K = G.K;
    DWResult res;

    // Trivial cases
    if (K == 0) { res.weight = 0; return res; }
    if (K == 1) {
        res.weight = 0;
        res.edge_list.clear();
        return res;
    }
    if (K == 2) {
        auto d = dijkstra(G, G.terminals[0]);
        int64 w = d.dist[G.terminals[1]];
        res.weight = w;
        if (w < INT64_MAX) {
            vector<bool> in_sg(G.m, false);
            extract_sp_edges(G, G.terminals[0], G.terminals[1],
                             d.parent_vertex, d.parent_edge, in_sg);
            res.edge_list = build_mst(G, in_sg);
        }
        return res;
    }

    const int FULL = (1 << K) - 1;

    // dp[mask][v] = (dist, parent_v, parent_mask)
    //   parent: how dp[mask][v] was obtained
    //     parent_mask = mask  =>  path from some vertex (parent_v) to v
    //     parent_mask < mask   =>  dp[parent_mask][parent_v] + dp[mask^parent_mask][v]
    struct Cell {
        int64 d;
        int pv;          // parent vertex (or -1)
        int pmask;       // parent mask
    };
    vector<vector<Cell>> dp(FULL + 1, vector<Cell>(n, {INT64_MAX, -1, -1}));
    vector<vector<bool>> filled(FULL + 1, vector<bool>(n, false));

    // Base: singletons
    vector<DijkstraResult> base_dij;
    for (int s = 0; s < K; ++s) {
        int t = G.terminals[s];
        auto d = dijkstra(G, t);
        base_dij.push_back(d);
        int mask = 1 << s;
        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            dp[mask][v] = {d.dist[v], d.parent_vertex[v], mask};
            filled[mask][v] = (d.dist[v] < INT64_MAX);
        }
    }

    // Enumerate subsets in increasing popcount order
    for (int mask = 1; mask <= FULL; ++mask) {
        int pc = __builtin_popcount(mask);
        if (pc <= 1) continue;

        // Phase 1: combine two disjoint non-empty subsets
        int half = mask >> 1;
        for (int sub = (half & mask); sub > 0; sub = (sub - 1) & half) {
            int comp = mask ^ sub;
            if (comp == 0) continue;
            if (__builtin_popcount(sub) > __builtin_popcount(comp))
                continue;
            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                if (!filled[sub][v] || !filled[comp][v]) continue;
                int64 nd = dp[sub][v].d + dp[comp][v].d;
                if (nd < dp[mask][v].d) {
                    dp[mask][v] = {nd, v, sub};
                    filled[mask][v] = true;
                }
            }
        }

        // Phase 2: Dijkstra propagation
        // Potential improvement: only run from filled vertices
        // But full-graph Dijkstra with early exit is often simpler and fast enough.
        int64 inf = INT64_MAX;
        vector<int64> dist(n, INT64_MAX);
        vector<int> pv(n, -1);
        vector<int> pe(n, -1);
        using pqe = pair<int64, int>;
        priority_queue<pqe, vector<pqe>, greater<pqe>> pq;

        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            if (filled[mask][v]) {
                dist[v] = dp[mask][v].d;
                pv[v] = v;
                pe[v] = -1;
                pq.emplace(dp[mask][v].d, v);
            }
        }

        while (!pq.empty()) {
            auto [d, u] = pq.top(); pq.pop();
            if (d != dist[u]) continue;
            for (auto [v, ei] : G.adj[u]) {
                int64 w = get<2>(G.edges[ei]);
                int64 nd = d + w;
                if (nd < dist[v]) {
                    dist[v] = nd;
                    pv[v] = u;
                    pe[v] = ei;
                    pq.emplace(nd, v);
                }
            }
        }

        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            if (dist[v] < dp[mask][v].d) {
                dp[mask][v] = {dist[v], pv[v], mask};
                filled[mask][v] = true;
            }
        }
    }

    // Find minimum over all vertices for FULL mask
    int best_v = -1;
    int64 best_d = INT64_MAX;
    for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
        if (filled[FULL][v] && dp[FULL][v].d < best_d) {
            best_d = dp[FULL][v].d;
            best_v = v;
        }
    }

    res.weight = best_d;

    if (best_d >= INT64_MAX) return res;

    // ---- Reconstruct the tree ----
    // We have dp[FULL][best_v].
    // Walk the DP tree and collect all shortest-path subgraph edges,
    // then extract MST.
    vector<bool> in_sg(G.m, false);

    // Recursive reconstruction: collect path edges for each (mask, src, dst)
    function<void(int, int, int)> collect_path_edges = [&](int mask, int src, int dst) {
        // Use base_dij for singletons, otherwise need fresh Dijkstra
        if (__builtin_popcount(mask) == 1) {
            int si = 0;
            for (; si < K; ++si) if (mask == (1 << si)) break;
            if (src == G.terminals[si]) {
                extract_sp_edges(G, src, dst,
                                 base_dij[si].parent_vertex,
                                 base_dij[si].parent_edge, in_sg);
            } else {
                auto d = dijkstra(G, src);
                extract_sp_edges(G, src, dst, d.parent_vertex, d.parent_edge, in_sg);
            }
        } else {
            auto d = dijkstra(G, src);
            extract_sp_edges(G, src, dst, d.parent_vertex, d.parent_edge, in_sg);
        }
    };

    function<void(int, int)> reconstruct = [&](int mask, int v) {
        if (__builtin_popcount(mask) <= 1) return;
        Cell &c = dp[mask][v];
        if (c.pm == mask) {
            // Path: from c.pv to v under mask
            reconstruct(mask, c.pv);
            collect_path_edges(mask, c.pv, v);
        } else {
            // Combine: mask = c.pm | (mask ^ c.pm), both have c.pv
            reconstruct(c.pm, c.pv);
            reconstruct(mask ^ c.pm, v);
        }
    };

    reconstruct(FULL, best_v);
    res.edge_list = build_mst(G, in_sg);

    return res;
}

// ================================================================
//  Shortest Path Heuristic (SPH) with full MST extraction
// ================================================================
struct SPHResult {
    int64 weight;
    vector<int> edge_list;
};

SPHResult shortest_path_heuristic(const Graph &G) {
    int K = G.K, n = G.n;
    SPHResult res;

    if (K == 0) { res.weight = 0; return res; }
    if (K == 1) { res.weight = 0; return res; }

    // Build a complete metric graph on the K terminals
    // We reuse Dijkstra results when possible
    vector<vector<int64>> term_dist(K, vector<int64>(K, INT64_MAX));
    vector<vector<int>> term_parent_v(K, vector<int>(K, -1));
    vector<vector<int>> term_parent_e(K, vector<int>(K, -1));

    for (int s = 0; s < K; ++s) {
        auto d = dijkstra(G, G.terminals[s]);
        for (int t = 0; t < K; ++t) {
            term_dist[s][t] = d.dist[G.terminals[t]];
            if (term_dist[s][t] < INT64_MAX) {
                // Store parent chain info — we'll re-run Dijkstra for reconstruction
                // For efficiency, just record dist here
            }
        }
    }

    // Build MST on K terminals using Kruskal
    struct Edge { int u, v; int64 w; };
    vector<Edge> kv;
    for (int i = 0; i < K; ++i)
        for (int j = i + 1; j < K; ++j)
            if (term_dist[i][j] < INT64_MAX)
                kv.push_back({i, j, term_dist[i][j]});
    sort(kv.begin(), kv.end(), [](const Edge &a, const Edge &b) { return a.w < b.w; });

    DSU dsu(K);
    vector<pair<int,int>> mst_term_edges;
    res.weight = 0;
    for (auto &e : kv) {
        if (dsu.unite(e.u, e.v)) {
            mst_term_edges.emplace_back(e.u, e.v);
            res.weight += e.w;
        }
    }

    // Collect shortest-path subgraph edges
    vector<bool> in_sg(G.m, false);
    for (auto [si, ti] : mst_term_edges) {
        int s = G.terminals[si], t = G.terminals[ti];
        auto d = dijkstra(G, s);
        extract_sp_edges(G, s, t, d.parent_vertex, d.parent_edge, in_sg);
    }

    res.edge_list = build_mst(G, in_sg);
    return res;
}

// ================================================================
//  Local Search: Steiner drop (BFS-based for large graphs)
// ================================================================
void local_search(Graph &G, vector<int> &tree_edges) {
    if (tree_edges.empty()) return;

    // Build adjacency for the current tree
    int n = G.n;
    vector<bool> is_tree_edge(G.m, false);
    for (int ei : tree_edges) is_tree_edge[ei] = true;

    vector<vector<pair<int, int>>> tree_adj(n);
    for (int ei : tree_edges) {
        auto [u, v, w] = G.edges[ei];
        tree_adj[u].emplace_back(v, ei);
        tree_adj[v].emplace_back(u, ei);
    }

    // Find leaf nodes and internal nodes of the tree
    vector<bool> is_terminal(n, false);
    for (int t : G.terminals) is_terminal[t] = true;

    // Repeatedly try to drop non-terminal leaves
    bool improved = true;
    while (improved) {
        improved = false;

        // Recompute degrees
        vector<int> deg(n, 0);
        vector<int> edge_count(tree_edges.size(), 0);
        vector<bool> active_edge(G.m, false);
        for (size_t i = 0; i < tree_edges.size(); ++i) {
            int ei = tree_edges[i];
            if (!is_tree_edge[ei]) continue;
            active_edge[ei] = true;
            auto [u, v, w] = G.edges[ei];
            ++deg[u]; ++deg[v];
        }

        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            if (deg[v] == 1 && !is_terminal[v]) {
                // Find the incident tree edge
                for (auto [nbr, ei] : tree_adj[v]) {
                    if (active_edge[ei]) {
                        // Check if removing this edge disconnects terminals
                        is_tree_edge[ei] = false;
                        active_edge[ei] = false;

                        // BFS from any terminal to check connectivity
                        // Pick a terminal as start
                        int start = G.terminals[0];
                        vector<bool> vis(n, false);
                        queue<int> q;
                        vis[start] = true;
                        q.push(start);
                        int tc = 0;
                        while (!q.empty()) {
                            int u = q.front(); q.pop();
                            if (is_terminal[u]) ++tc;
                            for (auto [nbr2, ej] : tree_adj[u]) {
                                if (active_edge[ej] && !vis[nbr2]) {
                                    vis[nbr2] = true;
                                    q.push(nbr2);
                                }
                            }
                        }
                        if (tc == G.K) {
                            improved = true;
                            break;
                        } else {
                            is_tree_edge[ei] = true;
                            active_edge[ei] = true;
                        }
                    }
                }
                if (improved) break;
            }
        }

        if (improved) {
            tree_edges.erase(
                remove_if(tree_edges.begin(), tree_edges.end(),
                          [&](int ei) { return !is_tree_edge[ei]; }),
                tree_edges.end());
        }
    }
}

// ================================================================
//  Edge swap local search (Steiner insert + Steiner drop)
// ================================================================
void edge_swap_search(Graph &G, vector<int> &tree_edges) {
    if (tree_edges.empty()) return;

    const int MAX_ITER = 200;
    int n = G.n;
    vector<bool> is_terminal(n, false);
    for (int t : G.terminals) is_terminal[t] = true;

    for (int iter = 0; iter < MAX_ITER; ++iter) {
        // Build current tree adjacency
        vector<vector<pair<int, int>>> tad(n);
        vector<bool> te(G.m, false);
        for (int ei : tree_edges) {
            te[ei] = true;
            auto [u, v, w] = G.edges[ei];
            tad[u].emplace_back(v, ei);
            tad[v].emplace_back(u, ei);
        }

        // Compute tree weight
        int64 cur_w = 0;
        for (int ei : tree_edges) cur_w += get<2>(G.edges[ei]);

        bool found = false;
        // For each non-tree edge
        for (int ei = 0; ei < G.m && !found; ++ei) {
            if (te[ei]) continue;
            auto [eu, ev, ew] = G.edges[ei];
            // Try adding this edge and removing the heaviest edge on the cycle

            // BFS/DFS to find path between eu and ev in the tree
            vector<int> par(n, -1);
            vector<int> par_edge(n, -1);
            queue<int> q;
            par[eu] = eu;
            q.push(eu);
            while (!q.empty() && par[ev] < 0) {
                int u = q.front(); q.pop();
                for (auto [nbr, ej] : tad[u]) {
                    if (par[nbr] < 0) {
                        par[nbr] = u;
                        par_edge[nbr] = ej;
                        q.push(nbr);
                    }
                }
            }
            if (par[ev] < 0) continue; // should not happen if tree connects everything

            // Walk back to find cycle edges and find heaviest
            vector<int> cycle_edges;
            int64 max_cycle_w = -1;
            int max_cycle_idx = -1;
            for (int v = ev; v != eu; v = par[v]) {
                int ce = par_edge[v];
                cycle_edges.push_back(ce);
                int64 cw = get<2>(G.edges[ce]);
                if (cw > max_cycle_w) {
                    max_cycle_w = cw;
                    max_cycle_idx = ce;
                }
            }

            if (max_cycle_w <= ew) continue; // no improvement

            int64 new_w = cur_w - max_cycle_w + ew;
            if (new_w >= cur_w) continue;

            // Check: removing max_cycle_idx should not disconnect any two terminals
            // The cycle connects eu and ev, so removing any single cycle edge
            // keeps connectivity. Safe to remove.
            // Apply swap
            te[max_cycle_idx] = false;
            tree_edges.erase(remove(tree_edges.begin(), tree_edges.end(), max_cycle_idx),
                             tree_edges.end());
            tree_edges.push_back(ei);
            te[ei] = true;
            found = true;
        }

        if (!found) break;
    }
}

// ================================================================
//  Main
// ================================================================
int main() {
    Graph G;
    G.read_input();

    int64 total_weight;
    vector<int> result_edges;

    if (G.K == 0) {
        total_weight = 0;
        result_edges.clear();
    } else if (G.K == 1) {
        total_weight = 0;
        result_edges.clear();
    } else if (G.K <= 18) {
        // Use exact Dreyfus-Wagner DP
        auto res = dreyfus_wagner(G);
        total_weight = res.weight;
        result_edges = std::move(res.edge_list);
    } else {
        // Use heuristic: SPH + MST + Local search
        auto res = shortest_path_heuristic(G);
        result_edges = std::move(res.edge_list);

        // Local search: edge swaps
        edge_swap_search(G, result_edges);

        // Compute weight
        total_weight = 0;
        for (int ei : result_edges) total_weight += get<2>(G.edges[ei]);
    }

    // Validate connectivity (BFS)
    if (!result_edges.empty()) {
        vector<bool> active(G.m, false);
        for (int ei : result_edges) active[ei] = true;
        if (!is_connected(G, G.terminals[0], active)) {
            // Fallback: output single shortest-path tree from first terminal
            // to all terminals
            result_edges.clear();
            total_weight = 0;
            auto d = dijkstra(G, G.terminals[0]);
            vector<bool> in_sg(G.m, false);
            for (int t : G.terminals) {
                extract_sp_edges(G, G.terminals[0], t,
                                 d.parent_vertex, d.parent_edge, in_sg);
            }
            result_edges = build_mst(G, in_sg);
            total_weight = 0;
            for (int ei : result_edges) total_weight += get<2>(G.edges[ei]);
        }
    }

    // Output
    io.writei(total_weight);
    io.pc(' ');
    io.writei((int64)result_edges.size());
    io.pc('\n');
    for (int ei : result_edges) {
        auto [u, v, w] = G.edges[ei];
        io.writei(u);
        io.pc(' ');
        io.writei(v);
        io.pc('\n');
    }

    return 0;
}
